data.description:
# Discord Link Aggregation Bot

> **Summary**: A TypeScript Discord bot that monitors a channel for shared links, stores them in PostgreSQL with pgvector, generates LLM summaries and embeddings, and enables natural language semantic search to rediscover content.

## Problem Statement

Users share valuable links in Discord channels, but these links are easily lost in chat history and difficult to rediscover later. There is no way to search or retrieve previously shared content by topic or context.

## Users

**Primary User: Community Members**
- As a community member, I want to share a link in Discord and have it automatically catalogued so that I don't need to manually organize or tag my shares.
- As a community member, I want to ask natural language questions about previously shared content so that I can rediscover relevant links without scrolling through chat history.
- As a community member, I want to see a brief summary and preview image of stored links so that I can quickly assess relevance before clicking.

**Secondary User: Server Administrators**
- As a server admin, I want the bot to gracefully handle downtime and catch up on missed messages so that no shared links are lost.
- As a server admin, I want the bot to monitor a configurable channel (initially single, expandable later) so that I can control which conversations are indexed.

## Success Criteria

1. **Link Detection & Storage**: Bot detects all URLs in monitored channel messages and creates/updates records in PostgreSQL with pgvector storage within 30 seconds of message posting.
2. **Content Summarization**: Each stored link includes an LLM-generated summary of the source content, produced via custom OpenAI-compatible LLM proxy with configurable model.
3. **Image Extraction**: Bot extracts Open Graph or meta images from linked pages to provide visual previews.
4. **Semantic Search**: Users can query stored content using natural language, with results returned via semantic similarity search using vector embeddings.
5. **Downtime Recovery**: On startup, bot identifies the last processed Discord message and processes all subsequent messages before resuming real-time monitoring.
6. **Query Detection**: Bot automatically detects when messages appear to be questions about stored content and responds in the same channel.
7. **Duplicate Handling**: When the same URL is posted again, the existing record is updated rather than creating duplicates.
8. **Error Feedback**: Failed link processing is indicated via emoji reaction on the original message.
9. **Configuration**: Bot configuration (Discord token, channel ID, LLM proxy URL, model name, database connection) is managed via environment variables or config file.

## Constraints

- **Technical**:
  - TypeScript with discord.js framework
  - PostgreSQL with pgvector extension for vector storage
  - Custom LLM proxy with OpenAI-compatible API (not Ollama directly)
  - Local development environment only (no containerization initially)
  - Must use established article extraction library (e.g., @extractus/article-extractor or mozilla/readability)
  
- **Operational**:
  - Initially monitors a single channel (architecture should support future multi-channel expansion)
  - Embeddings generated via the same custom LLM proxy
  - Query access open to all users in the channel

## Existing State

This is a greenfield project with no existing codebase. The repository currently contains only workflow documentation (AGENTS.md) and worklog configuration.

## Desired Change

Build a complete Discord bot application that:

1. **Discord Integration Layer**
   - Connect to Discord using discord.js
   - Monitor designated channel for new messages
   - Implement startup recovery to process missed messages
   - Respond to queries in-channel with natural language detection

2. **Content Processing Pipeline**
   - Extract URLs from message content
   - Fetch and parse linked pages using article extraction library
   - Extract Open Graph/meta images
   - Generate summaries via LLM proxy
   - Generate embeddings via LLM proxy

3. **Data Storage Layer**
   - PostgreSQL database schema with pgvector extension
   - JSONB storage for flexible metadata
   - Track last processed Discord message ID for recovery
   - Upsert logic for duplicate URL handling

4. **Query/Retrieval System**
   - Natural language query detection in chat messages
   - Semantic search using pgvector similarity
   - Optional hybrid search (semantic + keyword) if beneficial
   - Format and return results in Discord

## Related Work

- No related work items found in worklog (this is the first work item: SB-0MN1L2P9Z03HOQJN)
- No existing Discord bot code in repository
- No existing database schemas or configurations
- Greenfield repository initialized for this project

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Rate Limiting**: Discord and external websites may rate-limit requests; mitigation: implement backoff and queuing.
- **Content Extraction Failures**: Some sites may block scraping, have paywalls, or dynamic content; mitigation: graceful error handling with emoji feedback.
- **LLM Proxy Availability**: Custom LLM proxy downtime could block processing; mitigation: queue failed items for retry.
- **Query Detection Accuracy**: Automatic question detection may have false positives/negatives; mitigation: tune detection logic, consider adding explicit command fallback later.
- **Scope Creep**: Feature expansion (multi-channel, web UI, etc.) could delay MVP; mitigation: record enhancement ideas as separate work items linked to this epic rather than expanding scope.
- **Discord API Changes**: Discord may change API or rate limits; mitigation: use stable discord.js version, monitor changelog.
- **Large Message Backlog**: Extended downtime could result in many messages to process on startup; mitigation: implement batching with progress logging.

**Assumptions:**
- Custom LLM proxy is already operational and accessible
- PostgreSQL with pgvector extension is available locally
- Discord bot token and permissions will be provided
- Article extraction libraries will handle majority of web content types

## Appendix: Intake Interview Q&A

### Round 1: Core Technology Choices

**Q: What types of links should the bot process? Should it handle all URLs, or only specific types (e.g., articles, YouTube videos, GitHub repos)?**
A: All URLs

**Q: Which LLM provider should be used for generating summaries?**
A: Local/self-hosted

**Q: What programming language/framework do you want to use for the bot?**
A: TypeScript (discord.js)

### Round 2: Features & Scope

**Q: How should the representative image be created? The description mentions 'an image to represent it'.**
A: Extract from page metadata

**Q: How will users query the bot to retrieve content lists? Through Discord commands, a web interface, or both?**
A: Natural language in Discord

**Q: Should the bot monitor a single channel, multiple specific channels, or all channels it has access to?**
A: Initially a single channel, but this may change in the future.

### Round 3: Infrastructure & Data Handling

**Q: For the local LLM, which Ollama model should be used? Or should it be configurable?**
A: It won't be Ollama, it is my custom LLM proxy which uses the OpenAI API. The model used will be configurable.

**Q: What deployment approach should be used? Docker Compose, Kubernetes, or local development only?**
A: Local dev only

**Q: How should the bot handle duplicate links (same URL posted multiple times)?**
A: Update existing record

### Round 4: Search & Access

**Q: How should the pgvector capability be used? For semantic similarity search via embeddings, keyword search, or both?**
A: I certainly want semantic search. If there is benefit in having keyword search as well then both.

**Q: When a user queries for content, where should the results be displayed?**
A: Reply in same channel

**Q: Who should be able to query the bot for stored content?**
A: Open to all users

### Round 5: Implementation Details

**Q: For generating vector embeddings, should the bot use your custom LLM proxy (OpenAI-compatible), a local embedding model, or should this be configurable?**
A: Custom LLM proxy

**Q: How should the bot extract content from linked pages for summarization? Basic HTML scraping, reader-mode extraction, or content-type aware handling?**
A: Are there suitable libraries that cover this already? If so use one (Note: @extractus/article-extractor or mozilla/readability recommended)

**Q: When the bot fails to process a link (site unreachable, paywall, etc.), how should it handle the error?**
A: Emoji reaction on failure

### Round 6: Query Interaction

**Q: For natural language queries, how should the bot know when a message is a query vs. regular conversation? Should users mention the bot, use a keyword, or should it try to detect questions automatically?**
A: Automatic detection

data.description:
# Intake Brief: Process startup missed URLs via queue

## Problem statement
When the bot restarts after downtime, any URLs posted to the monitored channel while the bot was offline are not processed, causing those links to never be indexed or made searchable. This results in data loss and degrades the bot's usefulness.

## Users
- Bot administrators/maintainers who expect the bot to recover gracefully after downtime.
- End users who share links and rely on the bot to index and make them available for semantic search.

## Success criteria
- On startup, the bot identifies all messages posted to the monitored channel after its last processed checkpoint.
- All URLs contained in those messages are enqueued into the existing DocumentQueue for sequential processing.
- The queue processes each URL with the standard progress reporting (editing a status message) and adds Discord success/failure reactions (👀 → ✅/⚠️) as normal.
- Messages are processed in chronological order (oldest first) to preserve original arrival sequence.
- Existing processing rules (e.g., deduplication, reaction handling, backfill) are applied as they would be for any URL.

## Constraints
- Reuse the existing DocumentQueue infrastructure; do not create a separate processing path.
- Maintain ordering: process URLs from oldest to newest message.
- Respect any applicable rate limits (e.g., Discord API, LLM API) as enforced by the existing ingestion pipeline.

## Existing state
- The bot stores a checkpoint per channel (channel_id, last_processed_message_id) in the `app_checkpoints` table.
- On startup, the bot loads this checkpoint but does not fetch or process any messages that arrived after it.
- The `DocumentQueue` class already exists and provides sequential, one‑by‑one processing of URLs with per‑item progress messages (edited status message) and integration with the `IngestionService`.
- The `IngestionService` currently adds Discord reactions (👀 → ✅/⚠️) on a per‑URL basis.
- The ingestion service already contains logic to handle already‑processed URLs (e.g., skipping embedding generation if present) and to add reactions only for new processing.

## Desired change
- At bot startup, after loading the checkpoint, query the monitored channel for all messages posted after the checkpoint’s timestamp (or after the last processed message ID) up to the present.
- Extract URLs from each message and enqueue each URL into the `DocumentQueue`, without pre‑checking whether the URL is already processed.
- When enqueuing from startup, the queue processor will call the ingestion service in normal mode (including adding reactions).
- Ensure the queue processes items in FIFO order, which combined with fetching messages in chronological order guarantees oldest‑first handling.
- Allow the existing ingestion pipeline (including its deduplication and backfill logic) to decide what work is actually needed for each URL.

## Related work
- Startup recovery configuration and validation (current work item SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO).
- DocumentQueue implementation (`src/ingestion/queue.ts`).
- IngestionService progress reporting and reaction handling (`src/ingestion/service.ts`).
- Checkpoint management in `src/db/repository.ts` (`saveCheckpoint`, `getCheckpoint`).
data.title: Startup recovery, configuration, and end-to-end validation

data.description:
# Intake Brief: Make the repo public

Add MIT license, improve README, and make the repository public.

## Problem statement
The repository currently lacks a public license file and its README could be more comprehensive. To make the repository public, we need to add a standard MIT license and improve the existing README.

## Users
- Open‑source developers and contributors who discover the project on GitHub.
- Users of the Discord Link Aggregation Bot who need clear documentation.

## Success criteria
- A `LICENSE` file containing the standard MIT license text is added to the repository.
- The existing `README.md` is reviewed and updated to include key sections such as project overview, installation, usage examples, contribution guidelines, and licensing information.
- No existing functionality is altered; all tests and CI checks continue to pass.
- The repository is set to public on GitHub (no additional settings required).

## Constraints
- Use the standard MIT license boilerplate (no custom modifications).
- Do not change any repository settings beyond making it public.
- Ensure no secrets or sensitive information are introduced.

## Risks & assumptions
- Risk: repository visibility may not be set to public after changes; mitigation: verify visibility on GitHub after committing.
- Risk: missing or incorrect license text; mitigation: use standard MIT boilerplate.
- Assumption: the contributor has permission to push to the repository and change its visibility.

## Existing state
- The repo contains a `README.md` with basic information but lacks a `LICENSE` file.
- The repository is currently private.

## Desired change
- Add a `LICENSE` file with the MIT license.
- Review and improve `README.md` to provide clearer documentation and contribution guidance.
- Verify the repo is public on GitHub.

## Related work
- SB-0MN1P9BEP1TDLTG3 – Make the repo public (this work item)

## Related work (automated report)
- No additional related work items or documentation were found beyond the current work item.

## Appendix
- Q: "README content – What key sections should the README include?" — Answer (user): "there is already a README, review it and improve it".
- Q: "MIT license text – Do you have a specific MIT‑license text you’d like to use, or should we generate the standard MIT license boilerplate?" — Answer (user): "Use the standard MIT boilerplate".
- Q: "Additional repository settings – ..." — Answer (user): "no".
data.title: Make the repo public

data.description:
Problem statement

Discord-shared YouTube links are not being ingested correctly. We need reliable extraction and storage of YouTube video metadata (title, description, thumbnail), optional transcripts, LLM-generated summaries, and embeddings so video content becomes searchable and summarizable like other link types.

Users

- Primary: Community members who share YouTube links and later want to rediscover or search video content.
  - As a community member, when I post or see a YouTube link, I want it indexed with a short summary and searchable embeddings so I can find it later by topic.
- Secondary: Server operators/administrators who want ingestion to be robust and respectful of rate limits and API quotas.
  - As an operator, I want ingest behavior to fail gracefully, log useful diagnostics, and support backfill of missing embeddings or transcripts.

Success criteria

- New YouTube link ingestion records are created on detection and persist video metadata (video id, title, description, thumbnail URL) within 30s of message processing.
- LLM-generated summary and vector embedding are produced and stored for every ingested video; missing embeddings are queued for backfill and completed within configured SLA (example: 24h) for backlog items.
- Transcripts/captions are fetched when available (or via light-weight caption API) and stored; when transcript retrieval is enabled, summaries reflect transcript content.
- Tests cover detection, normalization (YouTube URL -> canonical video id), metadata extraction, summary/embedding generation, and backfill behavior.

Constraints

- Use existing ingestion abstractions and LLM/embedding pipeline (src/ingestion/*, src/llm/*, PostgreSQL+pgvector) to avoid large refactors.
- External APIs (YouTube data/captions) impose rate limits and may require API keys; design must allow configurable API credentials, rate limiting and graceful retry/backoff.
- Storage cost for transcripts and embeddings should be considered (configurable toggle for transcript storage vs. summary-only indexing).

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: API rate limits may cause ingestion delays; mitigation: implement exponential backoff and respect Retry-After headers.
- Risk: Transcript availability varies; mitigation: gracefully fall back to metadata-based summary when captions unavailable.
- Assumption: YouTube Data API key is valid and has sufficient quota.
- Assumption: Existing LLM/embedding pipeline can handle YouTube metadata and transcript text.
- Risk: Scope creep (adding extra features like comment extraction, thumbnail processing); mitigation: record enhancement ideas as separate work items linked to this item.

Existing state

- Ingestion pipeline and LLM/embedding generation exist (see src/ingestion/service.ts, src/llm/*, README.md) and handle web page links and other content types.
- There is an existing work item: "Ingest Yout Tube links" (SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9) created as an idea; console logs show a failure when handling YouTube links.
- Tests and helpers for ingestion are present (tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts) and should be extended for YouTube-specific flows.

Desired change

- Detect YouTube URLs during message ingestion, normalize to canonical video id, and create an ingestion record linking to that id.
- Fetch video metadata (title, description, thumbnail, channel) via YouTube API or safe HTML fallback, store metadata in the existing DB schema alongside other link records.
- If captions/transcripts are available (via YouTube API or light-weight caption fetch), store transcripts and use them as input to LLM summarization and embedding. If unavailable, fall back to metadata-based summary.
- Produce an LLM summary and vector embedding using the existing LLM/embedding pipeline; queue missing embeddings/transcripts for background backfill.
- Add tests and docs; expose config flags for enabling transcript fetching and API credentials.

Related work

- Potentially related docs:
  - README.md — high-level ingestion architecture and components (doc)
  - src/ingestion/service.ts — ingestion orchestration (code)
  - src/ingestion/url.ts — URL extraction and normalization utilities (code)
  - src/llm/embeddings.ts, src/llm/client.ts — existing LLM/embedding integrations (code)
  - tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts — existing tests to extend (tests)

- Potentially related work items:
  - Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline (SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS) — implements core ingestion pipeline used by other link types
  - Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M) — related to multi-page crawling and discovery
  - Report on page processing progress (SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1) — progress-reporting behavior to apply across ingestion flows
  - Make crawler better behaved (SB-0MN2C45HA1O839C4) — crawl politeness and robots handling (constraints to respect)
  - Enable the ingestion of PDF content (SB-0MN2W9WNY0JTX1PZ) — similar content-type ingestion concerns

Notes / open questions

- Confirm whether a YouTube Data API key will be provided, or if we should implement an HTML/meta scrape fallback first.
- Confirm expected transcript source: prefer YouTube captions via API (if available) or a separate speech-to-text job (costly) — user selected transcripts in success criteria, please confirm acceptable source and SLA.

Responses received:

- YouTube Data API key will be provided.
- Transcript source: YouTube captions via API; backlog backfill within 24h.

Please review this draft and tell me if you want changes or if I should proceed to finalize it for SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9.
Related work (automated report)
- Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline (SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS): Implements the core ingestion pipeline that this work item will extend to handle YouTube-specific flows.
- Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M): Related to multi-page crawling and discovery; YouTube ingestion will benefit from similar robustness patterns.
- Report on page processing progress (SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1): Progress-reporting behavior that should be applied across all ingestion flows including YouTube.
- Make crawler better behaved (SB-0MN2C45HA1O839C4): Crawl politeness and robots handling; constraints to respect when fetching YouTube metadata.
- Enable the ingestion of PDF content (SB-0MN2W9WNY0JTX1PZ): Similar content-type ingestion concerns; shares patterns for extending ingestion to new media types.
data.title: Ingest Yout Tube links

data.description:
Implement handling for messages like 'Crawl http://...'.

Goal:
When a crawl instruction is received, fetch the target page and then fetch all pages linked from the main body/content region of that page. Each fetched page should be processed as a separate entry into the database.

User story:
As an operator issuing crawl instructions, I want one command to ingest a seed page and its in-body linked pages so that related content is captured in one pass.

Expected behavior:
- Detect messages in the form 'Crawl <URL>' (or equivalent phrasing already handled by parser/router).
- Retrieve the seed URL.
- Extract links from the main body/content area (not global nav/footer/chrome where feasible in current architecture).
- Resolve relative links to absolute URLs.
- Fetch linked pages with sensible safeguards (dedupe, skip invalid/unfetchable URLs).
- Process every successfully fetched page as an individual DB entry.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Given a crawl message with a valid URL, the system fetches the seed page.
2) Links from the seed page main body are discovered and normalized.
3) Seed page plus each discovered linked page are processed into DB entries.
4) Duplicate URLs are not processed more than once in a single crawl request.
5) Failures on some linked pages do not prevent processing others; errors are surfaced/logged.
6) Automated tests cover link extraction/normalization and multi-page ingestion flow.

Notes:
- Keep implementation aligned with existing ingestion and storage abstractions.
- discovered-from: user request in current session
data.title: Add crawl command handling for linked pages

data.description: Improve the crawl feature of this bot by making it respect robots.txt and adding delays between retrieval. If possible use a crawler library rather than implementing this yourself.
data.title: Make crawler better behaved

data.description:
Bug discovered while validating Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M).

Observed behavior:
A message 'crawl https://howtomarketagame.com/blog/' only ingests the seed URL and does not ingest linked pages.

Expected behavior:
When crawl seed is processed, linked pages from the main body should be discovered and ingested even if extractor-provided link list is empty.

Likely cause:
Some pages return extracted body content without populating extractor links[] metadata. Current crawl discovery relies primarily on links[].

Plan:
- Add fallback link extraction from extracted body HTML anchor tags.
- Normalize/dedupe and merge extractor links[] + body-derived links.
- Add tests covering empty links[] with body anchors.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Crawl plan discovers links when extractor links[] is empty but content contains anchor hrefs.
2) Seed + discovered pages are ingested as separate entries.
3) Existing URL extraction behavior remains unchanged.
4) Tests pass.

related-to:SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M
data.title: Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML

data.description:
# Headline
Add in-channel progress reporting for link ingestion by posting one bot status message and editing it as work advances. Apply this to all ingestion paths, with per-URL updates and crawl progress counts.

## Problem statement
Operators currently receive only reaction-based signals during ingestion, which do not clearly communicate current progress for longer crawls or multi-URL ingestion runs. This makes it hard to tell what the bot is doing, how far it has progressed, and whether it is stalled.

## Users
- Discord channel operators who submit single-link or crawl ingestion commands.
- Team members monitoring bot behavior during long-running ingestion.

User stories:
- As an operator, I want to see one status message updated in place so channel noise stays low while I can still track ingestion progress.
- As an operator, I want crawl progress shown as `x/y` so I can estimate remaining work.
- As an operator, I want per-URL status text so I can identify which URL is currently being processed.

## Success criteria
- For each ingestion-triggering message, the bot posts a single status message and edits that same message throughout processing.
- The behavior applies to all link ingestion flows (single URL and crawl flows).
- For crawl flows, status updates include a running `x/y` count across URLs processed.
- Status updates are per-URL and reflect meaningful processing phases (for example: fetching, extracting links, storing).
- On completion, the status message is updated to a terminal state indicating success, partial success, or failure.

## Constraints
- Keep channel noise low by using one editable status message rather than many progress messages.
- Preserve existing ingestion behavior and error handling; this item is focused on progress visibility, not ingestion logic changes.
- Work within current Discord API/message capabilities in the existing bot architecture.

## Existing state
- `src/index.ts` manages monitored-message handling and currently uses reactions (`👀`, `🤔`, and configured success/failure reactions) as lightweight progress signals.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts` executes the ingest pipeline for URLs and crawl-discovered pages but does not expose user-visible step-by-step text progress.
- No in-place edited status message is currently produced for ingestion operations.

## Desired change
- Introduce a progress-reporting path for ingestion that sends one status message and updates it in place as ingestion progresses.
- Emit per-URL progress updates for all ingestion flows.
- For crawls, include a running `x/y` progress counter as discovered URLs are processed.
- Keep existing reactions unless implementation constraints require adjustment.

## Related work
- Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M): Parent crawl feature where user-visible crawl progress naturally fits.
- Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML (SB-0MN2C6W8Y13RVKWI): Recent crawl behavior work that influences discovered-URL processing.
- `src/index.ts`: Entry point for message handling and likely place to initiate/manage status-message lifecycle.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts`: Core per-URL ingestion orchestration and best place to emit progress events.
- `src/discord/client.ts`: Discord client abstraction relevant if progress update hooks are added at interface boundaries.

## Related work (automated report)
- Add crawl command handling for linked pages (SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M): This in-progress parent feature already defines crawl ingestion behavior across seed and discovered pages, so progress messaging should align with its processing flow and error tolerance expectations.
- Fix crawl fallback link discovery from extracted body HTML (SB-0MN2C6W8Y13RVKWI): This completed item changed how crawl URLs are discovered from extracted HTML content, which directly affects how many URLs can appear in `x/y` progress counters during crawl runs.
- `src/index.ts`: The monitored-message orchestrator currently drives reactions and invokes ingestion; it is the natural place to create a single status message and manage its lifecycle per user request.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts`: The service already has clear per-URL processing stages (fetch/extract/summarize/embed/store) and is the best source of structured progress events used to edit the status message.
- `README.md`: Existing docs describe reaction-based ingestion signals and should be updated to document in-place status message edits and crawl progress counting behavior.

## Risks and assumptions
- Assumption: A single editable status message per ingestion request is acceptable UX for both short and long ingestion runs.
- Assumption: Existing reaction-based signals remain in place unless implementation constraints require changing them.
- Risk: Message edit rate limits may affect very frequent updates; mitigation is to keep update cadence aligned to meaningful stage transitions.
- Risk: Partial URL failures in a crawl can lead to misleading final status text; mitigation is to explicitly represent partial success in terminal status.
- Risk: Scope creep into broader UX redesign (threads, rich embeds, dashboards); mitigation is to capture extra UX ideas as separate linked work items instead of expanding this item.
data.title: Report on page processing progress

data.description:
# Enable PDF Content Ingestion

When a PDF link is shared in Discord, extract the text content and generate summary and embedding for semantic search, treating PDFs like any other ingestible content.

## Users
- **Primary**: Discord bot users who share PDF documentation, research papers, or technical documents
- **Use case**: Share a PDF link → system extracts text → generates summary → creates embedding for semantic search

## Success criteria
1. PDF URLs (ending in `.pdf`) are detected during link extraction
2. PDF text content is extracted and stored in the `content` field
3. Summary is generated from extracted PDF text using existing LLM summarizer
4. Embedding is generated from PDF text content using existing embedder
5. Extracted PDF content includes metadata (page count, author if available)
6. Implementation follows existing pattern for content-type specific ingestion (like YouTube)

## Constraints
- Must reuse existing summarizer and embedder services
- Must integrate with existing `IngestionService` architecture
- Should handle extraction failures gracefully (fallback or error handling)
- Consider file size limits for large PDFs

## Existing state
- The system currently handles HTML web pages via `@extractus/article-extractor`
- YouTube links have specialized handling with transcript fetching
- URL detection uses regex patterns in `src/ingestion/url.ts`
- The `ContentExtractor` interface exists in `src/ingestion/extractor.ts`

## Desired change
1. Add PDF URL detection pattern to `url.ts` (similar to YouTube detection)
2. Create a PDF-specific extractor or extend the existing `ContentExtractor`
3. Add PDF ingestion handling to `IngestionService` (similar to `ingestYouTubeUrl`)
4. Store PDF-specific metadata (page count, author)

The implementation should follow the same pattern used for YouTube ingestion for consistency.

## Related work
- **SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9**: "Ingest YouTube links" (completed) - similar content-type ingestion pattern
- **SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS**: "Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline" (completed)
- **SB-0MN4FHM2B0PI69AE**: "Add Playwright fallback for blocked content extraction" (open)
- Files: `src/ingestion/extractor.ts`, `src/ingestion/service.ts`, `src/ingestion/url.ts`

## Risks & assumptions
- **Risk**: Scope creep to other document types (DOCX, PPTX). **Mitigation**: Record opportunities for additional document types as separate linked work items rather than expanding this item.
- **Risk**: Large PDF files may cause memory/time issues. **Mitigation**: Add file size limits and chunking for large PDFs.
- **Risk**: PDF extraction may fail on scanned/image-based PDFs. **Assumption**: Developer will handle gracefully with appropriate error messages.
- **Risk**: PDFs behind authentication cannot be extracted. **Assumption**: Focus on publicly accessible PDFs only.

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "What should happen when a PDF link is encountered? Should it extract text content for summarization/embedding, or just store the link with metadata?" — Answer (user): "Extract text content". Source: interactive reply.
- Q: "Are there specific PDF libraries or tools you want to use, or should the developer choose based on the existing codebase?" — Answer (user): "Developer choice". Source: interactive reply.
- Q: "Should this support other document types (e.g., DOCX, PPTX) in the future, or focus only on PDF for now?" — Answer (user): "Design for extensibility". Source: interactive reply.

Related work (automated report)

- **SB-0MN1Z8HBC03YSOJ9**: "Ingest YouTube links" - Completed implementation that handles YouTube-specific content ingestion. Provides the core pattern (`ingestYouTubeUrl` method in `src/ingestion/service.ts`) that PDF ingestion should follow. The YouTube implementation shows how to handle content-type specific metadata, transcripts/captions, and embedding generation.
- **SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS**: "Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline" - Completed work that implements the core ingestion pipeline used by all link types. Establishes the `ContentExtractor` interface in `src/ingestion/extractor.ts` and the `IngestionService` orchestration in `src/ingestion/service.ts`. PDF ingestion will extend this existing architecture.
- **SB-0MN4FHM2B0PI69AE**: "Add Playwright fallback for blocked content extraction" - Open work item for adding Playwright as a fallback when direct HTTP fetching fails. This is tangentially related as it enhances content extraction capabilities; however, it's not directly required for PDF ingestion.

### Repository files for reference

- `src/ingestion/url.ts` - Contains YouTube URL detection patterns (`YOUTUBE_PATTERNS`) that should be extended with a similar PDF detection pattern. The `isYouTubeUrl` and `extractYouTubeVideoId` functions show the pattern for content-type specific URL detection.
- `src/ingestion/extractor.ts` - Contains the `ContentExtractor` interface and `ArticleExtractorContentExtractor` implementation. A new PDF extractor should follow this same interface pattern.
- `src/ingestion/service.ts` - Contains the `IngestionService` class with the `ingestYouTubeUrl` method (lines 450-582) that demonstrates how to handle content-type specific ingestion. PDF ingestion should add a similar method or extend the existing flow.
- `src/ingestion/youtube.ts` - YouTube API client used for fetching metadata and captions. May provide useful patterns if PDF extraction requires external libraries.

data.description:
# Intake Brief: Ensure that file:// urls are honoured

## Problem statement

The Discord Link Aggregation Bot currently only supports http:// and https:// URLs via regex patterns in `src/ingestion/url.ts`. Users who want to index local files (PDFs, text documents, etc.) for personal knowledge management cannot use file:// URLs, limiting the bot's utility for local content indexing.

## Users and User Stories

**Primary user:** Bot operator/owner who wants to build a searchable knowledge base from local files.

**User Stories:**
- As a bot operator, I want to add local PDF documents to the search index so I can search my document collection alongside web content.
- As a bot operator, I want to use the CLI (`sb add file:///home/user/docs/report.pdf`) to index local files without security restrictions.
- As a whitelisted Discord user, I want to share file:// URLs in Discord messages so the bot can index them with appropriate security controls.
- As a bot operator, I want to prevent unauthorized users from accessing arbitrary files on the server via Discord messages.

## Success Criteria

1. **CLI Support**: The `sb add` and `sb queue` commands accept file:// URLs without restrictions and process them successfully.
2. **Discord Support**: file:// URLs are accepted from Discord messages only from whitelisted Discord users (configured via environment variable).
3. **Security**: Non-whitelisted Discord users receive a clear "access denied" message when attempting to submit file:// URLs.
4. **File Type Support**: All readable file types are supported (leveraging existing PDF extractor and adding support for text files, HTML, and other formats).
5. **Symlink Protection**: Symbolic links are rejected with an appropriate error message.
6. **Error Handling**: File access errors (not found, permission denied) return standard error messages without exposing sensitive file path information.
7. **URL Extraction**: The `extractUrls()` function in `src/ingestion/url.ts` recognizes file:// URLs alongside http:// and https:// URLs.

## Constraints

1. **Security First**: Local file access must be strictly controlled to prevent unauthorized file disclosure.
2. **Environment-based Configuration**: User whitelist must be configurable via .env file (e.g., `ALLOWED_FILE_URL_USERS=userid1,userid2`).
3. **No Symlink Following**: Symbolic links must be detected and rejected to prevent directory traversal attacks.
4. **Error Message Sanitization**: Error messages must not leak file system information (absolute paths, directory structure).
5. **Discord.js Compatibility**: Implementation must work within existing Discord.js message handling infrastructure.
6. **Cross-platform**: Must work on Linux, macOS, and Windows (different file path conventions).

## Existing State

**Current URL handling (`src/ingestion/url.ts`):**
- Line 1: URL regex only matches `https?:\/\/...` - excludes file://
- Line 47: Protocol check explicitly filters to `http:` and `https:` only
- No concept of protocol-specific security controls

**Current ingestion flow (`src/ingestion/service.ts`):**
- URL extraction happens before security checks
- No user-specific permission system exists
- Content extraction uses `@extractus/article-extractor` for HTTP URLs and `PDFParse` for PDFs

**Current CLI (`src/cli/commands/add.ts`):**
- Accepts URLs as command arguments
- No URL protocol validation currently implemented

## Desired Change

1. **URL Pattern Updates** (`src/ingestion/url.ts`):
   - Extend URL regex to match file:// URLs
   - Add `isFileUrl()` detection function
   - Add file protocol validation

2. **Security Layer** (`src/ingestion/service.ts` or new middleware):
   - Check if URL is file:// protocol
   - If from Discord: verify user ID against whitelist
   - If from CLI: allow unrestricted access
   - Reject symlinks before file access

3. **Content Extraction** (`src/ingestion/extractor.ts`):
   - Add `FileContentExtractor` class for local file reading
   - Support text files, binary detection for non-readable files
   - Leverage existing PDF extractor for local PDFs

4. **Configuration** (`.env.example` and `src/config.ts`):
   - Add `ALLOWED_FILE_URL_USERS` environment variable
   - Parse comma-separated Discord user IDs

5. **Error Handling**:
   - Standard error messages: "Failed to process file" without path details
   - Symlink rejection: "Symbolic links are not supported"
   - Permission denial for non-whitelisted users: "You are not authorized to submit file URLs"

## Related Work

- **Code:** `src/ingestion/url.ts` - URL extraction patterns (line 1, 47 need modification)
- **Code:** `src/ingestion/service.ts` - Ingestion orchestration (needs security checks)
- **Code:** `src/ingestion/extractor.ts` - Content extraction (needs file:// handler)
- **Code:** `src/cli/commands/add.ts` - CLI add command (already accepts URLs, needs testing)
- **Code:** `src/config.ts` - Configuration management (needs new env var)
- **Work Item:** SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH - Modularize bot bootstrap (may affect where security layer is added)

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **Security vulnerability**: Improper implementation could expose sensitive files. *Mitigation:* Strict whitelist enforcement, symlink rejection using `fs.lstat()` to detect links before reading, path sanitization review.
- **Cross-platform issues**: Windows file:// URLs have different format (file:///C:/path). *Mitigation:* Test on all platforms, use Node.js path utilities.
- **Performance**: Large local files could block the ingestion pipeline. *Mitigation:* File size limits, streaming reads for large files.
- **Scope creep**: Request for additional protocols (ftp://, sftp://). *Mitigation:* Document as future work items, stay focused on file:// only.
- **Race condition**: File content or permissions could change between security check and content read. *Mitigation:* Acceptable risk for personal use; document that file:// URLs reference file state at time of read.

**Assumptions:**
- Bot runs in an environment where local file system access is appropriate and secure.
- Whitelisted users are trusted and understand the security implications.
- Local files are text-readable or have extractable content (not encrypted/proprietary formats).

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q:** "Who is the primary user for this feature?"  
  **A:** (user) Bot operator for personal knowledge management - local file indexing use case.

- **Q:** "Should file:// URLs be accepted from Discord messages or only via CLI commands?"  
  **A:** (user) Both - Discord messages with security checks, CLI has no restrictions.

- **Q:** "What types of local files should be supported?"  
  **A:** (user) All readable file types (not limited to specific extensions).

- **Q:** "What security model should restrict who can submit file:// URLs via Discord?"  
  **A:** (user) User whitelist for Discord access, no security restrictions for CLI.

- **Q:** "Should file:// URLs be restricted to specific directories for security?"  
  **A:** (user) Any path accessible to the bot process (no directory restrictions).

- **Q:** "How should the bot handle errors when processing file:// URLs?"  
  **A:** (user) Standard error messages without exposing file path details.

- **Q:** "How should the user whitelist for Discord file:// access be configured?"  
  **A:** (user) Via .env file (environment variable with comma-separated Discord user IDs).

- **Q:** "Should the bot follow symbolic links when processing file:// URLs?"  
  **A:** (user) Reject symlinks for security (do not follow).

## Related work (automated report)

### Highly Related Work Items

- **SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH**: "Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration" - Open high-priority refactoring that affects where the security layer for file:// URLs will be added. This work splits `src/index.ts` into focused modules and will determine the best location for Discord user whitelist checks.

- **SB-0MN2W9WNY0JTX1PZ**: "Enable the ingestion of PDF content" - Completed work that demonstrates the exact pattern for adding content-type specific URL support. The PDF implementation shows how to: (1) add URL detection patterns to `src/ingestion/url.ts`, (2) create content-type specific extractors in `src/ingestion/extractor.ts`, and (3) add handling logic to `src/ingestion/service.ts`. This is the primary reference implementation for file:// support.

- **SB-0MN3UEBPD1P8MA2X**: "Unify URL parsing and normalization across ingestion and queueing" - Open work that consolidates URL extraction logic. When adding file:// URL support to `src/ingestion/url.ts`, changes should align with this consolidation effort to avoid creating new divergence.

- **SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS**: "Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline" - Completed foundational work that established the `ContentExtractor` interface and `IngestionService` architecture. All content-type specific implementations (including file://) extend this base architecture.

### Repository Files for Reference

- `src/ingestion/url.ts:1,47` - URL regex patterns currently only match `https?:`, file:// support needs to extend these patterns and add `isFileUrl()` detection similar to `isPdfUrl()` and `isYouTubeUrl()` patterns.

- `src/ingestion/service.ts:147-461` - Main ingestion orchestration where file:// security checks (Discord user whitelist) need to be integrated before content extraction.

- `src/ingestion/extractor.ts:12-14` - `ContentExtractor` interface that a new `FileContentExtractor` should implement, following the pattern established by `PdfContentExtractor` (lines 40-80).

- `src/config.ts:6-34` - Configuration schema where `ALLOWED_FILE_URL_USERS` environment variable needs to be added alongside existing YouTube and backfill configuration options.
data.title: Ensure that file:// urls are honoured

## Feature Summary
Implement YouTube URL detection and normalization to canonical video IDs during message ingestion.

## Target Outcome
When a Discord message contains a YouTube URL (youtube.com, youtu.be, or youtube shorts), the system detects it and extracts a canonical video ID for consistent storage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detect standard YouTube URLs (youtube.com/watch?v=, youtu.be/, youtube.com/shorts/)
- Normalize all URL variants to canonical 11-character video ID
- Handle edge cases: playlists, timestamps, extra parameters
- Skip non-video URLs (channels, playlists without video context)
- Unit tests cover all URL patterns with >95% coverage
- Integration test: URL detected and normalized in ingestion flow

## Minimal Implementation
- Add isYouTubeUrl() and extractYouTubeVideoId() functions in src/ingestion/url.ts
- Update URL detection in ingestion service to branch for YouTube URLs
- Create ingestion record with video ID as primary key
- Add 15+ unit tests for URL patterns
- Update .env.example with YouTube API config placeholder

## Dependencies
- Existing ingestion pipeline (src/ingestion/service.ts)
- PostgreSQL schema (video ID storage)

## Deliverables
- Code: src/ingestion/url.ts extensions
- Tests: tests/ingestion.youtube.test.ts
- Docs: Update README with YouTube URL handling

## Prototype/Experiment
- Test with 10 real YouTube URLs to validate pattern matching
- Success: All 10 correctly normalized to video ID

## Risks
- URL format changes by YouTube (rare but possible)
- Mitigation: Loose pattern matching with validation

## Feature Summary
Integrate YouTube Data API v3 to fetch video metadata (title, description, thumbnail, channel) and store in database.

## Target Outcome
When a YouTube URL is detected, the system fetches and stores video metadata within 30 seconds via YouTube Data API.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fetch video metadata using YouTube Data API v3
- Store: video ID, title, description, thumbnail URL, channel name, published date
- Handle API rate limits with exponential backoff
- Handle API errors gracefully (log, skip, continue)
- Configurable API key via environment variable
- Store metadata in existing link_records table with YouTube-specific fields
- Fallback to HTML meta tags if API unavailable

## Minimal Implementation
- Create src/ingestion/youtube.ts with YouTubeApiClient class
- Add YOUTUBE_API_KEY to config.ts and .env.example
- Implement fetchVideoMetadata(videoId) method
- Add rate limiting and retry logic (max 3 retries)
- Store metadata in link_records with content_type='youtube'
- Add integration tests with mocked API responses
- Handle API quota exceeded errors gracefully

## Dependencies
- YouTube URL detection and normalization (SB-0MN2XLAOO1RTXKKE)
- Existing ingestion service architecture
- YouTube Data API key (user-provided)

## Deliverables
- Code: src/ingestion/youtube.ts
- Tests: tests/ingestion.youtube.test.ts (API mocking)
- Docs: Update README with YouTube API setup
- Config: .env.example updates

## Prototype/Experiment
- Test API integration with 5 real videos
- Verify rate limit handling with intentional quota exceed
- Success: All metadata fields populated, errors handled gracefully

## Risks
- API rate limits (10,000 quota units/day for free tier)
- Mitigation: Implement caching, backfill queue, graceful degradation
- API key exposure risk
- Mitigation: Store in environment, never commit to repo

## Feature Summary
Fetch and store YouTube captions/transcripts when available via API or caption download.

## Target Outcome
When a YouTube video has captions available, fetch and store transcript text for LLM summarization and search indexing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Check if captions available via YouTube Data API
- Download captions in preferred language (configurable, default: en)
- Store transcript text in database (or file storage reference)
- Handle missing captions gracefully (fallback to metadata summary)
- Support auto-generated vs manual captions preference
- Configurable: enable/disable caption fetching
- Transcript stored within 60s of metadata fetch

## Minimal Implementation
- Extend YouTubeApiClient with fetchCaptions(videoId, language)
- Add caption download via youtube-captions-scraper or similar
- Store transcript in link_records.transcript_text or separate table
- Add ENABLE_YOUTUBE_CAPTIONS config flag
- Implement language fallback (requested -> en -> any available)
- Handle caption download failures gracefully
- Add tests for caption availability checking and download

## Dependencies
- YouTube Data API integration (previous milestone)
- Storage schema extension for transcript text

## Deliverables
- Code: src/ingestion/youtube.ts extensions
- Tests: Caption fetching tests with mocks
- Docs: Caption configuration in README
- Config: ENABLE_YOUTUBE_CAPTIONS, YOUTUBE_CAPTION_LANGUAGE

## Prototype/Experiment
- Test caption fetching on 5 videos with known captions
- Verify graceful handling of 5 videos without captions
- Success: >80% caption fetch rate for available captions

## Risks
- Not all videos have captions (especially non-English)
- Mitigation: Graceful fallback to metadata-based summary
- Caption download can be slow
- Mitigation: Async processing, timeout handling

## Feature Summary
Generate LLM summaries for YouTube videos using metadata and/or transcripts as input.

## Target Outcome
Every ingested YouTube video has an LLM-generated summary stored within 60 seconds of metadata fetch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generate summary using existing LLM pipeline (src/llm/client.ts)
- Use transcript text if available, otherwise use title + description
- Summary length: 100-200 characters (configurable)
- Store summary in link_records.summary field
- Handle LLM failures gracefully (retry once, then skip)
- Integration with existing progress reporting (SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1)
- Summary generated within 60s of ingestion start

## Minimal Implementation
- Extend ingestion service to call LLM for YouTube videos
- Create YouTube-specific prompt template:
  - With transcript: "Summarize this video transcript in 2-3 sentences..."
  - Without transcript: "Summarize this video based on title and description..."
- Reuse existing LLM client and error handling
- Add summary generation to ingestion flow after metadata fetch
- Update tests to verify summary generation
- Handle LLM timeout (30s default)

## Dependencies
- YouTube metadata fetching (previous milestone)
- Caption fetching (optional enhancement)
- Existing LLM client (src/llm/client.ts)
- Progress reporting feature (SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1)

## Deliverables
- Code: Integration in src/ingestion/service.ts
- Tests: Summary generation tests
- Docs: Update README with YouTube summarization

## Prototype/Experiment
- Generate summaries for 5 videos with transcripts
- Generate summaries for 5 videos without transcripts
- Success: All summaries coherent and relevant

## Risks
- LLM API latency could slow ingestion
- Mitigation: Timeout handling, async queue for backfill
- Transcript too long for LLM context window
- Mitigation: Truncate to first N characters, or chunk and summarize
data.title: LLM summary generation for YouTube videos

data.description:
## Feature Summary
Generate vector embeddings for YouTube videos and implement backfill queue for missing embeddings/transcripts.

## Target Outcome
All YouTube videos have vector embeddings stored in pgvector for semantic search, with a backfill queue ensuring missing embeddings are completed within 24h SLA.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generate embeddings using existing embedding pipeline (src/llm/embeddings.ts)
- Use transcript text + metadata if available, otherwise metadata only
- Store embeddings in pgvector (existing vector column)
- Implement backfill queue for failed/missing embeddings
- Backfill job runs periodically (every hour) to process queue
- Queue SLA: 24h maximum from ingestion to embedding completion
- Track backfill metrics: queue depth, success rate, processing time

## Minimal Implementation
- Extend ingestion to call embedding generation after summary
- Store embedding in link_records.embedding (pgvector)
- Create backfill queue table: backfill_queue(video_id, type, attempts, created_at)
- Implement BackfillService with processQueue() method
- Add scheduled job (hourly) to process backfill queue
- Max 3 attempts per item before marking failed
- Add metrics logging for observability

## Dependencies
- LLM summary generation (previous milestone)
- Existing embedding pipeline (src/llm/embeddings.ts)
- PostgreSQL pgvector extension

## Deliverables
- Code: src/ingestion/backfill.ts service
- Database: Migration for backfill_queue table
- Tests: Backfill queue tests, embedding generation tests
- Docs: Backfill configuration in README
- Config: BACKFILL_INTERVAL_MS, MAX_BACKFILL_ATTEMPTS

## Prototype/Experiment
- Test embedding generation for 10 videos
- Simulate embedding failures and verify queue behavior
- Test backfill job processes queue within SLA
- Success: >95% embedding success rate, backfill clears within 24h

## Risks
- Embedding API rate limits
- Mitigation: Queue-based throttling, batch processing
- Large queue could overload system
- Mitigation: Configurable batch size, circuit breaker pattern
data.title: Vector embedding generation and backfill queue

Implement a queue system to ensure only one document is processed at a time. Update messages should end with a line indicating how many items remain in the queue.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Only one document is processed at a time
2. Queue tracks pending documents
3. Progress messages include queue size (e.g., '3 more in queue')
data.title: Create document processing queue system

data.description:
Generate a unique session_id for each URL being processed and pass it to the LLM client. This ensures clean context isolation between different documents when using llama.cpp server mode.

**User Story:**
As a bot operator using llama.cpp, I want each URL to have its own isolated context so that summaries aren't influenced by previous documents.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Each URL gets a unique session_id (UUID)
2. session_id is passed to /chat/completions endpoint
3. session_id is logged for debugging
4. Works with llama.cpp OpenAI-compatible API
data.title: Add llama.cpp session_id support per URL

data.description:
Update queue messaging to show accurate queue length after items are added, and always display current queue state when processing.

Requirements:
1. When URLs are added: Added [url] to Queue. Queue is now x long.
2. Delete previous queue message when queue changes
3. When processing starts: Processing [url] from the Queue. Queue is now x long.
4. Continue updating as queue is processed

Acceptance Criteria:
- Queue message shows correct length after URLs are added
- Previous queue message is deleted when queue changes
- Processing message shows correct remaining queue length
- Message updates work with Discord message editing/deletion
data.title: Improve queue messaging format

data.description:
Detect and skip URLs that are already in the queue. When a duplicate is detected, show a skip message instead of adding it again.

Requirements:
1. Check if any URL from the message is already in the queue
2. If duplicate found, skip adding and show message: Skipping [url] as it is already in the queue. Queue is x long.
3. If message has multiple URLs and some are duplicates, add only the new ones
4. Queue length should reflect actual queue size after handling duplicates

Acceptance Criteria:
- Duplicate URLs are not added to queue
- Skip message is shown for duplicates
- Partial duplicates (some URLs new, some duplicate) are handled correctly
- Queue length is accurate in all messages
data.title: Prevent duplicate URLs in queue

data.description:
Check if a URL already exists in the database when processing. If it does, show 'Updating' in progress reports and use a different completion reaction.

Requirements:
1. Check database for existing URL before processing
2. If URL exists, change progress label from 'Extracting' to 'Updating'
3. Use different completion reaction for updates (e.g., 🔄 instead of ✅)
4. Track which URLs are updates vs new in progress tracking

Acceptance Criteria:
- Existing URLs show 'Updating' in progress messages
- New URLs show 'Extracting' in progress messages
- Update reactions are different from new URL reactions
- Database check happens early in processing
data.title: Detect existing URLs in database

data.description:
When a message contains multiple URLs, each URL should be added to the queue as a separate item and processed independently.

Requirements:
1. Split multi-URL messages into individual queue items
2. Each URL gets processed independently (not sequentially in one batch)
3. Queue status messages should reflect individual URL processing
4. Reactions should still work correctly per original message

Acceptance Criteria:
- Multi-URL messages are split into separate queue entries
- URLs from different messages can interleave in processing
- Progress tracking works per URL
- Queue length is accurate per URL
- Original message reactions work correctly
data.title: Process each URL independently in queue

data.description:
Fixed missing progress updates and summary reports when URLs are processed from the queue.

Problem: When processing items from the queue, no status message was created for progress tracking, so progress updates were not visible.

Solution: Modified onQueueUpdate callback to create a progress status message when processing starts. This allows onProgress callback to find and update the message with extraction progress and final summary.

Changes:
- When queue status is processing, delete queue status message and create progress status message
- Progress status message is stored in statusMessages for onProgress to find
- Queue status messages (added/skipped) continue to work as before
data.title: Fix progress updates when processing from queue

data.description:
Keep both queue status messages and progress updates visible as separate messages.

Requirements:
1. Queue status message shows Added/Processing/Skipped status
2. Progress message shows extraction progress (Downloading, Extracting, Storing, Summary)
3. Both messages coexist - queue message is not deleted when progress starts
4. Progress message is updated independently during processing
5. Queue message remains visible to show queue state

Acceptance Criteria:
- Queue status message stays visible throughout processing
- Progress updates appear in separate message
- User can see both queue length and extraction progress
- Messages are properly tracked and updated
data.title: Separate queue status and progress messages

data.description:
Update progress messages to show more granular progress including chunk-level updates during summarization and embedding, similar to console logs.

Requirements:
1. Show progress when processing each chunk (Summarizing chunk 2 of 3)
2. Show progress when embedding each chunk (Embedding chunk 3 of 5)
3. Updates should be frequent enough to show progress through long operations
4. Keep messages readable and not too spammy

Acceptance Criteria:
- Progress messages show current chunk being processed
- Format: Summarizing chunk X of Y
- Format: Embedding chunk X of Y
- Works with the existing progress message system
data.title: Add chunk-level progress updates

data.description: Enhance semantic query detection to recognize trigger phrases like 'tell me about', 'what is', 'explain', etc. to initiate semantic search
data.title: Add semantic query trigger phrases

data.description:
Implement the web crawling feature that discovers URLs from a seed URL and adds them to the processing queue.

Requirements:
1. Detect crawl commands in Discord messages (crawl https://...)
2. Fetch seed URL content and extract links
3. Normalize discovered URLs relative to base URL
4. Filter out duplicates and already-processed URLs
5. Add discovered URLs to DocumentQueue for processing
6. Respect robots.txt crawl policy (delays, disallows)
7. Limit crawl depth or URL count to prevent runaway crawling

Acceptance Criteria:
- crawl https://example.com discovers and queues linked pages
- Respects robots.txt crawl-delay directives
- Does not crawl disallowed paths
- Shows progress as URLs are discovered and queued
- Limits prevent infinite crawling
- Works with existing queue system
data.title: Implement web crawl feature

data.description: Bug: An error in an LLM request seems to mess up the progress message. The existence of an error in processing puts the processing message into a bad state, resulting in it not appearing when it should. This affects user experience by hiding important status updates during LLM processing.
data.title: LLM request error corrupts progress message display

## Summary
Evaluate the current SourceBase codebase to identify concrete refactoring opportunities that improve maintainability, testability, and separation of concerns without changing behavior.

## User story
As a maintainer, I want a prioritized refactoring backlog so I can improve code quality incrementally with clear scope and expected outcomes.

## Expected outcomes
- A documented assessment of key refactoring opportunities.
- A set of actionable work items, each with clear rationale and acceptance criteria.
- No production code changes as part of this assessment task.

## Acceptance criteria
- The project structure and core modules are reviewed.
- Refactoring opportunities are identified and prioritized by impact/risk.
- A work item is created for each proposed refactoring.
- No source code or configuration files are modified.

# Intake Brief: Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration (SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH)

## Problem Statement

The main bootstrap module  (914 lines) currently mixes dependency wiring, message orchestration, progress formatting, queue status UX, crawl command handling, and startup/shutdown concerns in one large module. This violates the Single Responsibility Principle, increases change risk, and makes targeted testing difficult. Any change to message formatting, command handling, or lifecycle logic requires modifying the same file, raising the risk of unintended side effects.

## Users

**Primary users:**
- **Developers maintaining the bot** - Need clear boundaries to make safe, localized changes
- **Developers writing tests** - Need testable seams for message formatting and orchestration logic
- **DevOps/SRE teams** - Need reliable startup/shutdown behavior with clear observability

**User Stories:**
- As a developer, I want to modify progress message formatting without touching command handling code
- As a developer, I want to unit test queue status updates without booting the full Discord client
- As a maintainer, I want to add new commands without modifying the composition root
- As an SRE, I want to understand and modify lifecycle behavior (startup recovery, graceful shutdown) in one focused location

## Success Criteria

- [ ]  is reduced to composition and bootstrap responsibilities only
- [ ] Crawl command handling is extracted into a dedicated  module/class
- [ ] Stats command handling is extracted into a dedicated  module/class
- [ ] Progress and queue message formatting and lifecycle behavior are extracted behind testable utilities or services
- [ ] Startup/shutdown logic is extracted into a  or  module
- [ ] All existing behavior remains functionally equivalent and all tests pass
- [ ] New modules have clear, documented interfaces and dependencies
- [ ] Unit tests exist for each extracted module
- [ ] Integration tests verify end-to-end behavior remains unchanged
- [ ] Interface definitions are established for sibling refactoring coordination

## Constraints

- **Backward compatibility:** Existing Discord message processing behavior must remain unchanged
- **Testing:** Current test suite must continue to pass; new tests should be easier to write
- **Dependencies:** Must work with existing Discord.js client, database connections, and service injections
- **Scope:** This is a refactoring only—no new features or behavior changes
- **Risk mitigation:** Changes should be incremental; consider feature flag or rollback strategy if issues arise in production

## Existing State

 currently contains:

1. **Dependency wiring (lines 92-100):** Repository, LLM client, embedding provider, query service instantiation
2. **Progress formatting (lines 21-90):**  function with phase emojis, labels, and message truncation logic
3. **Queue formatting (lines 209-220):**  function for queue status updates
4. **Ingestion service setup (lines 123-207):** Complex  callback handling status message updates and final summary posting
5. **Document queue setup (lines 318-377):**  callback handling channel cache, status message lifecycle
6. **Stats command handler (lines 388-466):** Discord slash command for database statistics
7. **Crawl command handling (lines 489-562):** Crawl command detection, URL discovery, progress updates, and queue insertion
8. **Message processing (lines 468-581):** Main message handler with query detection, crawl detection, and queue enqueueing
9. **Startup notifications (lines 223-304):**  for announcing bot availability
10. **Startup recovery (lines 585-614):**  for catching up on missed messages
11. **Status restoration (lines 645-695):**  for reconnecting to pre-restart queue state
12. **Graceful shutdown (lines 701-907):** Comprehensive shutdown handling with notification, message cleanup, and database closure

## Desired Change

Decompose  into focused modules:

1. **Composition root ( reduced):** Keep only dependency instantiation and service composition
2. ** module:** Encapsulate crawl command detection, URL discovery, progress updates, and queue insertion
3. ** module:** Encapsulate database statistics command handling
4. **/ modules:** Handle message formatting and status message lifecycle
5. **/ module:** Encapsulate startup notifications, recovery, status restoration, and graceful shutdown
6. **Interface definitions:** Define clear interfaces that sibling refactoring tasks must conform to

## Related Work

- **Parent work item:** SB-0MN3UDE2M16HUXQA "Implement refactoring backlog" - This task is one of 7 refactoring opportunities identified during the codebase assessment
- **Related refactoring tasks:**
  - SB-0MN3UEBH41ENA4DI: Introduce dedicated backfill persistence layer
  - SB-0MN3UEBND02UT7OM: Extract progress formatting and queue presenters (may overlap—coordinate with implementer)
  - SB-0MN3UEBPD1P8MA2X: Extract CLI orchestration from add.ts and queue.ts
  - SB-0MN3UEBPJ0MBAPAE: Extract query command detection and handling
  - SB-0MN3UEBPJ1JXRVPJ: Extract LinkRepository into focused read/write interfaces
  - SB-0MN3UEG5L1RYP5YQ: Extract configuration schema and validation into dedicated module
- **Key files:**
  -  - Main file to refactor (914 lines)
  -  - Existing crawl service (to be used by new handler)
  -  - Document queue with callbacks
  -  - Existing recovery service
  -  - Discord bot client wrapper

## Risks & Assumptions

- **Risk:** Breaking existing message processing flow during refactoring
  - *Mitigation:* Comprehensive test coverage before changes; incremental refactoring with feature branch
- **Risk:** Complexity of  and  callbacks may make extraction non-trivial
  - *Mitigation:* Let implementer decide on class-based vs. functional approach based on discovered complexity
- **Risk:** State management (statusMessages, queueStatusMessages, channelCache maps) spread across callbacks
  - *Mitigation:* Consider encapsulating state in presenter classes or passing state containers explicitly
- **Risk:** Scope creep by including stats command extraction or other unplanned refactors
  - *Mitigation:* Record opportunities for additional extractions (stats command, query handling) as follow-up work items linked to this task; keep this refactor focused on crawl handling and lifecycle management
- **Assumption:** Discord.js client lifecycle behavior remains consistent
- **Assumption:** Existing test suite provides sufficient coverage for regression detection

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

**Q1: Feature Scope Confirmation**
The Appendix already states we'll extract: (A) standalone , (B) / modules, and (C) . Should we also extract the stats command handler (lines 388-466) as a 4th module for consistency, or leave it as future follow-up work?

**A1**: Yes, extract the stats command handler now for consistency. Having all command handlers modularized together creates a more coherent architecture.

**Q2: Testing Strategy**
What testing coverage is required for the new modules? Should we require:
A) Unit tests for each extracted module
B) Integration tests verifying end-to-end behavior remains unchanged
C) Both unit and integration tests
D) Let implementer decide based on module complexity

**A2**: C) Both unit and integration tests

**Q3: Rollout & Coordination**
The description mentions 6 sibling refactoring tasks. Should we:
A) Implement this work independently and coordinate integration later
B) Wait for specific sibling items (e.g., SB-0MN3UEBPJ0MBAPAE for message types) before starting
C) Define clear interfaces now that siblings must conform to later

**A3**: C) Define clear interfaces now that siblings must conform to later

---

**Headline Summary:** Refactor the 914-line  to separate bootstrap composition from message orchestration by extracting crawl handling into , stats handling into , progress/queue UX into presenter modules, and lifecycle management into a dedicated , while keeping all existing behavior intact. Include both unit and integration tests and define clear interfaces for sibling refactoring coordination.
data.title: Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration

## Summary
BackfillService currently reaches into LinkRepository internals via repository bracket access to execute direct SQL. This bypasses encapsulation, weakens typing, and makes persistence behavior hard to test independently.

## Refactoring goal
Create an explicit backfill repository abstraction that owns all backfill queue SQL and transactions, then inject it into BackfillService.

## Expected outcome
- BackfillService depends on clear interfaces instead of hidden internals.
- Better isolation for unit tests and easier schema evolution.
- Reduced chance of regressions from ad hoc SQL usage.

## Acceptance criteria
- Backfill SQL reads and writes are moved out of BackfillService into a dedicated repository module.
- BackfillService no longer uses bracket access to internal repository fields.
- Repository and service interfaces are strongly typed without any in critical paths.
- Existing backfill behavior remains unchanged and tests pass.
data.title: Introduce dedicated backfill persistence layer

data.description:
# Intake Draft: Centralize embedding dimension and vector normalization (SB-0MN3UEBND02UT7OM)

Headline
--------
Centralize the embedding-dimension constant and extract a shared vector normalization utility so ingestion, backfill, and query code use the same dimension and normalization behaviour, preventing drift and making dimension changes auditable.

Problem statement
-----------------
Embedding dimension and resize/padding logic are duplicated across ingestion, backfill, and query paths (for example, scripts/full-backfill.js reads LLM_EMBEDDING_DIM and contains slice/pad logic). Duplicated logic and stale comments increase the risk of mismatched vector sizes between stored vectors and query vectors, which can cause incorrect search results or runtime errors when dimensions change.

Users
-----
- Primary: Engineers and maintainers who run ingestion, backfills, and query services.
- Secondary: Release engineers and SREs who coordinate dimension changes and Qdrant migrations.

Example user stories
- As a maintainer, I can change the embedding dimension in a single place and rebuild without hunting for duplicated constants.
- As an engineer running a backfill, I want the backfill script to use the canonical dimension and normalization logic so saved embeddings are consistent with query-time vectors.
- As a developer implementing a new retrieval path, I can import and use the shared normalization utility so query vectors match stored vectors.

Success criteria
----------------
- A single source-of-truth for embedding dimension (constant or accessor) is used by ingestion, CLI/backfill scripts, and query code. Verification: code search shows imports from the shared module rather than local constants.
- Vector normalization and pad/trim logic are extracted into a shared utility with unit tests covering: shorter-than-dim (zero-padding), equal-length (no-op), longer-than-dim (truncate), and numeric normalization where applicable.
- scripts/full-backfill.js (and any other backfill/ingestion scripts) import and use the shared API and log the canonical dimension at start. Verification: run a sample backfill over N items and confirm stored vector lengths equal canonical dimension.
- All stale or duplicated dimension comments are corrected or removed.
- All related documentation (README, scripts comments, and any inline docs) updated to reference the shared dimension API.
- Full project test suite passes with the new changes.

Constraints
-----------
- Do not change the DB/vector column type or Qdrant collection dimension as part of this change. If the canonical dimension must change, that will be a coordinated migration (recreate Qdrant collection + full backfill) in a follow-up work item.
- Preserve existing environment variable behaviour: LLM_EMBEDDING_DIM should remain available as an override; the shared accessor should read the env variable when appropriate.
- Keep runtime overhead minimal; normalization functions should be synchronous and small.

Existing state
--------------
- scripts/full-backfill.js reads LLM_EMBEDDING_DIM (default 1024) and contains pad/slice logic; it also documents that Qdrant must be recreated when changing dimensions.
- Several work items reference embedding implementation, dimension limits, and related refactors (see Related work below).

Desired change
--------------
- Create a small shared module (e.g. src/lib/embeddings.ts or src/cli/lib/embeddings.ts depending on project layout) that exposes:
  - getEmbeddingDim(): number
  - padOrTruncate(vec: number[], dim?: number): number[]
  - normalizeVector(vec: number[], dim?: number): number[]  // optional numerical normalization (e.g., L2) if used by query path
- Replace local constants and inline pad/trim logic with imports from the shared module (backfill, ingestion, query code paths, CLI helpers).
- Add unit tests for the new utilities and update any integration/backfill tests to assert vector lengths.
- Update README and inline script comments to reference the shared API.

Related work
------------
Potentially related docs
- scripts/full-backfill.js — backfill script that currently reads LLM_EMBEDDING_DIM and pads/truncates vectors. (path: scripts/full-backfill.js)

Potentially related work items
- Replace embeddings implementation (SB-0MNDG2MNZ002N7YV) — related replacement/refactor of embedding implementation.
- Auto-propagate LLM embedding size limits into runtime (SB-0MN8PR81T003R8JP) — related to syncing embedding size limits into runtime config.
- Refactor for Libraries (SB-0MNBSCZDC0021Q2J) — ongoing refactor that may affect module locations for shared code.
- Split CLI from Discord Bot (SB-0MN8Q6A6E000LPUT) — completed; may have moved CLI-only code into src/cli/lib which is the expected place for shared CLI utilities.

Related-work (placeholder)
- [The find_related skill will append a short report here when run.]

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
---------------------------------------
- Q: "Should this change include a DB schema change or Qdrant collection dimension migration?" — Answer (agent inference): "No. The repo and scripts indicate Qdrant/migration is a separate step (scripts/full-backfill.js notes Qdrant recreation required). This intake will centralize logic only; dimension changes/migration are a separate work item." Source: scripts/full-backfill.js lines ~27-29. Final: yes.
- Q: "Where should the shared module live (CLI-only folder vs shared src)?" — Answer (agent inference): "Prefer src/cli/lib if code is CLI-specific; otherwise place in a shared src/lib location if both bot and CLI use it. There are existing items (SB-0MNBSCZDC0021Q2J, SB-0MN8Q6A6E000LPUT) refactoring CLI/lib layout — verify module location with the team." Source: wl search results. Final: TBD; implementation should prefer the existing refactor conventions and coordinate with Refactor for Libraries (SB-0MNBSCZDC0021Q2J).
- Q: "Are numerical normalizations (e.g., L2) required or already in use?" — Answer (agent inference): "No explicit L2-normalization function was found in quick searches; existing backfill averages embedding vectors and pads/truncates but does not L2-normalize. If query code requires L2 normalization, add it to the shared utility and include tests. Source: scripts/full-backfill.js averaging logic and pad/truncate behavior." Final: TBD pending verification by implementer.
data.title: Centralize embedding dimension and vector normalization
