## Summary
URL extraction behavior is implemented in multiple places using different regex logic. Divergent parsing rules can cause duplicate handling differences between queued and directly ingested URLs.

## Refactoring goal
Consolidate URL extraction and normalization into shared utilities used by ingestion, queueing, and crawl entry points.

## Expected outcome
- Consistent URL handling across all code paths.
- Fewer duplicate ingestion edge cases.
- Easier maintenance of URL parsing rules.

## Acceptance criteria
- Queue module uses shared URL utilities instead of maintaining separate regex extraction.
- URL normalization and deduplication rules are applied consistently in all ingestion entry points.
- Tests cover punctuation stripping, duplicates, and edge case URL formats across call sites.
data.title: Unify URL parsing and normalization across ingestion and queueing

data.description:
## Summary
Queue initialization recreates minimal Discord message objects and casts them to the full Message type. This creates fragile runtime assumptions and hides incompatibilities from TypeScript.

## Refactoring goal
Introduce explicit queue payload and reaction capability interfaces so persisted queue entries do not depend on unsafe Message casting.

## Expected outcome
- Safer type boundaries between persistence and Discord transport.
- Clear contract for queue processing in restart and live message scenarios.
- Fewer runtime surprises due to partially mocked message shapes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Queue item model distinguishes transport data from Discord SDK objects.
- Startup recovery path no longer casts synthetic objects to Message.
- Ingestion and queue contracts are updated to accept explicit domain payloads or adapters.
- Existing queue recovery behavior remains equivalent and tested.
data.title: Replace synthetic Discord message casting with domain queue payloads

data.description:
## Summary
Discord message size limits and truncation logic are duplicated in multiple modules with hard coded values and reserved space heuristics. This makes formatting behavior inconsistent and difficult to reason about.

## Refactoring goal
Create shared formatting and truncation policies for Discord outputs and use them consistently for progress, completion, and query responses.

## Expected outcome
- Consistent and testable rendering behavior for Discord messages.
- Reduced duplication and fewer off by one formatting bugs.
- Easier future changes to output formatting constraints.

## Acceptance criteria
- Message length limits and truncation heuristics are centralized in one presenter or utility module.
- Progress, queue, and query response paths use the shared formatter.
- Snapshot or unit tests validate formatting and truncation behavior for representative long content cases.
- User facing output remains functionally equivalent.
data.title: Extract Discord response formatting policies into shared presenters

data.description:
## Summary
Database repository code still relies on loosely typed row objects in several places. Weak typing around SQL result mapping increases maintenance cost and can hide schema drift until runtime.

## Refactoring goal
Standardize typed row interfaces and mapping utilities across repositories, removing avoidable use of untyped row access.

## Expected outcome
- Better compile time guarantees for persistence code.
- Lower risk when evolving migrations and repository methods.
- Clearer contracts between SQL queries and domain models.

## Acceptance criteria
- Queue and backfill related repository methods use explicit row interfaces and typed mapping functions.
- Loosely typed row mapping is minimized in repository code paths that affect ingestion and queue processing.
- Existing tests continue to pass and additional mapping tests are added where useful.
data.title: Strengthen repository typing and row mapping boundaries

data.description:
# Intake Brief for SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q

## Problem statement
When the bot is restarted while there are items in the processing queue, the progress tracking and queue messaging functionality stops working correctly. Users no longer receive updates on job progress or queue status, leading to a poor user experience and lack of transparency about job processing.

## Users
- Discord users who submit URLs for processing
- Users expect to see real-time progress updates when their submitted content is being processed
- Users expect to see queue status updates showing their position in the processing queue

## Success criteria
- [ ] After bot restart with queued items, progress messages continue to update normally for each queued item
- [ ] After bot restart with queued items, queue status messages continue to update normally showing current queue length
- [ ] No loss of progress tracking or queue status functionality after restart
- [ ] Users receive appropriate Discord messages showing processing progress and queue position
- [ ] The bot continues processing queued items sequentially as expected

## Constraints
- Must maintain existing queue persistence mechanism using PostgreSQL database
- Cannot change the fundamental architecture of how queue items are stored and retrieved
- Must work with existing Discord.js message handling system
- Should not introduce significant performance overhead
- Must maintain backward compatibility with existing progress and queue message formats
- Implementation must follow Test Driven Development (TDD) approach

## Existing state
- The bot uses a DocumentQueue class that loads pending items from database on startup via 
- Progress messages are tracked using an in-memory  Map keyed by Discord message ID
- Queue status messages are tracked using an in-memory  Map keyed by channel ID
- Both Maps are lost when the bot restarts, breaking the connection between queued items and their status messages
- The queue processing logic itself works correctly after restart (items are loaded from DB and processed)
- The issue is purely in the status message tracking and updating mechanism

## Desired change
- Modify the status message tracking mechanism to persist the necessary information across bot restarts
- When the bot restarts and loads queued items from the database, it should recreate the appropriate status message tracking entries
- Ensure that progress updates and queue status updates continue to function correctly for items that were queued before restart
- Maintain the existing behavior for newly submitted items after restart

## Related work
- SB-0MN3RC13413ZRD9M: Fix progress updates when processing from queue (completed)
- SB-0MN3RDGWH02AP64E: Separate queue status and progress messages (completed)
- SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1: Report on page processing progress (completed)
- SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH: Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration (open)
- SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO: Startup recovery, configuration, and end-to-end validation (open)

## Risks & Assumptions
- Risk: Inconsistent state between database queue entries and Discord message tracking - Mitigation: Recreate tracking entries based on actual database state during initialization
- Risk: Duplicate status messages if reconnection logic fails - Mitigation: Check for existing messages before creating new ones
- Risk: Performance impact from additional database queries during startup - Mitigation: Only query for pending items that need message tracking
- Assumption: Database persistence is reliable and queue entries survive bot restarts
- Assumption: Discord channel and message references remain valid after restart
- Scope creep mitigation: Link additional improvements as separate work items rather than expanding this fix

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "When the bot restarts with items in the queue, what specific behavior breaks regarding progress and queue messages?" — Answer (user): "Both progress and queue messages fail". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "What is the expected behavior when the bot restarts with queued items?" — Answer (user): "Continue processing queue with normal progress updates". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Have you observed any error messages or logs when this issue occurs?" — Answer (user): "Haven't checked logs". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

## Related work (automated report)

This section identifies existing work that is directly related to the current work item.

### Related Work Items

1. **SB-0MN3RC13413ZRD9M: Fix progress updates when processing from queue** (completed)
   - This work fixed missing progress updates when URLs are processed from the queue by modifying the onQueueUpdate callback to create a progress status message when processing starts. This is directly related as it deals with the same progress tracking mechanism that fails after restart.

2. **SB-0MN3RDGWH02AP64E: Separate queue status and progress messages** (completed)
   - This work implemented separate queue status and progress messages that coexist during processing. Understanding this separation is crucial for correctly recreating both types of messages after a restart.

3. **SB-0MN2CJF990ZLPVC1: Report on page processing progress** (completed)
   - This work established the foundation for progress reporting in the system, which is the mechanism that needs to be restored after restart.

4. **SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH: Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration** (open)
   - This open work item aims to separate concerns in the main bootstrap module, which includes progress formatting and queue status UX. Changes made here could affect how status messages are handled and should be coordinated with this work.

5. **SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO: Startup recovery, configuration, and end-to-end validation** (open)
   - This work item focuses on recovering missed URLs on startup by querying the monitored channel for messages posted after the last checkpoint. While it deals with startup recovery, it focuses on new messages rather than restoring status for already-queued items, making it complementary rather than duplicative.

### Related Code Files

1. **src/index.ts** - Contains the in-memory  and  Maps that are lost on restart, and the logic that uses them for progress and queue status updates.

2. **src/ingestion/queue.ts** - Implements the DocumentQueue class that loads pending items from database on startup via  but doesn't recreate status message tracking.

3. **src/db/queue-repository.ts** - Provides access to pending queue entries including the Discord message and channel IDs needed to recreate status messages.

### Key Insights

The core issue is that while the bot correctly persists queue items in the database and reloads them on restart, it fails to persist the transient state needed for user-facing status updates. The solution involves modifying the DocumentQueue.initialize() method to recreate the appropriate entries in the statusMessages and queueStatusMessages Maps based on the reloaded queue items.
data.title: Progress and queue messages fail after bot restart with queued items

data.description:
Headline
-------
Enable the Discord bot to monitor multiple servers and channels with per-channel monitoring toggles persisted in config.yaml and in a lightweight DB table. Monitoring is disabled by default for newly observed channels and only server administrators may enable it.

Final summary
-------------
Add persistent per-server and per-channel monitoring toggles (config.yaml + DB) so administrators can opt-in channels for URL ingestion; maintain command availability everywhere and keep monitoring disabled by default.

Problem statement
-----------------
The Discord bot currently supports a single monitored channel via environment variable (DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID). We need the bot to work across multiple servers and channels, with a per-channel monitoring toggle persisted in a configuration (config.yaml) and managed by server administrators. Monitoring should be disabled by default for new channels.

Users
-----
- Primary users: Server administrators who configure which channels the bot should monitor for link ingestion.
- Secondary users: Community members who will continue to use the bot for commands and queries in any channel.

Example user stories
--------------------
- As a server admin, I can add or remove channels to the bot's monitored list using a slash command or by editing a repository-managed config.yaml so the bot ingests links from only selected channels.
- As a regular user, I can still run commands (/stats, /search) in any channel even if monitoring is disabled for that channel.

Success criteria
----------------
- The bot stores per-server and per-channel monitoring preferences in a persistent config (config.yaml backed by DB), and uses them when deciding to ingest URLs.
- Slash command(s) exist to enable/disable monitoring per-channel; only users with Manage Channels or admin role can run them.
- Existing bot commands and interactions continue to work unchanged in channels where monitoring is disabled.
- Automated tests cover checkpointing and ingestion behavior across multiple channels (including app_checkpoints DB usage).
- Migration or data model changes (if any) are documented and migrations provided.


Constraints
-----------
- Config.yaml must be loadable by both the bot runtime and the CLI tooling if needed; environment-based DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID remains a fallback for single-channel setups.
- Monitoring remains disabled by default for newly observed channels.
- Admin permission checks should align with Discord permissions (Manage Channels or equivalent role).
- Avoid breaking the CLI/CLI-only workflows (CLI must not require Discord env vars).

Existing state
--------------
- Current codebase enforces a single monitored channel via DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID (src/config/bot.ts, src/index.ts, src/discord/client.ts).
- Database table app_checkpoints already exists and is used to save per-channel processing state (tests and repository code reference INSERT INTO app_checkpoints).
- Several related work items exist that split bot/CLI boundaries and introduce config modules (see Related work).

Desired change
--------------
- Introduce a config.yaml schema supporting multiple servers and channel entries (per-channel monitoring flag and metadata).
- Persist channel monitoring preferences to the DB and make the bot consult DB/config on message receive to decide whether to ingest links.
- Add slash commands (and optionally CLI flags) to manage per-channel settings; enforce admin permissions.
 - Add tests and migrations as needed; update README and example .env/.env.example to document the new configuration approach and fallback behavior.

Potentially related docs
------------------------
- README.md — Project overview and existing guidance for DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID and app_checkpoints; update to document per-channel config, admin/fallback semantics, and migration notes.
- .env.example — Shows DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID; update to document admin/fallback channel and point to config.yaml for multi-channel setups.
- src/config/bot.ts — Bot config schema and validation; primary extension point to add multi-server/channel schema or DB-backed lookups for per-channel overrides.
- src/index.ts — Entrypoint that reads monitoredChannelId and triggers ingestion; a main location to consult per-channel settings before enqueuing work.
- src/discord/client.ts — Discord client and slash-command handlers; natural place to register enable/disable monitoring commands and permission checks.
- migrations/* (migrations/001_initial_schema.sql, 003_add_transcript.sql, 004_backfill_queue.sql) — Review to determine where to add channel_settings table or extend app_checkpoints.

Potentially related work items
-----------------------------
- SB-0MN9CIGI6005KN9H — Split Config into CLI and Bot Modules: foundational work separating CLI and bot configs; use as the integration point for bot-only settings.
- SB-0MN9GDBXZ0041PXE — Create Bot config module: current bot config schema and validation for DISCORD_* vars; logical place to extend.
- SB-0MN9GD42J00624QD — Create CLI config module: confirms CLI is Discord-free; helpful to preserve CLI/bot boundary.
- SB-0MN9GDNNO0067ZDI — Update all config import paths: useful reference for where imports were updated and where single-channel logic remains.
- SB-0MN9GDYCT001Z4UI — Update tests for config split: example of tests that were adapted post-config-split; useful as template for new tests.
- SB-0MN9CL02100258DH — Refactor Bot to Invoke CLI as Subprocess: historical context for how channel flags were passed to CLI subprocesses.


Related work
------------
- SB-0MN9GDBXZ0041PXE: Create Bot config module — separates bot config and introduces bot-specific config fields.
- SB-0MN9CIGI6005KN9H: Split Config into CLI and Bot Modules — relevant for maintaining CLI independence.
- SB-0MN4BB9QJ0GVN9MA (this item): Add multi-server/multi-channel support with per-channel monitoring toggle.
- README.md: contains current guidance about DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID and app_checkpoints table usage.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
--------------------------------------
- Q: "How should per-channel monitoring be configured and persisted? Choose one or provide a different approach." — Answer (user): "Create a more flexible config.yaml system that allows multiple channels. The one in .env is the admin channgel". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Who should be permitted to enable/disable monitoring in a channel?" — Answer (user): "Server administrators only (recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "When the bot is added to new servers or sees new channels, should monitoring be enabled by default?" — Answer (user): "Disabled by default (recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Notes
-----
- This draft preserves the existing DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID env var as an admin/fallback channel entry; the preferred new workflow uses config.yaml and DB persisted settings with slash commands for runtime management.

Please review this intake draft. Approve or provide edits (I will incorporate up to three concise edits per round).

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: DB schema changes could require complicated migrations or reveal hidden coupling. Mitigation: create a small spike and migration plan, add migration tests, and run a staging migration before production.
- Risk: Permission checks may be bypassed or misconfigured, causing unwanted ingestion. Mitigation: require standard Discord permissions, add unit and integration tests for permission paths, and make permission checks a code-review focus.
- Risk: Scope creep (adding per-server UI, dashboards, or advanced rules). Mitigation: record new feature opportunities as separate work items and do not expand this item's scope.
- Assumption: The CLI/bot config split in the repo will be used as the integration point for bot-only settings.
- Assumption: No major refactor of the ingestion queue is required; if discovered, create a follow-up spike.

Finished Completeness review: no substantive changes needed beyond adding Risks & assumptions.

Finished Capture fidelity review: wording confirmed to match user's answers; clarified "admin channel" wording in Appendix.

Finished Related-work & traceability review: related items and docs referenced; report appended and linked via work item comments.

Finished Risks & assumptions review: scope-sccreep mitigation present, risks clarified; no further edits required.

Finished Polish & handoff review: tightened headline and added final summary.
data.title: Add multi-server/multi-channel support with per-channel monitoring toggle

data.description:
# Intake Brief: /search Command and Free-form RAG Article Generation

**Headline:** Add `/search` slash command for tiered-relevance link retrieval and convert free-form messages into RAG-generated articles delivered as .md attachments with source quality metadata.

## Problem Statement

The Discord bot currently detects natural language questions via heuristics and returns a ranked list of the top 3 stored links with their summaries. Users must manually read each linked source to synthesize an understanding of a topic. There is no explicit search command -- search is only triggered implicitly via question detection, which can miss intentional queries that don't match question patterns. There is also no way for users to get a synthesized article that brings together information from multiple stored sources.

## Users

**Primary User: Community Members**
- As a community member, I want to type `/search <query>` and receive a list of matching stored articles with their links and relevance ratings, so that I can quickly find the most relevant previously shared content.
- As a community member, I want to type a free-form prompt (not a command, not a URL) and receive a generated article that synthesizes information from stored content based on my prompt, so that I get a cohesive answer without reading multiple sources individually.
- As a community member, I want to see tiered relevance ratings on search results, so that I can prioritize which sources to read.
- As a community member, I want the generated article to include metadata about source quality, so that I can gauge the reliability of the synthesized content.

**Secondary User: Server Administrators**
- As a server admin, I want the article generation model to be separately configurable from the summarization model, so that I can use a larger or more capable model for synthesis without affecting ingestion performance.

## Success Criteria

1. A `/search` Discord slash command is registered and accepts a text query; it retrieves top-10 relevant results from the vector DB and returns candidate articles with their links and tiered relevance ratings (e.g., "highly relevant", "somewhat relevant") -- a summary in the message body and full details as a `.md` file attachment.
2. Free-form text messages (non-command, non-URL) trigger article generation: the user's message becomes the seed prompt for a RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant stored content, generates a synthesized article via LLM, and returns it as a `.md` file attachment with a summary in the message body.
3. Generated articles include metadata at the top indicating the number and quality tier of sources used, based on configurable similarity score bands.
4. The bot sends a status message to the Discord channel while processing (both for `/search` and free-form article generation), providing user feedback during the wait.
5. The `.md` attachment filename follows the pattern `<sanitized-query>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.
6. The `/search` command is implemented using the existing `CommandHandler` interface pattern, setting the convention for future slash commands.
7. Article generation uses a separately configurable LLM model (`LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`) via the existing OpenAI-compatible client.
8. Article generation uses the full extracted content stored in the database (not just summaries) as RAG context.
9. If LLM article generation fails (timeout, error, model unavailable), the bot replies with an error message and still provides the retrieved link list so the user is not left empty-handed.

## Constraints

- **Technical:**
  - Must use the existing `OpenAiCompatibleLlmClient` for LLM calls (chat completions API).
  - Must use the existing Qdrant vector store for retrieval (top-10).
  - Must use the existing `CommandHandler` interface from `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts` for the `/search` slash command.
  - Discord message limit is 2000 characters; detailed results and article content must go in `.md` file attachments, with only summaries in the message body.
  - The LLM server uses model switching; article generation should use a separate config (`LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`) to allow using a different model from summarization.

- **Operational:**
  - `/search` returns a list of candidate articles with links and relevance ratings -- it does NOT generate a synthesized article.
  - Free-form messages (non-command, non-URL) trigger article generation -- the user's message is the seed prompt for the RAG pipeline.
  - These are two distinct behaviors sharing some underlying infrastructure (vector search, Qdrant, embedding).

## Existing State

- **Query detection:** `src/query/detector.ts` uses heuristics (question words, `?`, trigger phrases) to detect queries. Returns true/false.
- **Query service:** `src/query/service.ts` embeds the query, searches Qdrant for top-3 results, and formats a ranked list of titles + URLs + truncated summaries. No LLM generation.
- **LLM client:** `src/llm/client.ts` supports `embed()`, `embedBatch()`, and `summarize()` (chat completions). Only one prompt template exists (summarization).
- **Discord integration:** `src/discord/client.ts` registers only `/stats` as a slash command. No file attachment handling exists anywhere. `src/index.ts` routes interactions and messages.
- **Interfaces:** `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts` defines `CommandHandler` interface with `findHandler()` helper, but no commands implement it yet.
- **Database content:** The `links` table stores full extracted content, summaries, embeddings, and metadata. Content is available for RAG context.

## Desired Change

1. **`/search` slash command (candidate retrieval):**
   - Register `/search` as a Discord slash command accepting a `query` text parameter.
   - Implement using the `CommandHandler` interface pattern from `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts`.
   - On invocation: send status message -> embed query -> search Qdrant for top-10 results -> categorize results by tiered relevance (highly relevant, somewhat relevant, etc.) -> reply with summary in message body + `.md` file attachment with full list of candidate articles, links, and relevance tiers.
   - This does NOT invoke the LLM for article synthesis.

2. **Free-form article generation (RAG pipeline):**
   - Modify the existing `onMonitoredMessage` flow: when a message is not a slash command and does not contain URLs, treat it as a seed prompt for article generation. This replaces the current `isLikelyContentQuery` heuristic detection with a broader trigger -- any text message in the monitored channel that isn't a command or URL submission becomes a generation prompt.
   - Pipeline: embed the prompt -> retrieve top-10 results from Qdrant -> build a RAG prompt using the user's seed prompt and the full stored content of retrieved links -> send to LLM (using `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`) -> return the generated article as a `.md` file attachment with a summary in the message body.
   - The generated article includes source quality metadata at the top (tiered relevance, number of sources used).
   - RAG prompt template instructs the LLM to synthesize a coherent article from the provided context, with the user's message as the guiding prompt.

3. **Discord file attachment support:**
   - Implement `.md` file attachment creation and sending via discord.js `MessageAttachment` (or equivalent in current discord.js version).
   - Filename pattern: `<sanitized-query>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.

4. **Configuration additions:**
   - `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`: configurable model for article generation (separate from `LLM_MODEL`).
   - Similarity score thresholds for tiered relevance reporting (configurable bands).
   - Top-k retrieval count (default 10).

5. **User feedback during processing:**
   - Send an interim status message to the channel while the bot is processing (for both `/search` and article generation).
   - Update or follow up with the final response containing results/article.

## Related Work

- **SB-0MN1L2P9Z03HOQJN**: "Create a Discord bot that will process incoming messages with links" (completed epic) - Established the entire bot architecture including query detection, vector search, and LLM integration.
- **SB-0MN1MK6YF17YCHS1**: "Implement semantic query detection and retrieval replies" (completed) - Current query detection and link-list response. This work item replaces the implicit query behavior with article generation.
- **SB-0MN4OXL5P1XUMOAF**: "Create CLI tool for database shell access" (open) - Contains `sb search` CLI command (SB-0MN4QH9MZ03672Q5) which is separate from the Discord `/search` command but shares the same underlying search infrastructure.
- **SB-0MN7TJ3Z4006GCP2**: "Defer embeddings to backfill" (in-progress) - Changes embedding architecture. The RAG pipeline depends on embeddings being available in Qdrant, which BackfillService now manages.

### Key code files:
- `src/query/service.ts` - QueryService to be extended with tiered relevance and article generation.
- `src/query/detector.ts` - Query detection heuristics (to be adjusted for free-form prompt detection).
- `src/llm/client.ts` - LLM client to be extended with article generation method.
- `src/discord/client.ts` - Slash command registration (add `/search`).
- `src/index.ts` - Message routing (modify flow: `/search` -> candidate list; free-form -> article generation).
- `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts` - CommandHandler interface to implement.
- `src/vector/qdrant-store.ts` - Qdrant search (increase top-k to 10, expose similarity scores).

## Risks & Assumptions

**Risks:**
- **LLM context window limits:** Top-10 full content entries may exceed the LLM's context window. Mitigation: truncate or chunk context to fit within model limits; make max context size configurable.
- **Generation latency:** Article generation may take 10-30+ seconds depending on the model. Mitigation: interim status messages provide feedback; consider timeout with graceful failure.
- **Model switching overhead:** If `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL` differs from `LLM_MODEL`, switching models on the LLM server may add latency. Mitigation: this is an accepted trade-off per the deferred-embedding architecture.
- **Low-quality generation with sparse context:** When few relevant sources exist, the article may be thin or speculative. Mitigation: tiered source quality metadata in the article lets users assess reliability.
- **Discord rate limits:** Sending interim messages + final reply with attachment could trigger rate limits under heavy use. Mitigation: standard discord.js rate limit handling; consider debouncing rapid queries.
- **Free-form detection false positives:** Treating all non-command, non-URL messages as article generation prompts may trigger on casual conversation. Mitigation: may need to refine detection heuristics or add an opt-out mechanism; record as a follow-up work item if needed.
- **Scope creep:** Temptation to add features like conversation follow-up, article caching, or advanced formatting. Mitigation: record enhancement ideas as separate work items linked to this item rather than expanding scope.
- **Dependency on deferred embeddings (SB-0MN7TJ3Z4006GCP2):** If the backfill work item is not completed and merged, embeddings may not be populated in Qdrant, causing search to return no or incomplete results. Mitigation: this item should not be started until SB-0MN7TJ3Z4006GCP2 is merged and backfill has run.
- **Incomplete content for some links:** Some stored links may have NULL or partial content due to extraction failures (paywalls, dynamic content). Mitigation: skip NULL-content results when building RAG context; note in source metadata if fewer sources than expected were usable.

**Assumptions:**
- The LLM server supports chat completions with sufficient context window for RAG (at least 8K tokens of context).
- Full extracted content is stored in the `links` table and available for retrieval (confirmed via codebase review). Some links may have NULL content due to extraction failures; the pipeline must handle this gracefully.
- Qdrant search returns similarity scores that can be used for tiered relevance reporting.
- discord.js v13 supports file attachments via `MessageAttachment` or equivalent API.
- The `CommandHandler` interface is stable and suitable for this use case.
- The monitored channel is primarily used for link sharing and knowledge queries; casual conversation is minimal, reducing false positive risk for free-form article generation.

---

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q: Should both behaviors (slash command and automatic query handling) be included, or just one?**
  Answer (user): "Both behaviors" -- implement `/search` slash command AND automatic article generation for non-command messages. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: What should happen to the current link-list response when article generation is added?**
  Answer (user): Initially "Both modes coexist". Revised in follow-up: `/search` returns candidate articles with links and relevance ratings (no article generation); free-form messages generate articles. Source: interactive reply. Final: revised -- see next entry.

- **Q: Clarification on /search vs. free-form behavior split.**
  Answer (user): "Search should only return the candidate articles, their links and their similarity scores/ratings. Free form prompts will create articles." The user's free-form message becomes the seed prompt for article generation. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should article generation use stored summaries, stored full content, or re-fetch original pages?**
  Answer (user): "Stored full content" -- use the full extracted content stored in the database. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: How many results (top-k) should be retrieved for RAG context?**
  Answer (user): "Top 10" -- expand retrieval from current top-3 to top-10. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should article generation use the same LLM model as summarization, or a separately configurable model?**
  Answer (user): "Separate config" -- add a new config variable (e.g., `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`) for generation. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: How should the .md attachment filename be derived?**
  Answer (user): "Both (query + timestamp)" -- e.g., `how-to-deploy-kubernetes-20260326.md`. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: What should the bot do when no relevant results are found?**
  Answer (user): "Provide meta-data at the top of the article that indicates how many good quality sources were found and used." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should the bot provide feedback while generating?**
  Answer (user): "Send a message back to discord indicating the current status." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should the prior closure comment be considered stale?**
  Answer (user): "Treat as fresh work" -- existing query detection and link-list response are the baseline; article generation is entirely new work. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: How should 'good quality' sources be determined?**
  Answer (user): "Tiered reporting" -- categorize sources as "highly relevant", "somewhat relevant", etc. based on similarity score bands. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should /search accept additional parameters beyond query text?**
  Answer (user): "Query text only" -- simple `/search <query>`. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Should the /search command use the CommandHandler interface or direct routing?**
  Answer (user): "Use CommandHandler interface" -- implement `/search` as a proper CommandHandler, setting the pattern for future commands. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: Confirm: /search returns candidate list; free-form text generates articles?**
  Answer (user): "Yes, that is correct. In addition the prompt in the freeform version will become the seed prompt for the article generation." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: For /search results, should tiered ratings, raw scores, or both be shown?**
  Answer (user): "Tiered ratings" -- show quality tiers (highly relevant, somewhat relevant, etc.). Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q: For /search results, should response be in message body, .md attachment, or both?**
  Answer (user): "Both" -- summary in message body, full details in .md attachment. Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

## Related work (automated report)

*Generated 2026-03-26 by find_related skill. Covers worklog items and repository files with direct relevance to the /search command and free-form RAG article generation feature (SB-0MN4BS01C10I8EHT).*

### Directly related work items

- **SB-0MN7TJ3Z4006GCP2**: "Defer embeddings to backfill to avoid model switching during ingestion" (in-progress, high priority). This work item moves embedding generation and Qdrant indexing to BackfillService. SB-0MN4BS01C10I8EHT depends on this being complete because the RAG pipeline and /search command both require embeddings to be present in Qdrant. The adaptive polling and Qdrant re-indexing implemented here directly affect search result availability.

- **SB-0MN1MK6YF17YCHS1**: "Implement semantic query detection and retrieval replies" (completed). Established `src/query/detector.ts` and `src/query/service.ts` which are the primary files to be modified. The current top-3 retrieval and `isLikelyContentQuery` heuristic will be replaced/extended by the /search command (top-10) and free-form article generation trigger.

- **SB-0MN3SFN3A0BWRFOA**: "Add semantic query trigger phrases" (completed). Extended `isLikelyContentQuery` with trigger phrases. The free-form article generation feature replaces this heuristic-based detection with a broader trigger (any non-command, non-URL message), making this prior work the baseline that will be superseded.

- **SB-0MN77KAL10OGS4P6**: "Define interfaces for sibling coordination" (completed). Defined the `CommandHandler` interface in `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts` with `findHandler()` helper, `CommandHandlerRegistry`, and usage examples. The /search command must implement this interface, making this the foundational contract for the new slash command.

- **SB-0MN1MK6YH018JRP7**: "Add LLM summary and embedding generation" (completed). Created `src/llm/client.ts` with `embed()`, `embedBatch()`, and `summarize()` methods. The article generation feature needs a new `generate()` or equivalent method on this client for RAG synthesis, using the separate `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL` config.

- **SB-0MN4QH9MZ03672Q5**: "sb search Command - Semantic Search" (open, child of CLI tool). Implements `sb search` for the CLI using the same `LinkRepository.searchSimilarLinks()` and `OpenAiCompatibleLlmClient.embed()` infrastructure. While the Discord /search command is distinct, both share the same vector search and embedding pipeline; implementation patterns and output formatting from one may inform the other.

### Potentially related work items

- **SB-0MN3UDQFM0LB0LAH**: "Modularize bot bootstrap and message orchestration" (open, plan_complete). Plans to extract command handlers, message routing, and presenters from the 914-line `src/index.ts`. The /search command will add new routing and handler code to `src/index.ts`; if modularization happens first, /search would be implemented in the new modular structure. If /search is implemented first, it may need to be refactored when modularization occurs. Coordination is recommended.

- **SB-0MN77J4RV0V98Y3Q**: "Extract CrawlCommandHandler module" (open). Extracts crawl command handling into a `CommandHandler` implementation. This is a sibling command handler extraction that will establish the first concrete `CommandHandler` implementation pattern. The /search command should follow the same pattern.

- **SB-0MN77JB3S0FSIAL3**: "Extract StatsCommandHandler module" (open). Another sibling `CommandHandler` extraction. Together with CrawlCommandHandler, this establishes the convention that /search should follow for slash command handling.

- **SB-0MN3UEBND02UT7OM**: "Centralize embedding dimension and vector normalization" (open). Addresses duplicated embedding dimension constants between ingestion and query paths. The /search and article generation features add new query paths that will use embeddings; centralized dimension handling would reduce drift risk.

- **SB-0MN3UEBPJ1JXRVPJ**: "Extract Discord response formatting policies into shared presenters" (open). Plans to centralize Discord message truncation and formatting. Both /search results and article generation summaries need to respect the 2000-character message limit; shared formatting utilities would benefit this feature.

- **SB-0MN7GF5AP06IC2NT**: "Convert HTML content stored in the database into markdown" (completed). Changed stored content from HTML to plain text. Since article generation uses stored full content as RAG context, the cleaner plain text output from this completed work directly improves RAG context quality.

- **SB-0MN771YC91W02SB2**: "Search sites daily at 06:00 from config.yaml" (open, intake_complete). A separate feature that also produces articles/summaries and posts to Discord. While architecturally independent, it shares the pattern of generating content from stored data and posting to Discord with buttons/attachments. Implementation patterns may cross-pollinate.

- **SB-0MN7B31W20S076RG**: "Add batch embedding support (LLM embed)" (open). Adds batch embedding capability to the LLM client. Could improve performance if the article generation RAG pipeline needs to embed multiple chunks, though the /search and article generation features primarily embed a single query string.

### Repository files for reference

- `src/query/service.ts:14` - `QueryService` class. Currently embeds query and searches Qdrant for top-3 results via `store.searchSimilarLinks()`. Must be extended to support top-10 retrieval, expose similarity scores for tiered relevance, and (for article generation) retrieve full stored content for RAG context.
- `src/query/detector.ts:39` - `isLikelyContentQuery()` function. Current heuristic-based query detection. Free-form article generation replaces this with broader trigger logic (any non-command, non-URL message).
- `src/llm/client.ts:39-75` - `OpenAiCompatibleLlmClient` with `embed()`, `embedBatch()`, `summarize()`. Needs a new article generation method using chat completions with RAG prompt template and `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`.
- `src/discord/client.ts:37,61` - `registerSlashCommands()`. Currently registers only `/stats`. Must be extended to register `/search` with a `query` text parameter.
- `src/index.ts:20,502` - Imports `isLikelyContentQuery` and uses it at line 502 for message routing. The `onMonitoredMessage` flow must be modified: /search -> candidate list; free-form text -> article generation pipeline.
- `src/interfaces/command-handler.ts:123-230` - `CommandHandler` interface, `CommandHandlerRegistry`, and `findHandler()` helper. The /search command must implement this interface.
- `src/vector/qdrant-store.ts:46` - `QdrantVectorStore.indexBatch()` and search methods. Search must be extended to return similarity scores for tiered relevance classification.
- `src/db/repository.ts:169` - `LinkRepository.searchSimilarLinks()`. Currently returns `StoredLink[]` without similarity scores. May need modification to expose scores for tiered relevance, or the Qdrant search adapter can provide this.
- `src/llm/embeddings.ts:36-51` - `OpenAiCompatibleEmbeddingProvider` wrapping the LLM client. Used by QueryService for embedding queries; the same provider will be used for /search and article generation query embedding.
- `src/config.ts` - Environment configuration. Must be extended with `LLM_ARTICLE_MODEL`, similarity score threshold bands, and top-k retrieval count.
data.title: /search command and free-form RAG article generation

data.description:
Summary:
Add support for the HuggingFace embedding model  to the proxy service and update the bot to call the proxy for embeddings using this model.

User story:
As a developer I want the proxy to provide embeddings from  so that the bot uses higher-quality, consistent embeddings served via our proxy.

Expected behaviour / acceptance criteria:
1. The proxy exposes the  embedding model behind its existing embedding endpoint (configurable name e.g.  or ).
2. When the bot requests embeddings through the proxy it uses the new model by default (or via a config switch).
3. Embedding output shape and dtype match what callers expect (vector length documented and validated).
4. Proper error handling and fallbacks: if the HF model cannot be reached, the proxy returns a clear error and the bot falls back to the previous embedding provider if configured.
5. Tests added: unit tests for proxy model selection and an integration test exercising an embedding request to the proxy (can be mocked).
6. Documentation updated: README / proxy config docs include how to configure HF API token (if required), model name, and any rate limits or cost notes.

Implementation notes / suggested approach:
- Proxy changes (primary):
  - Add a new model entry in the proxy embedding config mapping to .
  - Implement a codepath to call HuggingFace inference endpoint or the HF client library to obtain embeddings for that model. Use server-side HF API token via env var .
  - Ensure request/response mapping conforms to existing proxy embedding API so the bot needs minimal changes.
  - Add configurable timeout and retries.
- Bot changes (secondary):
  - Replace direct embedding calls (or swap model name passed to proxy) to request embeddings from the proxy with the new model by default.
  - Add feature-flag / config key (e.g.  or ) so we can roll back easily.
- Tests & CI:
  - Add unit tests for proxy model selection and error handling.
  - Add integration test that mocks HF responses to validate end-to-end behaviour.

Files likely to change (examples; adjust to repo layout):
- proxy/* (proxy server code — model registry/config, embedding handler)
- bot/* (where embeddings are requested)
- docs/ or README for configuration notes
- tests/* (unit and integration tests)

Environment / security:
- HF API token must be provided via  env var; add notes to deployment docs and ensure token is not committed.

Acceptance test (manual):
- Start proxy locally with  set to a valid token; make a POST to the proxy embedding endpoint requesting  and verify returned vector length and non-empty values.
- Start bot locally configured to use the proxy; verify the bot uses new embeddings and no runtime errors occur.

Notes / risks:
- Using a hosted HF endpoint may incur cost and rate limits; document and consider adding caching.

Please implement the proxy side first and then switch the bot to use the new model; create child work-items for implementation and tests as needed.
data.title: Add HuggingFace mxbai-embed-large-v1 embeddings to proxy and switch bot to use it

data.description:
Summary:
This task will rebuild embeddings for the entire database by removing or archiving current embeddings and ensuring each item is present in the backfill database/queue for reprocessing. The backfill worker(s) will then produce new embeddings using the proxy (configured to use ). Validate that the bot gradually consumes items from the backfill DB and that embeddings are reattached to source items correctly.

Goals / Acceptance criteria:
1. Remove or archive existing embeddings in the primary vectors store so they will be regenerated; do not delete source documents.
2. Ensure every document/record that needs embeddings has an entry in the backfill table/queue (id, payload reference, status ).
3. Implement or reuse a backfill worker that processes pending items and writes embeddings back to the vectors store, marking items as  or  with error info.
4. The bot should read from backfill DB/queue and process items at a rate-limited pace; there must be automated verification that the bot takes items off the backfill DB over time (e.g., an integration test or monitoring probe).
5. A safety mechanism exists to throttle/stop backfill to avoid rate limits/costs (configurable batch size, delay, concurrency).
6. Backfill must be idempotent and resumeable: retry failed items, skip items already processed, and allow safe re-run.
7. Logs and metrics are emitted: total items enqueued, processed, failed, processing rate.
8. Tests: unit tests for backfill queue handling; integration test that simulates a small set of items and verifies embeddings are produced and that the bot consumes backfill items over time.

Implementation details / suggested approach:
- Prep step: create a script  (or a small service code) that enumerates all source records and writes a backfill row for any missing or expired embedding. Mark existing embeddings as archived or move to  table if desired.
- Backfill table schema: . Use  to record  (or config key).
- Worker: create  that pulls  rows, calls the proxy embedding endpoint with the configured model, and writes embeddings to the vectors store. Support concurrency limits and exponential backoff on transient errors.
- Bot validation: add an integration test that starts a small backfill DB, enqueues N items, runs the worker and bot for a short period, and asserts that the bot removed items from backfill (status transitions from  ->  -> ) and that embeddings exist on the target store. The test can mock the proxy responses for speed/reliability. Also add a monitoring script that queries counts over time to show consumption.
- Safety & observability: allow dry-run mode, configurable , , , and  env vars.
- Rollback: provide a quick means to stop workers and restore archived embeddings if something goes wrong.

Manual acceptance test:
- Run  and inspect the planned backfill items.
- Run prepare (non-dry-run) and verify backfill DB has expected counts.
- Start backfill worker with small concurrency and verify items are processed and embeddings are written.
- Start the bot and verify it consumes items from the backfill DB over time (logs/metrics).

Files likely to change / be added:
- scripts/backfill/prepare_backfill.sh (or equivalent code)
- services/backfill/worker.{js,py,go} (depending on repo language)
- db/migrations/add_backfill_table.sql (if needed)
- tests/backfill/* (unit & integration tests)
- docs/backfill.md with runbook & env var notes

Risk / notes:
- This operation can be expensive and hit HF rate limits / cost. Use throttling and consider sampling first.
- Ensure the HF API token and proxy are configured before running.

Please proceed to implement the backfill preparation and worker. If you want, I can create additional child work items for: (1) prepare script & migration, (2) worker implementation, (3) tests, (4) docs and runbook.,--issue-type:task,--priority:high,--json:true

data.description:
# Intake Brief for Graceful Bot Shutdown

## Problem Statement
When the bot shuts down (either for maintenance, updates, or unexpected restarts), multiple issues occur:

1. **Lost in-progress items**: Items being processed are removed from the in-memory queue immediately when processing starts. The database marks them as 'processing', but on restart, only 'pending' items are loaded. Interrupted 'processing' items are never re-queued and are effectively lost.

2. **Stale status messages**: Status messages tracking queue progress and item processing are left behind in Discord channels, confusing users who may think processing is still ongoing.

3. **No shutdown notification**: Users receive no notification that the bot is going offline, leaving them uncertain about the status of their queued items.

## User Story
As a Discord user who has submitted URLs for processing, I want:
- My submitted URLs to be preserved and re-queued if processing is interrupted by a bot restart
- To be notified when the bot is shutting down for maintenance
- To see stale status messages cleaned up so I understand the bot is temporarily unavailable

## Success Criteria
- [ ] When the bot receives a shutdown signal (SIGTERM/SIGINT), it posts a 'Bot Closing Down for Maintenance' message to all channels with active status messages
- [ ] All existing progress status messages are deleted or updated with a 'Processing paused - bot restarting' notice
- [ ] All existing queue status messages are deleted or updated with a 'Queue processing paused - bot restarting' notice
- [ ] Items with database status 'processing' are re-queued on startup (treated as pending)
- [ ] The current in-memory processing item (if any) is preserved and re-added to the queue on restart
- [ ] The bot gracefully completes any current processing item before shutting down (with timeout)
- [ ] The database connection is properly closed before exit
- [ ] The shutdown process handles edge cases (e.g., Discord API unavailable, no active status messages)

## Constraints
- Must handle SIGTERM and SIGINT signals
- Must complete or gracefully abort current processing within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 30 seconds)
- Must not leave stale messages in Discord channels
- Must maintain existing database queue persistence (items remain queued for restart recovery)
- Should work with existing statusMessages and queueStatusMessages Maps in src/index.ts
- Must re-queue 'processing' items as 'pending' on restart
- Must handle errors gracefully - if Discord API is unavailable, log error and exit anyway
- Cannot guarantee processing completion for interrupted items - must re-queue them

## Existing State
- Items are removed from in-memory queue immediately when processing starts (queue.shift())
- Database status changes from 'pending' to 'processing' when item processing begins
- The DocumentQueue.initialize() method only loads items with status = 'pending'
- Items with status = 'processing' are NOT loaded on restart
- The bot currently has no graceful shutdown handling
- Status messages are tracked in in-memory Maps: statusMessages and queueStatusMessages
- The DocumentQueue processes items sequentially and can be interrupted mid-processing
- Database connections are managed via getDbPool() but not explicitly closed on exit
- The bot exits immediately on SIGTERM/SIGINT without cleanup

## Desired Change
- Modify DocumentQueue.initialize() to also load 'processing' items and treat them as pending
- Add method to re-queue the current in-memory processing item if shutdown occurs
- Add signal handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT in src/index.ts
- Implement cleanup function that:
  1. Re-queues current processing item back to the front of the queue
  2. Marks all 'processing' items as 'pending' in database
  3. Posts maintenance notification to channels with active status messages
  4. Deletes or updates all tracked status messages
  5. Completes current processing item or aborts after timeout
  6. Closes database connection
  7. Exits cleanly
- Ensure cleanup handles errors gracefully
- Maintain existing restart recovery functionality (SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q)

## Related Work
- SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q: Progress and queue messages fail after bot restart with queued items (parent - implements restart recovery)
- SB-0MN3RC13413ZRD9M: Fix progress updates when processing from queue (completed)
- SB-0MN3RDGWH02AP64E: Separate queue status and progress messages (completed)

## Related Work (Automated Report)

### Related Code Files

1. **src/index.ts** - Contains the in-memory statusMessages and queueStatusMessages Maps that track Discord messages, and the restoreStatusMessages() function for restart recovery. This is where graceful shutdown signal handlers must be added.

2. **src/ingestion/queue.ts** - Implements DocumentQueue class with initialize(), processQueue(), and queue management. The initialize() method currently only loads 'pending' items and needs modification to also load 'processing' items.

3. **src/db/queue-repository.ts** - Provides database access for queue entries including markProcessing() and getPending(). Currently getPending() only returns items with status 'pending' - needs update to also return 'processing' items.

### Related Work Items

1. **SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q: Progress and queue messages fail after bot restart with queued items** (parent, completed)
   - This parent work item restored status message tracking after restart. It established the restoreStatusMessages() function and channel caching mechanism that will be used during graceful shutdown cleanup.

2. **SB-0MN3RC13413ZRD9M: Fix progress updates when processing from queue** (completed)
   - Fixed progress tracking for queue items. The onProgress callback mechanism needs to be preserved during graceful shutdown to allow final status updates before cleanup.

3. **SB-0MN3RDGWH02AP64E: Separate queue status and progress messages** (completed)
   - Separated queue status and progress message handling. Both message types must be cleaned up during graceful shutdown.

## Risks & Assumptions
- Risk: Shutdown process takes too long if many status messages need cleanup - Mitigation: Implement timeout and parallel cleanup
- Risk: Discord API rate limits during bulk message deletion - Mitigation: Handle rate limit errors gracefully
- Risk: Current processing item cannot complete in time - Mitigation: Re-queue it and mark as pending
- Risk: Multiple 'processing' items accumulate in database from previous crashes - Mitigation: On startup, treat all 'processing' as 'pending' and re-queue them
- Risk: Race condition between shutdown signal and item completion - Mitigation: Implement proper state checking and locking
- Assumption: Discord bot token remains valid during shutdown
- Assumption: Database connection is healthy enough to close properly
- Assumption: Items can be safely re-processed (idempotent ingestion)
- Scope creep mitigation: Focus on cleanup, notification, and re-queuing - do NOT attempt to resume partial processing

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- Q: "What happens to items currently being processed when the bot restarts?" — Answer (ImplementAgent): Items are removed from the in-memory queue immediately when processing starts (queue.shift()). Database status changes to 'processing'. On restart, only 'pending' items are loaded via DocumentQueue.initialize() - 'processing' items are ignored and effectively lost. Source: code analysis in comment SB-C0MN4DLRU11UHEMXZ and review of src/ingestion/queue.ts:40-85. Final: yes.

- Q: "Are status messages currently being cleaned up on bot shutdown?" — Answer (ImplementAgent): No, the bot exits immediately on SIGTERM/SIGINT without any cleanup. Status messages remain in Discord channels indefinitely. Source: code analysis in src/index.ts:588 - no signal handlers present. Final: yes.

- Q: "Is there existing graceful shutdown handling?" — Answer (ImplementAgent): No. The bot has no signal handlers or cleanup logic. Database connections are managed via getDbPool() but not explicitly closed on exit. Source: code analysis in src/index.ts and src/db/client.ts. Final: yes.

- Q: "What is the current queue loading behavior?" — Answer (Map): DocumentQueue.initialize() calls repository.getPending() which only returns items with status = 'pending'. Items with status = 'processing' are excluded. Source: review of src/ingestion/queue.ts:42 and src/db/queue-repository.ts:37-44. Final: yes.

- Q: "How are status messages tracked?" — Answer (Map): Status messages are tracked in two Maps: statusMessages (keyed by Discord message ID) and queueStatusMessages (keyed by channel ID). These are populated during restoreStatusMessages() on startup. Source: src/index.ts:112-121. Final: yes.

- Q: "What existing restart recovery exists?" — Answer (Map): The parent work item SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q implemented restoreStatusMessages() which repopulates the Maps after restart. This functionality will be leveraged during graceful shutdown. Source: parent work item comments and src/index.ts:536-586. Final: yes.
data.title: Graceful shutdown with status message cleanup

data.description:
## Problem
Currently, when a 403 Forbidden error occurs, we only log the HTTP status code and status text (e.g., "HTTP 403: Forbidden"). This provides insufficient information to diagnose why access was denied.

## Context
403 errors can occur for multiple reasons:
- **YouTube API**: Quota exceeded, invalid API key, API key restrictions, or video not accessible
- **Crawl service**: robots.txt blocking, IP-based blocking, authentication required, or paywall
- **Article extraction**: Cloudflare protection, rate limiting, or authentication requirements

## Current Behavior
1. YouTube API (src/ingestion/youtube.ts:124-127): Logs generic "quota exceeded or invalid API key"
2. Crawl service (src/ingestion/crawl.ts:138-140): Throws generic error with status and statusText
3. Article extraction: Handled by external library, error details not captured

## Desired Enhancement
Extract and log additional details from 403 response bodies:
- Error codes/reasons from response JSON (for APIs)
- Response headers that indicate blocking reason (e.g., CF-RAY for Cloudflare)
- Retry-After header if present
- Response body preview (first 500 chars) for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Parse YouTube API 403 response body to extract specific error reason
- Parse crawl service 403 response body and headers for blocking details
- Add utility function to safely extract error details from HTTP responses
- Log detailed error information including specific error code/reason, response headers, and body preview
- Ensure sensitive information (API keys, tokens) is not logged
- Add tests for 403 error detail extraction

## Files to Modify
- src/ingestion/youtube.ts
- src/ingestion/crawl.ts
- src/utils/http-errors.ts (new)

## Related Work
- discovered-from:SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO
data.title: Enhance 403 error handling with detailed response parsing

data.description:
## Problem
Many websites block direct HTTP requests (403/401 errors) using:
- Cloudflare protection
- Bot detection (User-Agent filtering)
- JavaScript challenges
- Cookie/session requirements
- IP-based blocking

This causes extraction failures for sites that would work fine with a real browser.

## Solution
Add Playwright (headless Chrome/Firefox) as a fallback mechanism when direct HTTP fetching fails.

## Benefits
- Bypasses most bot detection (real browser fingerprint)
- Executes JavaScript (SPA support)
- Handles cookies and sessions automatically
- Can wait for dynamic content to load
- Rotatable user agents and browser profiles

## Implementation Plan

### 1. New PlaywrightExtractor class
- Use Playwright to load pages in headless browser
- Wait for DOM ready/network idle
- Extract content using same article-extractor library or native DOM parsing
- Extract metadata, title, images

### 2. Fallback chain in ContentExtractor
```
1. Try direct HTTP fetch (fast, low resource)
2. If 403/401/timeout → Try Playwright (slower, more reliable)
3. Return null if both fail
```

### 3. Configuration options
- `PLAYWRIGHT_ENABLED`: Enable/disable fallback
- `PLAYWRIGHT_TIMEOUT`: Page load timeout (default 30s)
- `PLAYWRIGHT_WAIT_UNTIL`: When to consider page loaded (networkidle, domcontentloaded)
- `PLAYWRIGHT_MAX_RETRIES`: Retry count for Playwright attempts
- `PLAYWRIGHT_HEADLESS`: Run in headless mode (default true)

### 4. Resource management
- Use Playwright browser context pooling (reuse browser instances)
- Limit concurrent Playwright instances
- Add circuit breaker for repeated failures

### 5. Error handling
- Log when Playwright fallback is used
- Track success rates for direct vs Playwright
- Timeout handling for slow pages
- Graceful degradation if Playwright unavailable

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/ingestion/playwright-extractor.ts (new)
- src/ingestion/extractor.ts - Add fallback logic
- src/ingestion/crawl.ts - Use Playwright for crawl 403s
- src/config.ts - Add Playwright configuration
- package.json - Add playwright dependency

## Dependencies
```bash
npm install playwright
npx playwright install chromium  # Install browser binaries
```

## Usage Example
```typescript
const extractor = new FallbackContentExtractor({
  primary: new ArticleExtractorContentExtractor(),
  fallback: new PlaywrightContentExtractor({
    timeout: 30000,
    waitUntil: 'networkidle'
  })
});

const content = await extractor.extract(url);
// Tries direct first, falls back to Playwright on 403
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for Playwright extractor
- Integration tests with known-blocking sites
- Mock Playwright for unit tests
- Test timeout and retry logic

## Performance Considerations
- Playwright is ~10x slower than direct fetch
- Use only as fallback, not primary
- Browser pooling to reduce startup overhead
- Optional: cache Playwright results

## Related Work
- discovered-from:SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO
- related-to:SB-0MN4FG8AP1U77HZ6 (403 error detail enhancement)
data.title: Add Playwright fallback for blocked content extraction

data.description:
Collect a very large set of interesting URLs related to game development, including best practices, interviews, case studies, lessons learned, business-focused tutorials, design-focused tutorials, generative AI, marketing, storytelling, and adjacent production/publishing topics.

User story:
As an operator, I want a broad, deep link collection for game development research so that I can explore high-value resources without needing content summaries.

Expected outcome:
A curated response containing many relevant URLs only, spanning multiple subtopics and source types.

Suggested implementation:
Search broadly across respected industry sources, GDC and conference materials, studio postmortems, business analysis sites, marketing resources, narrative design resources, and generative AI in games coverage. Deduplicate and group lightly by theme if useful, but do not summarize content.

Acceptance criteria:
- Provide a large set of relevant URLs related to game development and adjacent business/design topics.
- Include links covering best practices, interviews, case studies, lessons learned, business, design, generative AI, marketing, storytelling, and more.
- Avoid technical coding tutorials as the primary focus.
- Do not summarize the linked content in the final operator-facing response; provide URLs only or near-only.
- Research should be meaningfully broad and deep across multiple categories and sources.

## Related Work Report

This report identifies existing work items and codebase files directly related to creating the CLI tool for database shell access (SB-0MN4OXL5P1XUMOAF).

### Related Work Items

**Database & Storage Layer:**
1. **SB-0MN1MK6YB0K5YDI2**: Implement PostgreSQL + pgvector storage layer (completed)
   - Implements the database schema and persistence layer that the CLI will use
   - Contains migrations for links table, pgvector extension, app_checkpoints
   - LinkRepository with upsert and search operations

**Ingestion Pipeline (for `sb add` command):**
2. **SB-0MN1MK6YG1L59RGS**: Build link ingestion and content extraction pipeline (completed)
   - URL detection and content extraction using article-extractor
   - OpenGraph/meta image extraction
   - Processing failure handling with emoji reactions
   - The CLI `sb add` command will reuse this entire pipeline

3. **SB-0MN1MK6YH018JRP7**: Add LLM summary and embedding generation (completed)
   - Embedding generation via custom LLM proxy for semantic search
   - CLI will need this for generating embeddings when searching

4. **SB-0MN2BXQDI0AUHU3M**: Add crawl command handling for linked pages (completed)
   - Shows how ingestion pipeline can be invoked outside of Discord messages
   - Demonstrates pattern for programmatic URL ingestion

**Semantic Search (for `sb search` command):**
5. **SB-0MN1MK6YF17YCHS1**: Implement semantic query detection and retrieval replies (completed)
   - Uses `searchSimilarLinks` method from LinkRepository
   - The CLI `sb search` command will reuse this search logic

6. **SB-0MN3SFN3A0BWRFOA**: Add semantic query trigger phrases (completed)
   - Related to query detection and semantic search infrastructure

**Queue & Progress System:**
7. **SB-0MN1MK71A0ALK4OO**: Startup recovery, configuration, and end-to-end validation (completed)
   - Uses DocumentQueue for sequential URL processing
   - Demonstrates patterns for database queue operations

8. **SB-0MN4B5EJ50OUD62Q**: Progress and queue messages fail after bot restart with queued items (completed)
   - Involves DocumentQueueRepository for queue persistence
   - Related to queue-based processing that CLI may leverage

### Related Code Files

**Database Layer (CLI will reuse directly):**
- `src/db/client.ts` - `getDbPool()` returns pg Pool for database connections
- `src/db/repository.ts` - `LinkRepository` class with:
  - `upsertLink()` - for storing ingested URLs
  - `searchSimilarLinks()` - for semantic search with pgvector
  - `getStats()` - for database statistics
  - `Queryable` interface for flexible query execution
- `src/db/queue-repository.ts` - `DocumentQueueRepository` for queue operations
- `src/db/migrate.ts` - Existing CLI pattern using tsx (npm run db:migrate)

**Ingestion Pipeline (for `sb add`):**
- `src/ingestion/service.ts` - `IngestionService` class with:
  - URL processing pipeline (fetch, extract, summarize, embed, store)
  - Progress callback system for status updates
  - YouTube transcript handling
- `src/ingestion/queue.ts` - `DocumentQueue` for sequential URL processing
- `src/ingestion/extractor.ts` - Content extraction via article-extractor
- `src/ingestion/url.ts` - URL normalization and validation utilities
- `src/ingestion/youtube.ts` - YouTube API client for transcript fetching

**LLM/Embedding (for search query embedding):**
- `src/llm/client.ts` - `OpenAiCompatibleLlmClient` with:
  - `embed()` method for generating query embeddings
  - `summarize()` method for content summarization
- `src/llm/embeddings.ts` - `OpenAiCompatibleEmbeddingProvider`

**Configuration:**
- `src/config.ts` - Environment configuration (DATABASE_URL, LLM settings)
- `src/logger.ts` - Logging utility

**Existing CLI Patterns:**
- `package.json` - Current scripts use tsx for TypeScript execution:
  - `"db:migrate": "tsx src/db/migrate.ts"` - pattern to follow
  - No `build:cli` script yet (needs implementation)
- `scripts/start-db.sh` - Database startup script
- `scripts/wait-for-db.js` - Database connection waiting utility

**Database Schema:**
- `migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` - links table, pgvector extension
- `migrations/003_add_transcript_column.sql` - YouTube transcript support
- `migrations/004_backfill_queue.sql` - backfill_queue table
- `migrations/005_document_queue.sql` - document processing queue

### Key Insights for Implementation

1. **No existing CLI tool**: The codebase currently has no CLI entry point. The migration script (`src/db/migrate.ts`) demonstrates a pattern for standalone TypeScript execution using tsx.

2. **Reuse patterns**: The CLI should follow the pattern established by `npm run db:migrate` which uses tsx to run TypeScript directly. For binary distribution, use npms

## Summary
Create the CLI entry point with command routing, help text, and integration with existing configuration system.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `sb --help` displays usage information for all commands
- [ ] `sb <unknown-command>` returns error message including unknown command name and suggests `sb --help`
- [ ] CLI loads configuration from environment via existing `config.ts`
- [ ] CLI outputs `Error: DATABASE_URL environment variable is required` to stderr when DATABASE_URL is missing
- [ ] Exit codes: 0 (success), 1 (error), 2 (invalid args)

## Minimal Implementation
- Create `src/cli/index.ts` as entry point using `tsx` pattern
- Use `process.argv` for argument parsing (no new dependencies)
- Import and reuse `config.ts` for environment validation
- Implement help text generation for all commands
- Add error handling wrapper with appropriate exit codes

## Dependencies
None (foundation feature)

## Deliverables
- `src/cli/index.ts` - CLI entry point
- `src/cli/commands/` directory structure
- Updated `package.json` with `bin` field for `sb` command
- Unit tests for argument parsing and help text

## Open Questions
