## Summary

Add CLI flags (--channel-id, --message-id, --author-id) so the Discord bot can pass context to the CLI subprocess as plain string arguments, and add --format/--webhook-url flags for output control.

## User Experience Change

Bot maintainers can invoke CLI with Discord context as plain string args (e.g., `sb queue --channel-id X --message-id Y <url>`). CLI users get --format and --webhook-url flags for output control.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `sb queue --channel-id <id> --message-id <id> --author-id <id> <url>` stores the provided context in the queue DB entry's generic fields
- `sb add --channel-id <id> --webhook-url <url> <url>` uses the webhook presenter and includes channel context in events
- All context flags are optional; CLI works without them (defaulting to 'cli' source values)
- `--format ndjson` and `--ndjson` select NDJSON output mode
- `--webhook-url <url>` enables webhook presenter (can be combined with stdout output)
- Passing an invalid --webhook-url (not a URL) results in a clear validation error and non-zero exit
- `--help` for each command documents the new flags
- Tests verify flag parsing and correct data flow

## Minimal Implementation

- Extend CLI arg parser in `src/cli/index.ts` to recognise new flags (--channel-id, --message-id, --author-id, --format, --ndjson, --webhook-url)
- Pass parsed context to command functions
- Commands propagate context to presenters and DB writes
- Validate --webhook-url is a valid URL

## Dependencies

- Feature 3: Remove Discord Coupling from CLI Commands (SB-0MN9CJIXA0031C2I)
- Feature 4: Implement CLI Progress Presenters (SB-0MN9CK3AL001I8S8)

## Deliverables

- Code changes to CLI arg parser and commands
- Updated --help output
- Flag-parsing tests

## Key Files

- `src/cli/index.ts` (arg parser)
- `src/cli/commands/add.ts` (propagate context and presenter selection)
- `src/cli/commands/queue.ts` (propagate context to DB writes)
data.title: Add CLI Flags for Context Pass-Through

data.description:
## Summary

Replace all direct imports of CLI modules and direct ingestion/queue calls in `src/index.ts` (and any bot files) with `child_process.execFile`/`spawn` calls to the `sb` binary, capturing progress via stdout NDJSON.

## User Experience Change

Discord users see no change — progress messages, reactions, queue status, and final summaries work as before. The bot now orchestrates all operations through the CLI subprocess rather than directly calling ingestion/queue code. This restores bot functionality after the temporary breakage from Feature 3.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/index.ts` (and any bot file) has zero import statements from `src/cli/`
- Bot invokes `sb add`, `sb queue` via `child_process.spawn` for URL ingestion/queueing
- Bot passes Discord context (--channel-id, --message-id, --author-id) as CLI arguments
- Bot reads NDJSON from CLI stdout to reconstruct Discord progress messages (edit/delete status messages, send final summaries)
- Crawl command in bot invokes `sb` subprocess (or equivalent subprocess pattern)
- /stats slash command invokes `sb stats --format json` and formats the JSON into a Discord embed
- Queue polling: bot monitors CLI subprocess for queue processing status
- Graceful shutdown: bot kills child processes on SIGTERM/SIGINT
- If CLI subprocess exits with non-zero code, bot logs the error and does not crash
- No functional regression: existing Discord user experience (progress messages, reactions, queue status) is preserved
- Integration tests verify subprocess invocation patterns

## Minimal Implementation

- Create `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` utility for spawning CLI processes and parsing NDJSON output
- Refactor `src/index.ts` to use cli-runner.ts instead of direct service instantiation
- Map NDJSON progress events back to Discord message updates
- Handle subprocess errors (non-zero exit, stderr output)
- Kill child processes on shutdown
- Preserve startup recovery, backfill, and queue polling through subprocess calls

## Dependencies

- Feature 3: Remove Discord Coupling from CLI Commands (SB-0MN9CJIXA0031C2I)
- Feature 4: Implement CLI Progress Presenters (SB-0MN9CK3AL001I8S8)
- Feature 5: Add CLI Flags for Context Pass-Through (SB-0MN9CKJVH007RR6G)

## Deliverables

- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` — CLI subprocess utility
- Refactored `src/index.ts` — bot entrypoint using subprocess calls
- Integration tests for subprocess invocation
- Updated graceful shutdown handling

## Key Files

- `src/index.ts` (940 lines, major refactor target)
- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` (new)
- `src/discord/client.ts` (unchanged, pure bot code)
data.title: Refactor Bot to Invoke CLI as Subprocess

data.description:
## Summary

Add automated checks to enforce the CLI/bot boundary and update README with new CLI usage patterns.

## User Experience Change

Developers get CI-time enforcement that the CLI/bot boundary is never violated. README accurately documents CLI usage without Discord dependencies, with NDJSON and webhook examples.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A CI-runnable lint/test script verifies: no discord.js imports in `src/cli/**`, no `src/cli/` imports in bot files, no DISCORD_* references in CLI code
- Boundary test fails if any discord.js import is added to `src/cli/**`
- README CLI section updated: removed requirement for DISCORD_* env vars, added NDJSON output examples, webhook usage examples, bot-invocation patterns
- `package.json` scripts updated if needed (e.g., separate build:cli if beneficial)
- `.env.example` updated to document which vars are CLI-only vs bot-only
- Running `npm test` includes the boundary verification
- All tests pass end-to-end

## Minimal Implementation

- Create `tests/boundary.test.ts` that scans import graphs and env var references in `src/cli/**`
- Update README CLI documentation section
- Update `.env.example` with CLI-only vs bot-only annotations
- Add boundary test to default test suite

## Dependencies

- Feature 6: Refactor Bot to Invoke CLI as Subprocess (SB-0MN9CL02100258DH) — all refactoring must be complete

## Deliverables

- `tests/boundary.test.ts` — automated boundary enforcement test
- Updated README.md
- Updated .env.example

## Key Files

- `tests/boundary.test.ts` (new)
- `README.md`
- `.env.example`
- `package.json`

## Summary

Create `src/config/cli.ts` with a Zod schema containing only CLI-relevant configuration fields, extracted from the current monolithic `src/config.ts`.

## User Experience Change

After this task, a standalone CLI config module exists that can be imported without requiring Discord environment variables.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/config/cli.ts` exists in a new `src/config/` directory
- Exports a validated config object using Zod with fields: DATABASE_URL, LLM_BASE_URL, LLM_MODEL, LLM_EMBEDDING_MODEL, LLM_EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE, LLM_EMBEDDING_MAX_CHARS, LLM_EMBEDDING_DIM, LLM_MAX_RETRIES, LLM_RETRY_DELAY_MS, SUMMARIZER_MAX_TOKENS, SUMMARIZER_CHUNK_CHARS, QDRANT_URL, QDRANT_COLLECTION, YOUTUBE_API_KEY, YOUTUBE_CAPTION_LANGUAGE, ENABLE_YOUTUBE_CAPTIONS, CRAWL_USER_AGENT, CRAWL_DELAY_MS, LOG_LEVEL, INGEST_SUCCESS_REACTION, INGEST_FAILURE_REACTION, INGEST_UPDATE_REACTION
- Schema does NOT include: DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID, ALLOWED_FILE_URL_USERS, BACKFILL_INTERVAL_MS, MAX_BACKFILL_ATTEMPTS, STARTUP_RECOVERY_MAX_MESSAGES
- Config parses successfully with no DISCORD_* env vars present
- Parsing with DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN set but no DATABASE_URL still fails (Discord vars are ignored, not treated as errors)
- Includes a CLI-specific `formatConfigError` that lists only CLI-relevant vars in error messages

## Minimal Implementation

- Create `src/config/` directory
- Create `src/config/cli.ts` extracting CLI fields from the existing Zod schema in `src/config.ts`
- Include CLI-specific `formatConfigError` function (omit Discord vars from error output)
- Export typed `cliConfig` object and `cliConfigSchema` for extension by bot config

## Dependencies

None (first task in sequence)

## Deliverables

- `src/config/cli.ts`

## Key Files

- `src/config.ts` (source of fields to extract)

## Summary

data.description:
## Summary

Build `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` to spawn CLI processes and parse NDJSON output for Discord bot consumption.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` exports functions: `runCliCommand()`, `runAddCommand()`, `runQueueCommand()`, `runStatsCommand()`
- Runner spawns `sb` binary with proper environment and working directory
- NDJSON stdout is parsed into progress event objects with type-safe interfaces
- stderr is captured and logged without crashing the bot
- Subprocess exit codes are handled: 0=success, non-zero=logged error, bot continues
- Context flags (--channel-id, --message-id, --author-id) are correctly passed as CLI arguments
- Runner returns a promise that resolves with final result or rejects with structured error

## Minimal Implementation

- Create `src/bot/` directory with `cli-runner.ts`
- Implement `spawn()` wrapper with NDJSON line parsing using readline interface
- Map CLI exit codes to success/failure without throwing unhandled exceptions
- Type definitions for NDJSON progress events matching CLI presenters

## Dependencies

Parent: Refactor Bot to Invoke CLI as Subprocess (SB-0MN9CL02100258DH)

## Deliverables

- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` - CLI subprocess utility module
- Type definitions for NDJSON progress events
- Error handling utilities for subprocess management
data.title: Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility

data.description:
## Summary

Replace all direct CLI imports and service instantiations in `src/index.ts` with calls to cli-runner.ts functions.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/index.ts` has zero import statements from `src/cli/` or CLI command modules
- `src/index.ts` imports only from `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` for CLI operations
- URL ingestion calls `sb add` via subprocess with NDJSON progress parsing
- Queue operations call `sb queue` via subprocess with proper context flags
- `/stats` slash command invokes `sb stats --format json` and formats into Discord embed
- Crawl functionality invokes `sb` subprocess (or equivalent pattern)
- Progress messages are reconstructed from NDJSON and edited/sent to Discord
- Discord reactions (✅/❌) are applied based on subprocess results
- Queue polling monitors subprocess status for completion
- No functional regression: existing Discord UX preserved

## Minimal Implementation

- Replace direct service instantiation with cli-runner function calls
- Map NDJSON progress events back to Discord message updates using existing formatProgressMessage logic
- Update URL extraction → CLI invocation flow
- Update queue monitoring to use subprocess status checks
- Preserve startup recovery and backfill polling through subprocess calls

## Dependencies

- Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility (child of SB-0MN9CL02100258DH)

## Deliverables

- Refactored `src/index.ts` - bot entrypoint using subprocess calls only
- Integration points between NDJSON events and Discord message updates
data.title: Refactor Bot Entrypoint for Subprocess Calls

data.description:
## Summary

Verify subprocess invocation patterns work end-to-end and implement clean process termination.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Integration tests verify `runCliCommand()` spawns correct process with expected arguments
- Tests verify NDJSON parsing handles valid progress events and malformed lines gracefully
- Tests verify non-zero exit codes don't crash the test runner (error is logged, operation fails gracefully)
- `SIGTERM`/`SIGINT` handlers kill active CLI child processes before bot exits
- Child processes receive `SIGTERM` then `SIGKILL` after timeout if not terminated
- No zombie processes remain after bot shutdown
- Tests cover: successful command, failed command, partial NDJSON, abrupt process exit

## Minimal Implementation

- Create `tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts` with mocked spawn and NDJSON streams
- Add graceful shutdown handler in `src/index.ts` (or separate `src/bot/shutdown.ts`)
- Track active child processes in a Set/CleanupManager
- Implement SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers that terminate children before process.exit
- Add test utilities for simulating NDJSON streams and process exits

## Dependencies

- Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility (child of SB-0MN9CL02100258DH)
- Refactor Bot Entrypoint for Subprocess Calls (child of SB-0MN9CL02100258DH)

## Deliverables

- `tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts` - integration tests for subprocess invocation
- Graceful shutdown handling with child process cleanup
- Test utilities for CLI subprocess mocking

## Summary
The epic has a clear, documented upgrade path (CLI_EXTRACTION_PLAN.md) but the planned library replacements and the critical CLI library move have not been implemented. Config validation using zod is already implemented, however core replacements (logger → pino, LLM client, Qdrant, pgvector) and the CLI move remain outstanding, so the epic cannot be closed.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | All identified custom modules replaced with open source equivalents | unmet | package.json:18-22 — dependencies list contains discord.js, dotenv, zod; pino/openai/@qdrant/pgvector are not present |
| 2 | Zero breaking changes to CLI and Bot functionality | unmet | src/index.ts:62-71 — bot replies that CLI features are "temporarily unavailable - CLI has been extracted to openBrain repository", indicating behavior changes |
| 3 | Improved error handling and edge case coverage | partial | dist/src/config/cli.js:34-45 — zod safeParse + formatConfigError provide richer validation/error messages, but not applied uniformly across modules |
| 4 | Reduced codebase size by ~30-40% | unmet | src/logger.ts:10 — custom Logger class still present; no large-scale removals detected |
| 5 | Clear upgrade path documented | met | CLI_EXTRACTION_PLAN.md:70-78 — two-phase plan and concrete refactor steps (LLM, Qdrant, pino, pgvector) are documented |

## Children Status

### Replace LLM Client with openai npm package (SB-0MNBSDEV8000N7GA) — blocked/idea

No acceptance criteria defined.

No acceptance criteria defined.

### Replace custom logger with pino (SB-0MNBSDUQE008P7CU) — open/idea

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | Remove src/logger.ts | unmet | src/logger.ts:10 — custom Logger class exists and has not been removed |
| 2 | Add pino to package.json | unmet | package.json:18-22 — pino (or pino-pretty) not listed in dependencies |

| 4 | Maintain log levels | partial | src/logger.ts:11 — Logger constructor accepts a level; dist/src/config/cli.js:28 — LOG_LEVEL is defined in config |
| 5 | Add pretty printing in dev | unmet | package.json:18-22 — no pino-pretty or equivalent dev-time pretty printer present |
| 6 | Configure from LOG_LEVEL env var | partial | dist/src/config/cli.js:28; src/index.ts:11 — LOG_LEVEL exists and is passed to Logger, easing migration |
| 7 | All tests pass | partial | tests/logger.test.ts:9-12 — tests cover current Logger implementation; no tests asserting a pino migration are present |

### Improve pgvector integration with pgvector npm package (SB-0MNBSEABT007T6E2) — blocked/idea

No acceptance criteria defined.

data.description:
# Epic: Refactor for Libraries

## Goal
Replace custom implementations with battle-tested open source libraries to reduce maintenance burden and improve reliability.

## Overview
This epic tracks the migration from custom-built shared modules to established open source libraries. This will significantly reduce the amount of code we need to maintain while gaining production-grade features, security updates, and community support.

## Success Criteria
- All identified custom modules replaced with open source equivalents
- Zero breaking changes to CLI and Bot functionality
- Improved error handling and edge case coverage
- Reduced codebase size by ~30-40%
- Clear upgrade path documented

## Strategy
Replace custom implementations where mature open source alternatives exist, keeping only domain-specific business logic (repository patterns, ingestion workflows).

## Dependencies
- Must maintain backward compatibility with existing CLI/Bot interface
- Requires thorough testing of each replacement
- Should be completed before CLI repository extraction

## Scope
### In Scope:
- LLM client replacement
- Logger replacement  
- Qdrant client replacement
- Database client simplification
- pgvector integration improvement

### Out of Scope:
- Repository pattern (domain logic)
- Ingestion service (workflow orchestration)
- Business logic changes
- Database schema changes
data.title: Refactor for Libraries

data.description: Replace custom LLM client with openai npm package. CLI-ONLY LIBRARY - This will be MOVED into src/cli/lib/ first (without refactoring), then replaced with openai package after CLI extraction. Benefits: 10.8k stars, production-ready, handles retries/streaming. Move file to src/cli/lib/llm/ first, then refactor.
data.title: Replace LLM Client with openai npm package

data.description: Replace Qdrant wrapper with @qdrant/js-client-rest. CLI-ONLY LIBRARY - This will be MOVED into src/cli/lib/ first (without refactoring), then replaced after CLI extraction. Benefits: Official Qdrant SDK, maintained by Qdrant team. Move file to src/cli/lib/vector/ first, then refactor.
data.title: Replace Qdrant wrapper with @qdrant/js-client-rest

data.description: Replace the custom Logger class with the pino logging library. HIGH PRIORITY - This is a SHARED library used by both CLI and Bot. Must be refactored BEFORE CLI extraction since Bot will still depend on it. Benefits: 17.6k GitHub stars, extremely fast, JSON-first logging, built-in prettification, child loggers. Acceptance Criteria: Remove src/logger.ts, Add pino to package.json, Update all imports, Maintain log levels, Add pretty printing in dev, Configure from LOG_LEVEL env var, All tests pass.
data.title: Replace custom logger with pino

data.description: Improve pgvector integration with pgvector npm package. CLI-ONLY LIBRARY - Database layer will be MOVED into src/cli/lib/ first (without refactoring), then improved after CLI extraction. Benefits: Better type handling, distance functions. Move files to src/cli/lib/db/ first, then refactor.
data.title: Improve pgvector integration with pgvector npm package

data.description: Simplify database client by removing wrapper. CLI-ONLY LIBRARY - Database client will be MOVED into src/cli/lib/ first, then simplified after CLI extraction. Move files to src/cli/lib/db/ first, then refactor to use pg directly.
data.title: Simplify database client by removing wrapper

data.description: Refactor configuration validation to use more idiomatic zod patterns. HIGH PRIORITY - This is a SHARED library (src/config/cli.ts) used by both CLI and Bot. Bot config extends CLI config, so both depend on this. Must be refactored BEFORE CLI extraction. Benefits: Better error messages, type inference, maintainability. Acceptance Criteria: Refactor cliConfigSchema with idiomatic zod, Add descriptive error messages, Use transforms appropriately, Add URL validation, Add numeric range validation, All tests pass.
data.title: Improve config validation with idiomatic zod patterns

data.description:
## Summary

Document the current content extraction approach and evaluate alternatives for future consideration.

## Current State
Using @extractus/article-extractor for web content extraction.

## Goal
Document why we chose this library and what alternatives exist.

## Research Areas
1. **Current Library Assessment**
   - @extractus/article-extractor capabilities and limitations
   - Performance characteristics
   - Content type support (articles, PDFs, videos)

2. **Alternative Libraries to Evaluate**
   - @mozilla/readability - Mozilla's readability.js port
   - @postlight/parser - Postlight's Mercury parser
   - cheerio-based custom extractors
   - JSDOM-based extractors
   - Puppeteer/Playwright for JavaScript-heavy sites

3. **Specialized Extractors**
   - PDF parsing (pdf-parse, pdfjs)
   - Video metadata extraction
   - Social media embeds

## Deliverables
- [ ] Document current extraction pipeline
- [ ] Create comparison matrix of alternatives
- [ ] Document when to use each approach
- [ ] Add extraction performance benchmarks
- [ ] Document fallback strategies
- [ ] Create ADR (Architecture Decision Record) for extraction approach

## Evaluation Criteria
- Extraction accuracy
- Performance (speed)
- Content type coverage
- Maintenance status
- Dependencies (bundle size)
- Error handling

## Note
This is research/documentation only - no code changes required unless we decide to switch.

## Priority Rationale
Low priority - Current solution works well. This is future-proofing and knowledge documentation. Can be done in spare time or when extraction issues arise.
data.title: Document content extraction strategy and alternatives

data.description:
# Intake Draft: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command

Headline
--------
Wire the newly-implemented OpenBrain CLI `add` command into the Discord bot so the bot can add URLs using the CLI `ob add` flow and present progress/results to users.

Problem statement
-----------------
The `ob add` (CLI) command has been implemented in the openBrain CLI. The Discord bot currently extracts URLs and attempts to call the CLI (or previously did so), but the bot's add functionality needs to be explicitly wired to use the new `ob add` implementation and surface progress and final results back to Discord users.

Users
-----
- Server users who post URLs in monitored channels and expect the bot to add those URLs to OpenBrain.
- Server administrators who may run explicit add/queue slash commands or expect bot feedback.

Example user stories
-------------------
- As a user, when I post a URL in a monitored channel, the bot should run `ob add <url>` and reply with progress or a final confirmation that the URL was added.
- As a user, if the CLI is unavailable, the bot should report the failure in a single, clear message.

Success criteria
----------------
- When a user posts a URL in the monitored channel, the bot invokes the `ob add` CLI (via the existing cli-runner or runOpenBrainCommand) with appropriate context flags and replies with a final success or failure message.
- Progress events (if available via NDJSON) are mapped to ephemeral or threaded updates so users can see ingestion progress (minimum: final success/failure messages must appear).
- The implementation reuses existing utilities (src/bot/cli-runner.js or runOpenBrainCommand patterns) and preserves existing reply formatting (wrap URLs in backticks to avoid embeds).
- If CLI is not found or returns non-zero, bot replies with a helpful error explaining CLI unavailability.

Constraints
-----------
- The CLI may not be present on PATH in all deployments; solution should gracefully handle missing binary (fallback message).
- Do not change OpenBrain CLI behaviour; the bot should call the CLI as subprocesses (NDJSON mode preferred for progress events).
- Prefer minimal changes to bot code; reuse `src/bot/cli-runner.js` which already offers runAddCommand and runCliCommand.

Existing state
--------------
- The CLI `add` command exists under `dist/src/cli/commands/add.js` and `src/cli` (extracted to openBrain repo). It supports `--format ndjson`, and context flags `--channel-id`, `--message-id`, and `--author-id`.
- The bot entrypoint (`src/index.ts`) currently calls `addUrlToOpenBrain()` which uses `runOpenBrainCommand()` (execFile approach) and includes fallback behavior. There is also a `src/bot/cli-runner.js` implementation in `dist/` that provides `runAddCommand()` async generator to consume NDJSON progress events.

Desired change
--------------
- Wire the bot's URL handling path to use the CLI runner utility (prefer `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` or equivalent) to run `sb add --format ndjson <url>` and map progress events to Discord replies.
- Ensure context flags are passed (`--channel-id`, `--message-id`, `--author-id`) and that replies avoid creating embeds.

Related work
------------
- Split CLI from Discord Bot (SB-0MN8Q6A6E000LPUT) — repo-level extraction and boundary work.
- Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility (SB-0MNAGZOLK007GMAN) — provides runAddCommand and helpers.
- Add CLI Flags for Context Pass-Through (SB-0MN9CKJVH007RR6G) — added --channel-id/--message-id/--author-id flags.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
--------------------------------------

Q: "How should the bot present ingestion progress to users when the CLI emits NDJSON progress events?" — Answer (user): "Progress updates (threaded)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Q: "If the OpenBrain CLI binary is not found or returns an error, what should the bot do?" — Answer (user): "Single user-facing error (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Q: "Which approach should we use to invoke the `add` flow in the bot?" — Answer (user): "Use existing cli-runner (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

*If additional clarifying questions are required they will be added here. Open questions remain none at this time.*

## Draft notes
This is a draft intake brief. Please review and answer the clarifying questions appended in the interactive interview so I can finalize the brief and update the work item SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71.
data.title: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command

## Summary

Create the TypeScript source file  to replace the compiled JavaScript in . This module provides utilities to spawn the Usage: sb <command> [options]

Commands:
  add     Add a URL to the database
  queue   Queue a URL for later processing
  search  Perform semantic search on indexed content
  stats   Display database statistics

Options:
  --help      Show this help message
  --version   Show version information
  --verbose   Enable verbose output (JSON logging)

Progress Output Formats (add command):
  --format, -f <format>  Progress output format (default: auto)
                         - console: Human-friendly TTY output
                         - ndjson:  One JSON line per event
                         - webhook: POST events to webhook URL
                         - auto:    console in TTY, ndjson otherwise
  --ndjson               Shorthand for --format ndjson
  --webhook-url <url>    Webhook URL (required when format=webhook)

Context Flags (for bot integration):
  --channel-id <id>      Discord channel ID to associate with the operation
  --message-id <id>      Discord message ID to associate with the operation
  --author-id <id>       Discord author ID to associate with the operation

Examples:
  sb add https://example.com/article
  sb add --verbose https://example.com/article
  sb add --format ndjson https://example.com/article | jq .

data.description:
## Summary

Implement support for mapping NDJSON progress events from the CLI `sb add --format ndjson` command to Discord threaded replies. Users should see ingestion progress updates as the CLI processes URLs.

## Background

The `runAddCommand()` function is an async generator that yields progress events during URL ingestion. These events include phases like "downloading", "extracting", "embedding", and "completed". Currently, the bot only shows final success/failure messages. This work item adds threaded progress updates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create a thread from the original message when processing begins
- [ ] Map NDJSON progress events to user-friendly Discord messages:
  - downloading: "Downloading content from URL..."
  - extracting: "Extracting text content..."
  - embedding: "Generating embeddings..."
  - completed: "✅ Added to OpenBrain: URL"
  - failed: "❌ Failed: error message"
- [ ] Send progress updates as ephemeral messages or in the thread
- [ ] Handle multiple URLs in a single message (create separate threads or sequential updates)
- [ ] Gracefully handle thread creation failures (fallback to channel replies)
- [ ] Progress updates should not spam users (throttle or batch similar events)
- [ ] Final result must always be shown regardless of progress visibility
- [ ] URLs wrapped in backticks to prevent Discord embeds
- [ ] Build passes (`npm run build`)
- [ ] All tests pass (`npm test`)

## Implementation Notes

### NDJSON Event Structure
```typescript
interface CliProgressEvent {
  phase: 'downloading' | 'extracting' | 'embedding' | 'completed' | 'failed';
  url: string;
  message?: string;
  title?: string;
  error?: string;
}
```

### Example Implementation Pattern
```typescript
const thread = await message.startThread({
  name: `Processing URL`,
  autoArchiveDuration: 60
});

for await (const event of runAddCommand(url, context)) {
  const progressMsg = formatProgressMessage(event);
  await thread.send(progressMsg);
}

await thread.send(`Final result: ${result.success ? '✅ Success' : '❌ Failed'}`);
```

### Error Handling
- If thread creation fails, fall back to message.reply()
- If thread.send() fails, log error but continue processing
- Always close/archive thread when processing completes

## Related Work

- Epic: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71)
- Depends on: CLI runner integration in message handler
- CLI Flags work: Add CLI Flags for Context Pass-Through (SB-0MN9CKJVH007RR6G)

## Dependencies

Blocked by: CLI runner integration
data.title: Implement NDJSON progress event handling with threaded Discord updates

- Thread messages and final replies now consistently wrap titles and URLs in backticks to avoid Discord embeds.
- Interim progress events fall back to channel replies when thread creation or thread.send() fails so users still receive updates.
- CLI subprocess spawn errors now include errno/code in logs and are propagated as CliRunnerError with enriched stderr content, improving diagnostics for ENOENT/EACCES cases.

I ran the TypeScript build and the test suite; build succeeded and all tests pass locally.

| 6 | Bot must remain operational after CLI errors (no crashes) | met | Try/catch blocks around runAddCommand/runQueueCommand and test run indicate no crashes (e.g., 326-362, 482-498) |
| 7 | URLs should still be wrapped in backticks in error responses | met | src/index.ts:252-262 & 307-318 & 333-350 — safeProgressMsg and final messages use backticked title/URL where applicable |

data.description:
## Summary

Create comprehensive unit and integration tests for the CLI runner integration. Tests should cover the new subprocess-based URL handling, progress event processing, and error scenarios.

## Background

The existing test suite has basic coverage but needs new tests for the CLI runner integration. The cli-runner.ts module and the updated index.ts message handlers require thorough testing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Create `tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts` with tests for:
  - Module exports all required functions
  - CliRunnerError class behavior
  - Type definitions are correct
  - Environment configuration (OB_CLI_PATH)
  - Subprocess spawning with context flags
  - NDJSON parsing and event generation
  - Timeout handling
  - Error handling for missing CLI
  - Graceful shutdown of child processes
- [ ] Update/create `tests/index.test.ts` with tests for:
  - URL extraction from messages
  - Message handler integration with runAddCommand
  - Thread creation for progress updates
  - Error handling when CLI unavailable
  - Backtick wrapping in responses
  - Context flag passing (channel-id, message-id, author-id)
- [ ] Mock CLI subprocess for testing (don't require actual sb binary)
- [ ] All new tests pass (`npm test`)
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] Test coverage report shows >80% coverage for bot/ module

## Implementation Notes

### Mocking Strategy
```typescript
import { spawn } from 'child_process';
vi.mock('child_process', () => ({
  spawn: vi.fn()
}));

// Mock successful CLI execution
const mockSpawn = vi.fn().mockReturnValue({
  stdout: { on: vi.fn(), pipe: vi.fn() },
  stderr: { on: vi.fn() },
  on: vi.fn().mockImplementation((event, cb) => {
    if (event === 'exit') cb(0);
  }),
  kill: vi.fn()
});
```

### Test Cases
1. **CLI Available**: Mock CLI returns success, verify progress events yielded
2. **CLI Not Found**: Mock CLI throws ENOENT, verify graceful error handling
3. **CLI Timeout**: Mock CLI hangs, verify timeout handling
4. **Invalid NDJSON**: Mock CLI returns garbage, verify parser resilience
5. **Thread Creation**: Verify Discord API calls for thread creation
6. **Progress Updates**: Verify correct messages sent for each phase

## Related Work

- Epic: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71)
- Depends on: All other child work items
- Previous work: Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility (SB-0MNAGZOLK007GMAN) - tests exist in dist/tests/

## Dependencies

Blocked by: All implementation work items
data.title: Add comprehensive tests for CLI runner integration

data.description:
Problem statement
---------------

The OpenBrain CLI fails to load its configuration when spawned as a subprocess by the Discord bot; it must reliably load its own configuration (.env and standard locations) independent of the parent's environment.

Users
-----

- Primary: Discord bot developers who spawn the CLI as a subprocess. Example user story: "As a bot developer, I can spawn the CLI without adding CLI-only environment variables to the bot's environment, so the bot and CLI remain decoupled."
- Secondary: Operators running the CLI directly who expect predictable configuration resolution.

Success criteria
----------------

- CLI loads .env from the CLI installation directory before checking per-user config and process.env.
- CLI runs successfully when the parent process does not provide DATABASE_URL in its environment (no manual bot changes required).
- Clear error messages list missing required variables and where the CLI looked for them.
- Unit test(s) validate loading .env from CLI directory.

Acceptance criteria (concrete)
-----------------------------

- Unit test: a unit test that mocks file locations confirms the CLI config loader reads the .env located in the CLI package directory and uses values from it.
- Error formatting: when required variables are missing, the CLI prints a concise list of variables it attempted to find and the paths checked (CLI dir, per-user, env) and exits non-zero.

Constraints
-----------

- Must not require changes to the Discord bot code or its .env file.
- Should work for non-interactive subprocesses (no interactive prompts).
- Respect standard per-user config locations (e.g., ~/.config/openbrain/) and the CLI install directory.
- Keep changes minimal and conservative; avoid broad refactors of existing config validation unless necessary.

Existing state
--------------

- The CLI currently validates configuration (zod) and fails when DATABASE_URL is missing even if the CLI's own .env exists in the install directory.
- The repository already contains config split work (Create CLI config module, Split Config into CLI and Bot Modules) and improvements to CLI error messages and subprocess runner utilities.

Desired change
--------------

- Update the CLI config/initialization code to search for configuration in this order:
  1. .env file in the CLI's own directory (detected via import.meta.url / __dirname)
  2. per-user config (e.g., ~/.config/openbrain/.env or config file)
  3. process.env as a last resort
- Keep existing zod validation; if required variables remain missing after the search, exit with a clear, actionable error listing missing variables and the files/locations checked.
- Add a focused unit test that mocks file paths to assert .env from CLI directory is loaded.

Non-goals
--------

- This work does not extract the CLI into a separate repository or change the public wire protocol between the bot and CLI. Those are out-of-scope and should be tracked as separate work items if desired.

Related work
------------

- docs/feature-requests/cli-configuration-loading.md (this seed) — describes the problem and proposed solution.
- SB-0MN9GD42J00624QD: Create CLI config module — existing work that introduced src/config/cli.ts and validation schema.
- SB-0MN9CJIXA0031C2I: Remove Discord Coupling from CLI Commands — ensured CLI doesn't depend on Discord env vars.
- SB-0MNAGZOLK007GMAN: Create CLI Subprocess Runner Utility — utilities for spawning the CLI from the bot.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
--------------------------------------

- Q: "Should I create a new Worklog work item from the seed file, or reuse one of these existing items? If you select reuse, the intake will be appended to that work item. (Choose one)" — Answer (user): "Create new (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Which configuration sources should the CLI prefer, in order? (Select one or provide custom order)" — Answer (user): "CLI directory .env → ~/.config/openbrain → process.env (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "If DATABASE_URL (or other required config) is missing after searching, which should happen?" — Answer (user): "Fail with clear error (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "What tests do you want added to validate this feature? (pick any)" — Answer (user): "Unit test: dotenv load from CLI directory (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

--

This draft was generated to seed implementation work and review; please review and indicate any clarifications or edits needed.

Risks & assumptions
--------------------

- Risk: Changing config load order may mask misconfigurations in environments where parent process intentionally overrides CLI settings. Mitigation: document precedence clearly and add tests that simulate parent env overrides.
- Risk: Tests that rely on working-directory-based .env may be flaky across CI runners. Mitigation: use explicit path-based mocking in unit tests and CI fixtures that simulate subprocess behavior.
- Assumption: `src/config/cli.ts` exists and exposes a validation entrypoint where the load-order logic can be implemented. Evidence: SB-0MN9GD42J00624QD (Create CLI config module).
- Scope-scope creep risk: additional features (config migration, interactive prompts, repo extraction) are out of scope and should be recorded as separate work items. Mitigation: record and link discovered opportunities as child work items rather than expanding this item.
data.title: CLI: Load configuration when spawned as subprocess

data.description:
# Intake Draft: Add tests for OB_CLI_PATH and setCliPath behavior (SB-0MNFT3M9F006OSGI)

Problem statement

Add unit tests to verify that the CLI runner respects the OB_CLI_PATH environment variable and that the exported setCliPath API correctly overrides the CLI executable path used when spawning child processes.

Users

- Developers running tests and writing local dev tooling who need to point the runner at a custom ob binary.

User stories

- As a developer I can set OB_CLI_PATH to point tests at a mocked ob binary so tests don't invoke the real binary.
- As a developer I can call setCliPath(path) from code to override the executable used by the runner for integration/testing.

Success criteria

- Tests added to tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts covering default behaviour, env override, and API override.
- Mocks (vi.mock) used for child_process.spawn so tests are hermetic and do not require a real ob binary.
- Tests restore process.env and any global state between tests.
- Tests pass locally and on CI (Linux runners) without requiring external binaries.
 - Tests restore process.env and any global state between tests, including resetting setCliPath to its previous value.

Constraints

- Use Vitest and existing test patterns; use vi.mock for mocking child_process.spawn.
- Keep tests Linux-only; rely on current CI matrix (no Windows specific handling).
- Do not change public runtime behaviour—this is a tests-only change.

Existing state

- There is an existing tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts which already contains related tests and a setCliPath describe block.
- The repo uses Vitest (vitest.config.ts, package.json test script).

Desired change

- Extend tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts with new test cases that assert:
  - Default executable used when OB_CLI_PATH unset.
  - When process.env.OB_CLI_PATH is set, spawn is called with that path.
  - When setCliPath(path) is called, spawn is called with that path (overriding env or default as appropriate).
  - Each test cleans up by restoring process.env and setCliPath to avoid test leakage.

Related work

 - docs/feature-requests/cli-configuration-loading.md — notes on CLI configuration loading and earlier discussion of env handling.
 - src/bot/cli-runner.ts — source for the CLI_EXECUTABLE default and exported setCliPath implementation.
 - tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts — existing tests to extend.
 - Parent work item: SB-0MNEIN9T70074ZRR — parent epic for broader CLI runner tests.
 - Related work items discovered by automated search:
   - SB-0MNFTJWE6006WA3G — Audit and replace SB_CLI_PATH references with OB_CLI_PATH (repo env var standardization).
  - SB-0MNAH01JI002FIAZ — Add Integration Tests and Graceful Shutdown (shows patterns for setup/teardown and child process termination).

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Tests may be flaky if global state (process.env or setCliPath) is not fully restored between tests. Mitigation: use beforeEach/afterEach to snapshot and restore env and API state.
- Risk: Existing tests may already assert similar behaviour leading to duplicated assertions. Mitigation: extend existing tests conservatively and avoid duplicating coverage.
- Risk: Environment variable naming mismatch (SB_CLI_PATH vs OB_CLI_PATH) may cause inconsistent behaviour. Mitigation: reference SB-0MNFTJWE6006WA3G and ensure tests use OB_CLI_PATH.
- Assumption: setCliPath is synchronous and affects the module-level variable used by the spawn wrapper; if setCliPath behaves differently, tests may need to import or re-require the module under test.
- Scope-scope creep risk: additional changes (refactoring setCliPath to be more testable) should be recorded as separate work items rather than expanded in this task.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Where should the new tests be placed?" — Answer (user): "Extend tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Which test framework and mocking approach should we use?" — Answer (user): "Use whatever we are currently using". Agent inference: repo uses Vitest; will use vi.mock. Evidence: tests import from "vitest" and package.json references vitest. Final: yes.
- Q: "Any CI or OS constraints to consider for the tests?" — Answer (user): "Linux-only (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Final headline summary

Add tests to ensure cli-runner respects OB_CLI_PATH and that setCliPath correctly overrides the spawned executable path; extend tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts using Vitest and vi.mock.
data.title: Add tests for OB_CLI_PATH and setCliPath behavior

Summary

Add focused unit tests that mock a child-process stdout stream emitting NDJSON lines and assert the runner parses them into the expected event objects and sequences.

Acceptance criteria

- Tests simulate a child_process.spawn return value whose stdout is a Readable that emits lines of NDJSON representing:
  - progress events
  - stats / queue results
  - final command results (AddResult / QueueResult / StatsResult)
- Tests assert that runAddCommand (or the internal NDJSON parser) yields the expected sequence of typed events.
- Include a test that emits invalid NDJSON and asserts the parser handles it gracefully (skips/buffers/errors as appropriate).
- Tests are deterministic and do not rely on a real ob binary.

Implementation notes

- Use vi.fn mocks for spawn and create a Readable stream (from 'stream') to push newline-terminated JSON strings.
- Place tests under tests/bot/cli-runner.ndjson.test.ts or similar.

Create integration tests for the bot entrypoint that validate message-to-runAddCommand flow, URL extraction, thread creation for progress updates, context-flag passing, backtick wrapping of responses and error handling when CLI unavailable.

  - URL extraction from sample Discord message payloads (single URL, multiple URLs, no URL).

  - Errors from the runner (e.g., ENOENT) cause a user-friendly reply and do not throw unhandled exceptions.

Implementation notes

data.description:
Summary:
When a new item is added to the OpenBrain database, the system should generate a human-readable summary of the item and post that summary to the associated Discord thread automatically.

User Stories:
- As a content moderator, when a new item is added to OpenBrain, I want the generated summary posted to the relevant Discord thread so the team can review and discuss it.
- As a developer, I want a robust, idempotent mechanism that posts summaries only once per item and retries on failures.

Expected behaviour and outcomes:
- On successful DB insert of a new item, the system generates a summary (using the existing generator) and posts a message to the Discord thread mapped to that item.
- The message includes the generated summary text, a link to the item in OpenBrain, and metadata (item id, author, timestamp).
- If posting fails, the system retries with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts, then logs an error and marks the item for manual review.

Acceptance criteria (measurable & testable):
1. When a new item is inserted, a message is posted to the configured Discord thread within 10 seconds.
2. Message body equals the generator output plus a link and meta fields.
3. Each item results in at most one message (idempotency; duplicates must not occur on retries).
4. Retry logic in place for transient failures (3 retries with exponential backoff).
5. Secrets (Discord bot token) are not stored in code and are configurable via environment variables or a secret manager.
6. Unit and integration tests exist verifying handler behaviour and posting to Discord (use a test Discord server).
7. Logging for both successes and failures present with sufficient context (item id, error).

Suggested implementation approach:
- Add an event handler that runs after item insert (prefer service-layer hook rather than raw DB triggers to keep logic consolidated).
- Handler flow:
  1. Call the existing summary generator (or use the ob CLI) to produce the summary.
  2. Determine Discord target thread id from item metadata or a mapping configuration. If thread id is missing, fall back to a default channel and create a thread or mark item for review.
  3. Post the message using the existing Discord bot client (discord.js) with a single-line message or embed containing summary+link+meta.
  4. Persist a sent-marker (DB flag or message-log table) to ensure idempotency.
  5. Implement retry with exponential backoff for transient failures and surface permanent failures for manual review.
- Configuration:
  - ENV: DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, DEFAULT_DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID, SEND_SUMMARY_ON_INSERT=true
  - Optional mapping service to map item -> thread id.
- Tests:
  - Unit tests for handler logic.
  - Integration test simulating a DB insert and asserting that the Discord client receives the expected message (use mocked discord client or a staging discord server).
  - E2E test in staging with a real Discord test server.

Dependencies and notes:
- Requires Discord bot token and proper permissions to post messages/create threads.
- Decide whether the handler runs in the API process, as a background job, or via an event queue.
- Ensure observability (metrics, logs) and do not store secrets in repo.

Deliverables:
- Implementation code and tests.
- Documentation for configuration and operation.
- Follow-up tasks if infra changes are needed (secrets, bot permissions).

Tags: discord,openbrain,automation
data.title: Send generated summary to Discord thread on DB insert

data.description:
## Summary
Some websites do not return meaningful content through the current OpenBrain retrieval path, especially when content is rendered by JavaScript. We need a fallback that uses a headless browser to retrieve rendered content when primary retrieval fails.

## Scope clarification
This repository is the Discord bot integration layer. URL ingestion and content retrieval are performed by OpenBrain via `ob add`. Therefore:
- SourceBase implementation scope is limited to bot command/behavior handling and observability.
- Retrieval fallback implementation belongs in OpenBrain.
- For out-of-scope platform capability requests, SourceBase will produce a feature request document for PM handoff.

## User story
As a Discord community operator,
I want links from JavaScript-heavy websites to ingest successfully,
So that shared knowledge is reliably indexed in OpenBrain even when the primary retrieval path fails.

## Expected behavior
- OpenBrain keeps the current retrieval path as the primary fast path.
- When primary retrieval fails or yields unusable content, OpenBrain can fall back to headless Playwright retrieval.
- Content retrieved via Playwright is fed into the existing extraction pipeline.
- Diagnostics clearly indicate when fallback was used.

## Implementation approach for this work item (SourceBase)
1. Produce an OpenBrain feature request document with clear requirements and acceptance criteria.
2. Link that document in this work item for PM handoff.
3. Keep SourceBase code unchanged unless a bot behavior/documentation update is explicitly requested.

## References
- Feature request document: docs/feature-requests/openbrain-playwright-fallback-retrieval.md
- Related historical (deleted) item: SB-0MN4FHM2B0PI69AE

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A feature request document exists in `docs/feature-requests/` describing Playwright fallback requirements for OpenBrain.
- [ ] The work item description includes clear scope boundaries (SourceBase vs OpenBrain).
- [ ] The work item stage is moved to `intake_complete` after clarification.
data.title: Use Playwright to retrieve content if existing retrieval path fails

data.description:
Summary:
Add unit tests to exercise the error and timeout branches of the cli-runner module. The goal is to make behaviour explicit and covered so future changes to the runner (process spawn, timeout handling, and error propagation) are protected by tests.

Context:
The cli-runner is responsible for executing the OpenBrain CLI (ob) as a child process. There are code paths that handle when the child process emits an error (spawn failure), and when the execution times out. Currently these branches lack focused unit tests.

What to do:
- Add unit tests that simulate/trigger a spawn error and verify the runner surfaces the error to callers with a clear message and stack.
- Add unit tests that simulate a command that exceeds the configured timeout and verify the runner kills the process and returns a timeout error/indicator.
- Use mocking/stubbing of child_process.spawn (or the internal process wrapper) to avoid spawning real binaries in unit tests. Keep tests fast and deterministic.

Acceptance criteria:
1) New unit tests are added under the unit test suite (e.g. tests/unit or test/units) and included in existing test runs.
2) There is at least one test verifying the runner's handling of a spawn error, asserting the error type/message and that no orphaned processes remain.
3) There is at least one test verifying timeout behaviour: when a child exceeds the configured timeout the runner terminates it and returns a timeout-specific error or code path.
4) Tests are deterministic, do not rely on the real ob binary, and run quickly (<~200ms each ideally).
5) All existing tests still pass when running the full test suite.
6) The work item is a child of SB-0MNFT3MI1005HXOD and marked task/high.

Suggested files to add or modify (implementation detail):
- tests/unit/cli-runner.spec.ts (or .js depending on project)
- A small helper in test utilities to stub child_process.spawn if one doesn't already exist.

Notes:
- Keep changes minimal and focused to avoid cross-cutting test infra changes.
data.title: Add unit tests for cli-runner error & timeout branches

data.description:
Summary:
Add integration tests that exercise how our code handles CLI outputs that are edge cases: NDJSON streamed to stdout, messages emitted to stderr, and commands that exit with non-zero exit codes. These tests ensure the higher-level CLI handling/parsing code correctly processes and surfaces these cases to callers.

Context:
Some interactions with the ob CLI produce NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) streams, sometimes the CLI emits useful diagnostics on stderr, and some commands legitimately exit with a non-zero status to indicate specific conditions. Consumers must be robust to these behaviours.

What to do:
- Add integration tests that run the CLI pipeline (using either a test double binary or the real ob binary in a controlled way) and assert the behaviour when:
  * The process writes NDJSON lines to stdout (verify the parser consumes each JSON object and emits expected events/results).
  * The process writes messages to stderr while also producing valid stdout (verify stderr is captured/logged and does not break stdout parsing unless a real error occurs).
  * The process exits with a non-zero exit code and ensure the integration layer surfaces the exit code and any stderr output to the caller.
- Prefer using small test shim binaries or node child processes that mimic ob's behaviour so tests are hermetic.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Integration tests are added under integration tests (e.g. tests/integration or test/integration) and are executed by the CI integration test target.
2) There is a test validating NDJSON streaming parsing correctness: multiple JSON objects are parsed and delivered in order.
3) There is a test validating stderr passthrough/capture: stderr lines are accessible to the caller and do not corrupt stdout parsing.
4) There is a test validating non-zero exit handling: the integration layer surfaces the exit code and provides the stderr/stdout context.
5) Tests are hermetic and document any required environment/setup; if they require the ob binary, document how the test provides a shim or sets OB_CLI_PATH.
6) The work item is a child of SB-0MNFT3MI1005HXOD and marked task/high.

Suggested files to add or modify (implementation detail):
- tests/integration/cli-edgecases.spec.ts
- test-shims/ob-ndjson-shim.js (a small Node script that writes NDJSON, optional stderr, configurable exit)
- Integration test runner updates or npm script if required.

Notes:
- If the project already has an integration test runner, add the new tests to the existing setup to avoid duplicating infra.
- Keep shims minimal and documented.
data.title: Add integration tests for CLI edge-case outputs (NDJSON/stderr/non-zero exit)

data.description:
Summary:
Add a small set of test utilities to make it straightforward to set OB_CLI_PATH to a test shim binary and ensure the environment is fully restored between tests. This reduces flakiness from environment leakage and simplifies writing integration tests that require a specific ob binary path.

Context:
Several tests need to run against a fake or shimbed ob CLI; setting OB_CLI_PATH in the environment is the recommended approach. However, tests currently sometimes mutate process.env without reliably restoring it, causing cross-test interference.

What to do:
- Implement a test utility (module) that provides an API to temporarily set OB_CLI_PATH for the duration of a test and then restore the original environment.
- Provide helpers for common patterns: withObCliPath(path, asyncFn) and setObCliPath(path) + restore functions for frameworks that prefer setup/teardown.
- Add documentation and examples showing how to use the utilities in unit and integration tests.
- Use the utility in at least one existing or new test to demonstrate correct behaviour.

Acceptance criteria:
1) A new test utility module is added (e.g. test/utils/obCliEnv.ts or test/helpers/obCliEnv.js) that exposes at minimum: withObCliPath(path, fn) and restoreObCliPath() or similar names appropriate for the codebase language.
2) The utility correctly saves and restores the previous OB_CLI_PATH and any other env keys it touches; it works with both synchronous and asynchronous test bodies.
3) At least one test is updated to use the utility, demonstrating that environment is restored after the test completes (including when the test throws/rejects).
4) The utilities are covered by a small unit test proving restore behaviour on success and on error.
5) The repository's test suite remains green after adding the utilities and demo usage.
6) The work item is a child of SB-0MNFT3MI1005HXOD and marked task/high.

Suggested files to add or modify (implementation detail):
- test/utils/obCliEnv.ts
- tests/unit/obCliEnv.spec.ts (tests for restore behaviour)
- Example usage in tests/integration/cli-edgecases.spec.ts

Notes:
- Keep the API small and ergonomic. Prefer explicit restore semantics or a single withObCliPath helper that guarantees restoration.

data.description:
# Intake Brief: Add optional `k` parameter to `/briefing` slash command

**Headline summary:** Extend the existing Discord `/briefing` command to accept an optional integer `k` option and pass it through to OpenBrain CLI as `--k` when generating a briefing. Keep current `/briefing` behavior intact while adding configurable top-k retrieval.

## Problem statement

The bot already supports `/briefing` with a required `query` option, but users cannot control how many results OpenBrain includes in the generated briefing. This prevents users from tuning briefing depth and alignment with `ob briefing run --k` capabilities.

## Users

- Discord users who run `/briefing` and want concise or broader briefings.
- Bot maintainers who need slash-command options to map cleanly to OpenBrain CLI flags.

Example user stories:

- As a Discord user, I can run `/briefing query:<topic> k:3` to generate a shorter, focused briefing.
- As a Discord user, I can omit `k` and still get the default briefing behavior.
- As a maintainer, I can trace slash options directly to CLI arguments in one place.

## Success criteria

- `/briefing` remains available and continues to require `query`.
- `/briefing` exposes an optional integer option named `k`.
- When `k` is provided, the bot includes `--k <value>` in the briefing CLI invocation.
- When `k` is omitted, the bot uses default behavior equivalent to `k=5`.
- `k` values are validated to CLI-compatible bounds (1-50), and invalid values are rejected by Discord option constraints or bot-side guards.

## Constraints

- Scope is limited to Discord bot command behavior and argument mapping; no OpenBrain CLI feature changes are included.
- The implementation must continue using OpenBrain CLI invocation patterns already used by the bot.
- Existing `/briefing` response formatting and error handling behavior should be preserved.
- Work should avoid unrelated command refactors.

## Existing state

- `/briefing` is already registered in `src/discord/client.ts` with one required option: `query`.
- `/briefing` handling exists in `src/index.ts` and currently runs CLI with args equivalent to:
  - `ob briefing run --json --query <query>`
- The flow parses JSON-first output and falls back to raw text formatting.
- Tests cover non-JSON briefing output handling in `tests/discord/interaction.test.ts`.

## Desired change

- Extend command registration to add optional integer option `k` for `/briefing`.
- In `/briefing` interaction handling, read `k` from command options.
- Include `--k <k>` in CLI args when present (or use default behavior equivalent to 5).
- Preserve current command reply lifecycle (`deferReply`, CLI availability checks, output parsing, and attachment behavior).
- Add/update tests to verify argument pass-through and behavior with/without `k`.

## Related work

- `/briefing` command registration and execution:
  - `src/discord/client.ts`
  - `src/index.ts`
  - `tests/discord/interaction.test.ts`
- CLI subprocess utilities used by command handlers:
  - `src/bot/cli-runner.ts`
- Related work items:
  - Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71)
    - Prior integration pattern for mapping Discord actions to OpenBrain CLI subprocess calls.
  - /search slash command with tiered relevance retrieval (SB-0MN83LDJR000RTAT)
    - Example of slash command option design and tests in this bot.
  - Create TypeScript source for CLI runner in src/bot/ (SB-0MNEIIIYO0017U63)
    - Established CLI runner interfaces used by slash command handling.

## Related work (automated report)

- `src/index.ts`: Existing `/briefing` route already invokes `runCliCommand("briefing", ...)`, so this is the primary implementation point for adding `--k` pass-through without changing broader command routing.
- `src/discord/client.ts`: Slash command registration currently defines only `query`; adding `k` here is required for Discord to expose the option and enforce integer bounds.
- `tests/discord/interaction.test.ts`: Existing `/briefing` tests validate output parsing behavior and are the most direct place to add assertions for `k` argument forwarding.
- Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71): Demonstrates approved pattern for invoking OpenBrain CLI from Discord interactions and handling user-facing errors.
- /search slash command with tiered relevance retrieval (SB-0MN83LDJR000RTAT): Shows precedent for optional slash command parameters and test coverage strategy.
- Create TypeScript source for CLI runner in src/bot/ (SB-0MNEIIIYO0017U63): Confirms stable CLI subprocess abstraction and argument handling support used by slash command implementations.

## Risks and assumptions

- Risk: scope creep into broader `/briefing` output refactors while adding `k`.
  - Mitigation: record any additional enhancements as separate linked work items instead of expanding this item.
- Risk: option-name drift (`k` vs `limit`) between Discord and CLI flags.
  - Mitigation: keep Discord option name `k` and map directly to CLI `--k`.
- Assumption: `ob briefing run --k` contract remains `1-50` with default `5`.
- Assumption: existing reply and attachment behavior is acceptable and should not change.

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "I found `/briefing` already implemented (with `query` only). For this work item, what should we do?" — Answer (user): "Extend existing /briefing (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes. Evidence: `src/index.ts`, `src/discord/client.ts`.
- Q: "How should `k` behave?" — Answer (user): "Optional, default 5 (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes. Evidence: `ob briefing --help` documents `--k <N>` default `5` and range `1-50`.
- Q: "For the slash option itself, what should it be called?" — Answer (user): "Use `k` (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes. Evidence: interactive reply.
data.title: Add /briefing slash command support for --k

Idempotency for repeated clicks on the same bot reply is implemented. Automated tests for the button rendering and ingestion flow were not found, so the work item cannot be closed yet.

data.description:
Problem statement
-----------------

When a briefing is generated using the `/briefing` slash command the bot currently returns the content (or attaches it) but provides no convenient way for users to ingest that briefing into the OpenBrain database. We need an inline "Save briefing" button on the `/briefing` response that, when clicked, will feed the generated briefing content into the same ingestion path used by `ob add` so the briefing becomes stored, indexed and searchable.

Users
-----

- Discord end users who run `/briefing` and want to save the generated briefing to OpenBrain for future retrieval and search.
- Community curators who want to collect and store well-formed briefings created in-chat.

Example user stories:
- As a Discord user, I can click "Save briefing" on a briefing reply and have the briefing saved into OpenBrain so I can later search it.
- As a curator, I can save an attached briefing file into the ingestion pipeline without leaving Discord.

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------

- A button labeled "Save briefing" appears inline on `/briefing` responses (either on the message body or the attached file message).
- Clicking the button ingests the briefing content into OpenBrain using the same ingestion semantics as `ob add` (i.e., content is processed, summarized/embedded and stored).
- The button action posts a confirmation message with a link to the created OpenBrain item or a helpful failure message. The confirmation must include a retrieval URL using the openbrain://sorra/<id> scheme when an item id is available.
- The implementation is idempotent for duplicate clicks (either deduplicate ingestion or present a clear message that it was already saved).
- Tests cover the button rendering and the ingestion flow, including success and failure paths.

Constraints
-----------

- The bot must not attempt to ingest content by fetching an external URL; instead it must use the briefing text already present in the message (or attached .md file) and pass it into the ingestion flow. If the CLI requires a file, the bot may write a temporary file and pass that path to the CLI just as `ob add` would when given a file path.
- Keep changes limited to the Discord bot; do not change OpenBrain CLI behavior. Reuse `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` helpers where appropriate.
- Maintain existing `/briefing` response formatting and permission checks.
- Respect Discord rate limits and permission boundaries (e.g., bot must have permission to post replies/perform interactions and attach files).

Existing state
--------------

- `/briefing` command exists and is implemented in `src/index.ts` and registered in `src/discord/client.ts`. It returns formatted briefing content and may attach a .md file for long outputs.
- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` provides process spawning routines and `runCliCommand` helper that can be used to call the OpenBrain CLI. It supports passing additional args and context tags.
- There is precedent for `ob add` invocation from the bot (see existing work item SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71). README documents examples and tag usage.

Desired change
--------------

- Add a new interactive button to the `/briefing` reply: "Save briefing".
- When clicked, the handler should extract the briefing text from the message content or attachment. If necessary, write the content to a temporary file and call the CLI ingestion path consistent with `ob add` (use `runCliCommand`/`runAddCommand` as appropriate). Include Discord context tags when invoking the CLI for traceability.
- Provide a reply acknowledging success with a link to the created OpenBrain item (when the CLI returns an item id) or a helpful error message on failure.
- Ensure duplicate clicks are handled gracefully: if the briefing has already been ingested, inform the user and avoid re-ingesting duplicate content.
- Add unit and integration tests that mock the CLI subprocess to validate behavior and error handling.

Related work
------------

- src/index.ts — `/briefing` handler: current generation and attachment logic.
- src/discord/client.ts — slash command registration for `/briefing`.
- src/bot/cli-runner.ts — CLI invocation helpers (runCliCommand, runAddCommand, tagging).
- SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71 — Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (shows pattern for invoking CLI from interactions).

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
---------------------------------------

- Q: "Which of these best matches what you want this intake to create?" — Answer (user): "Add save button (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "How should the 'save briefing' button behave when clicked?" — Answer (user): "ob add url first retrieves the content and then adds it to the database (generating a summary and embeddings too). In this case the content has been generated and is in the message (or an attached file). we need to send the content into the ingestion flow rather than retrieve it from a URL for ingestion." — Answer (user). Final: yes.
- Q: "Where should the button appear in the Discord response?" — Answer (user): "Inline reply (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
data.title: Add 'Save briefing' button to /briefing responses to ingest briefing content into OpenBrain

data.description:
Problem statement
-----------------
Headline: Allow Discord users to add raw text to OpenBrain via `ob add` by forwarding message text as a temporary file to the CLI.

Problem statement
-----------------
Allow users to add raw text blocks (not just URLs) from Discord into OpenBrain by forwarding message text to the OpenBrain CLI `ob add <string>` flow. The bot should accept message triggers like `ob add <text>` or a raw message selected by the bot, write the content to a temporary file, and invoke the CLI to ingest that content so it becomes an OpenBrain item.

Users
-----
- Community members who want to add notes, meeting minutes, or copied text into OpenBrain without hosting the content at a URL.
- Moderators or automation workflows that need to persist text snippets into OpenBrain for later search and summarization.

Example user stories
-------------------
- As a user, I can paste a block of text into Discord and type `ob add <text>` (or use the bot trigger) and have that text ingested into OpenBrain as an item.
- As a moderator, I can forward an existing message or select text and have the bot ingest it into OpenBrain using the same `ob add` flow used for URLs.

Success criteria
----------------
- For a single pasted text block, the bot writes the content to a temporary file and calls `ob add file://<path>`; the CLI returns success and the bot posts a confirmation message including the new OpenBrain item ID.
- The content ingestion supports multiline text of at least 64KB without truncation.
- The bot handles CLI unavailability gracefully, replying with a single clear error message when the CLI is missing or returns a non-zero exit code.
- All related documentation and README examples are updated to show `ob add` text ingestion and the bot usage.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

If any of the above are undecidable (e.g., maximum text size), flag as TBD in the Appendix and ask the product owner.

Constraints
-----------
- Do not change OpenBrain CLI behavior; call `ob add` as a subprocess with a temporary file URL when ingesting raw text.
- Avoid passing raw text as a single CLI arg to prevent shell-escaping issues; prefer temp file approach.
- Preserve bot boundaries: keep CLI-specific config out of the bot process (ensure subprocesses can load their own config or have environment defaults documented).

Existing state
--------------
- The OpenBrain CLI `ob add` exists and supports `--format ndjson` and context flags (channel-id, message-id, author-id). The bot already integrates the CLI for URL ingestion via the cli-runner utility.
- There is an existing epic to integrate `ob add` into the bot (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71) and multiple completed tasks that implement the CLI runner and NDJSON progress handling.
- Current bot code processes URLs and maps NDJSON events to threaded progress updates; it does not yet forward raw text blocks to `ob add` as files.

-Desired change
---------------
- Add a focused bot handler that recognizes message triggers `ob add <text>` and forwarded/selected message text. The handler should:
  - Write the provided text to a secure temporary file (using safe filename and appropriate permissions).
  - Invoke the CLI as subprocess: `ob add --format ndjson file://<tmpfile>` with Discord context flags.
  - Consume NDJSON progress (if present) and map to the existing threaded progress updates.
  - Post a final confirmation with OpenBrain item ID or a clear error message.

Related work
------------
- Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71) — existing epic integrating CLI into the bot.
- Create TypeScript source for CLI runner in src/bot/ (SB-0MNEIIIYO0017U63) — provides runAddCommand and context flag support.
- Implement NDJSON progress event handling with threaded Discord updates (SB-0MNEIMTXM0060IW8) — maps events to threaded replies.
- CLI configuration loading as subprocess (docs/feature-requests/cli-configuration-loading.md) — important context for ensuring CLI subprocess can load its own config.

Potentially related docs:
- docs/feature-requests/cli-configuration-loading.md — describes subprocess config loading expectations and suggested fixes.
- README.md (CLI Commands section) — documents current `sb add` usage and examples to update.

Potentially related work items:
- SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command (epic)
- SB-0MNEIIIYO0017U63: Create TypeScript source for CLI runner in src/bot/ (task)
- SB-0MNEIMTXM0060IW8: Implement NDJSON progress event handling with threaded Discord updates (feature)

Related work (automated report)
--------------------------------
Work items
- SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71 — Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command
  Direct predecessor: describes wiring the CLI add command into the bot's add command (exact integration work this item implements is the same pathway you'd use for text-block ingestion via `ob add <string>`).

- SB-0MNEIIU2R001QVTH — Integrate CLI runner into Discord bot message handler
  Shows how the CLI runner is invoked from the message handler and how context/tags are passed; important for adding support for `ob add <string>` so the message handler can call the runner with text blocks instead of URLs.

- SB-0MNEIIIYO0017U63 — Create TypeScript source for CLI runner in src/bot/
  Contains the TypeScript implementation the bot uses to spawn and parse `ob` subprocesses; foundational code to extend for text-block ingestion.

- SB-0MNFT3MDO000K9QV — Add NDJSON stdout parsing unit tests for runAddCommand
  Adds tests that validate NDJSON progress parsing from the `add` command; relevant because text-block ingestion via `ob add <string>` will also surface NDJSON progress events that must be parsed correctly.

- SB-0MNEIMV2B003CZ24 — Add comprehensive tests for CLI runner integration
  Higher-level integration tests around the CLI runner used by the bot—these are useful reference points and tests to extend when adding `ob add <string>` support.

Source files & test helpers
- src/bot/cli-runner.ts — core runner to spawn `ob add` and parse NDJSON; primary integration point for text-block ingestion.
- src/index.ts — message handler and processUrlWithProgress flow; extend here to detect `ob add <text>` messages and call the runner with a temp-file URL.
- tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts, tests/index.test.ts — unit and integration tests to extend.

Notes / suggested next steps
1. Extend src/bot/cli-runner.ts to accept a text payload mode and ensure spawn args / file:// handling are correct.
2. Add unit tests using existing test helpers to mock `ob` progress for text-block invocations.
3. Update message handler tests to include text-block ingestion flows.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
--------------------------------------
- Q: "Is SB-0MNM9ZYAZ000R2IZ intended to be a new, separate task from the existing epic 'Integrate \"ob add\" CLI into Discord bot add command' (SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71), or should it be a child/duplicate of that work item?" — Answer (user): "Separate task (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "When a Discord message contains multiple URLs or a block of text, how should the new `ob add <string>` behavior work?" — Answer (user): "The ob add command does this work, the bot just passes the value from the user." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Confirm the desired priority and at least one measurable acceptance target." — Answer (user): "I can add a text block and it gets added to openbrain. Priority high". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "How should users invoke the `ob add <string>` behavior from Discord?" — Answer (user): "Message trigger (bot forwards message text)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "When forwarding a raw text block from Discord to `ob add`, how should the bot pass the content to the CLI?" — Answer (user): "Temp file (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: Temporary file handling may leak sensitive content if not cleaned up. Mitigation: ensure files are created with restrictive permissions and deleted immediately after ingestion; add a follow-up task to audit temp-file lifecycle if needed.
- Risk: Very large text blobs may impact memory or exceed CLI limits. Mitigation: enforce a reasonable maximum (e.g., 64KB) and return an error message suggesting an alternative (upload to URL or break into smaller pieces). Mark exact size limit as TBD if PO wants a different threshold.
- Risk: Abuse/spam — users may paste large or malicious payloads. Mitigation: rate-limit `ob add` message triggers and respect channel permissions; record metrics for abuse detection.
- Assumption: The CLI supports adding file:// URLs and will read the provided temporary file when invoked as `ob add file://<path>`.
- Scope-scope creep mitigation: Record additional feature requests (e.g., UI for selecting text, richer ingestion options, per-channel policy controls) as separate work items rather than expanding this task.
data.risk: High (temp-file leakage); mitigations: restrictive perms, delete files, rate-limit
data.title: Add support for ob add <string>
