data.description:
# Intake Draft: Add ob recent CLI command (SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS)

Headline: Add `ob recent` command (default 5) — list recently modified items with summaries.

# Intake Draft: Add ob recent CLI command (SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS)

Problem statement
-----------------
Create a new `ob recent` command that lists the x most recently modified OpenBrain records and shows a short summary for each. The command defaults to `x=5` when not supplied.

Users
-----
- Power users and support engineers who want a quick view of recent items.
- Developers and automation scripts that need to inspect recently updated content.

Example user stories
-------------------
- As a user, I can run `ob recent` and see the 5 most recently modified items with their titles, timestamps, and a one-line summary.
- As a power user, I can run `ob recent 10` to see the 10 most recently modified items.

Success criteria
----------------
- All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.
- `ob recent` displays the most recently modified items (default 5) in descending order by modification time.
- The command accepts an optional numeric argument `x` and validates it (positive integer, upper bound 100).
- Each item output includes: item id, title, modified timestamp, and a one-line summary (or placeholder when summary unavailable).
- The command exits with code 0 on success and non-zero on failure; errors are reported to stderr with a human-friendly message.
- Unit and integration tests covering normal, edge, and error cases are added.

Constraints
-----------
- Must use the existing `ob` CLI and APIs; do not duplicate storage logic.
- Respect CLI output style and formatting conventions used elsewhere in the repo.
- Prefer calling `ob summary <id>` or a lightweight summary endpoint rather than fetching full item content when available.

Existing state
--------------
- The repo contains `ob` CLI integration code and existing commands such as `ob summary` and `ob add` in `src/index.ts` and `src/bot/cli-runner.ts`.
- Tests in `tests` and `dist/tests` show existing patterns for mocking the `ob` CLI.

Desired change
--------------
- Implement a new subcommand `ob recent [x]` in the CLI command registry, wire it to a handler that queries the data store for the most recent items, and prints formatted results. Add tests and documentation.

Related work
------------
- SB-0MN4QHBA9140EO5I: sb stats Command - Database Statistics (completed) — displays database stats including recent activity.
- SB-0MNM9ZYAZ000R2IZ: Add support for ob add <string> — related bot/CLI integration for `ob add` handling of inline text (various descriptions in repo).
- src/index.ts: existing message handlers and `ob add` handling.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
----------------------------------------
- Q: "What should be the maximum allowed value for x?" — Answer (agent inference): "100". Source: chosen upper bound to limit output size and resource usage. Final: yes.
- Q: "Should summaries be fetched via `ob summary` or constructed locally?" — Answer (agent inference): "Prefer `ob summary` when available to avoid duplicating summarization logic." Source: repository contains `ob summary` usage. Final: yes.


Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: Scope creep if summaries require heavy computation or downstream API changes. Mitigation: record follow-up work items for any additional features.
- Assumption: The data store supports efficient sort-by-modified-time queries.

data.description:
# Intake Draft: Wire up ob recent as slash command (SB-0MNP2ITRZ003GCCC)

Headline: Add a Discord slash command `/recent` that invokes the existing `ob recent` CLI and presents recent OpenBrain items in a concise, human-readable Discord reply.

One-line summary: Register `/recent` and implement a handler that calls `ob recent`, formats results (default 5), supports an optional `limit` (1–100), and attaches long output as markdown files.

Problem statement
-----------------
Users currently run `ob recent` from the CLI to list recently modified OpenBrain items. The Discord bot lacks a corresponding `/recent` slash command. Implement `/recent` so Discord users can request the same recent-item view without leaving Discord. The command should call the existing `ob recent` CLI, format output for Discord, and accept an optional `limit` parameter (default 5).

Users
-----
- Community members and support engineers who use Discord and want a quick view of recently modified OpenBrain items.
- Automation scripts or integrations that may trigger the bot to display recent activity.

Example user stories
-------------------
- As a Discord user, I can run `/recent` and see the 5 most recently modified items with id, title, modified timestamp, and a one-line summary.
- As a power user, I can run `/recent limit:10` to see the 10 most recently modified items.
- If the CLI or the requested limit is unavailable or invalid, the command returns a clear, human-readable error.

Success criteria
----------------
- `/recent` returns items in descending order by modification time with default limit 5.
- Command accepts optional integer `limit` (1–100). If user-provided value exceeds CLI constraints, show a helpful error.
- Each item entry shows: item id, title, modified timestamp, and a one-line summary. If full summary is long, message shows a short table-of-contents and attaches the full output as a markdown file.
- Replies match existing bot behavior (public by default) and keep ephemeral responses for sensitive cases.
- Unit and integration tests exercising normal, edge, and failure modes are added and pass locally.
- All related documentation is updated: README, bot README, code comments.
- Full project test suite passes with these changes.

Constraints
-----------
- Use the existing `ob` CLI; do not reimplement storage or summarization logic in the bot. The bot should invoke `ob recent` and, when appropriate, `ob summary` for per-item summaries.
- Register slash command per-guild at startup (same approach used by existing commands).
- Follow existing Discord message length limits and split/attach long content as files where necessary.

Existing state
--------------
- The repo already registers slash commands in `src/discord/client.ts` (stats, search, briefing). `StatsCommandHandler` shows how CLI results are integrated.
- `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` provides `runCliCommand`, `runStatsCommand`, and `runSummaryCommand` helpers and supports tagging for Discord context; `ob` CLI invocation is centralized there.
- There is an intake draft for an `ob recent` CLI command in the worklog (SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS) marked deleted; current work focuses on wiring the existing CLI invocation into Discord, not re-creating the CLI command.

Desired change
--------------
- Add a SlashCommandHandler for `recent` and register it in `registerSlashCommands` alongside existing commands.
- Handler behavior:
  - Parse optional `limit` integer argument (default 5). Validate 1 <= limit <= 100; if the CLI enforces a different upper bound, surface a helpful error.
  - Call `runCliCommand("recent", ["--json", `--limit`, String(limit)])` or appropriate flags supported by the CLI. Prefer `--json` or machine-readable output for parsing.
  - For each returned item, format a single-line summary (id, title, modified), and for longer summaries attach a .md file with full content.
  - Use ephemeral or public replies consistent with other commands (by default public replies are used for stats/search/briefing).
  - Add unit tests mirroring patterns in `tests/index.ob-add.test.ts` and `tests/bot/cli-runner.*` for mocking the CLI.

Related work
------------
- SB-0MNP2ITRZ003GCCC: Wire up ob recent as slash command (/recent) — this work item (created in wl).
- SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS: Add ob recent CLI command (default 5) — existing intake draft (deleted) that contains CLI-focused design notes; relevant for CLI flags and expected output shape.
- src/bot/cli-runner.ts — existing runner utilities: runCliCommand, runSummaryCommand, etc.
- src/discord/client.ts — where slash commands are registered; register `/recent` here.
- src/handlers/StatsCommandHandler.ts — example of a SlashCommandHandler implementation that integrates with cli-runner.
Traceability:
- Implementation should touch: `src/discord/client.ts` (register command), `src/handlers/` (new RecentCommandHandler), and `src/bot/cli-runner.ts` (invoke runner functions).

Related work (automated report)
--------------------------------
1. SB-0MN4QHBA9140EO5I — sb stats Command - Database Statistics (completed)
   - Why related: `sb stats` queries the data store and reports recent/time-based activity. Reuse formatting and CLI-parsing patterns from `StatsCommandHandler`.

2. SB-0MNP0BJ9K007BSRD — Repository cleanup: prune merged branches and sync with main (completed)
   - Why related: Contains operational and history notes about prior CLI and command work; helpful for traceability.

3. SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS — Add ob recent CLI command (default 5) (deleted draft)
   - Why related: Prior intake that describes expected CLI semantics for `ob recent`. If the CLI lacks accepted flags (e.g. `--json`/`--limit`), use this document as the basis for a follow-up CLI change.

4. src/bot/cli-runner.ts
   - Why related: Central runner used by existing slash commands; the new handler should invoke runner helpers rather than spawning ad-hoc processes.

Recommendation: Implement the slash-command handler to call `runCliCommand`/`runSummaryCommand` in `cli-runner.ts`. If the CLI lacks necessary flags or machine-readable output, create a follow-up work item referencing SB-0MNON3ZN4000R0GS to update the CLI.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
----------------------------------------
- Q: "Should we:" — Answer (user): "Register a Discord slash command (/recent) that calls existing `ob recent` CLI, leaving the CLI unchanged (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "For the slash command output, which fields should be shown for each recent item?" — Answer (user): "Output the human readable content as a discord message. If it is too long split it after the table of contents section and attach the full output as a markdown file." Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "Pick one: Permissions and guild scope" — Answer (user): "Register per-guild during bot start (current behavior) (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "What maximum value should the optional `limit` parameter accept for /recent?" — Answer (user): "This is set in the CLI, discord bot can accept any value, if it is over the limit then it should show an error". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Risk: CLI does not support a machine-readable JSON output or an expected `--limit` flag. Mitigation: Add verification tests and fallbacks: attempt `--json`, fallback to parsing plain text; if infeasible, create a follow-up item to add CLI flags.
- Risk: Long output may exceed Discord limits. Mitigation: Attach full content as .md files and keep in-message summaries compact.
- Assumption: `ob recent` exists or is available in the environment used to run the bot. If not, this work will still register the slash command but the handler will return a helpful CLI-unavailable message.
 - Risk: Scope creep if the command is expanded to provide interactive pagination, filters, or heavy summarization. Mitigation: Record additional features as separate follow-up work items and keep this item's scope limited to listing and short summaries.
 - Risk: Discord rate limits or bot permission errors when registering commands. Mitigation: Register per-guild like existing commands and surface clear error messages to operators; add tests for permission error handling.

Open Questions
--------------
- If the CLI's `recent` subcommand uses different flags than `--limit` or `--json`, should the handler translate or adapt? (Pending: agent to inspect CLI docs or user to confirm.)
data.title: Wire up ob recent as slash command (/recent)

Ready to close: No

## Summary

The ob add handler now fetches text attachments, writes them to a temporary file, and invokes the OpenBrain CLI; unit tests cover .md success, oversized attachments, and inline attachment cases. Content-type/filename validation exists to reject non-text files but there is no explicit unit test for that negative path; README does not document attachment ingestion. Not ready to close until README is updated and a unit test is added for non-text rejection.

## Acceptance Criteria Status

| # | Criterion | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `ob add` ingests text attachments (creates OpenBrain item and posts the item link/ID) for referenced messages. | met | src/index.ts:2023 — writes temp file and calls processUrlWithProgress(file://...) to run the CLI and post item link; tests/index.ob-add.test.ts:196 — unit test for .md attachment success. |
| 2 | Handles inline attachments on the same message as the `ob add` trigger. | met | src/index.ts:1922 — uses refMsg?.attachments || message.attachments to support inline attachments; tests/index.ob-add.test.ts:276 — inline attachment test. |
| 3 | Respects existing size limit (64KB by default) and returns a clear error message when exceeded. | met | src/index.ts:1946 — checks Content-Length / MAX_ADD_BYTES and replies with 'Attached file is too large to ingest directly'; tests/index.ob-add.test.ts:272 — unit test asserts oversized attachment rejected. |
| 4 | Validation prevents binary or non-text attachments from being ingested. | partial | src/index.ts:1941 — checks filename/content-type and replies when not text; no unit test found asserting this negative path. |
| 5 | Unit/integration tests added: positive case (`.md`), large attachment negative case, inline attachment case. | met | tests/index.ob-add.test.ts:196/245/276 — contains tests for .md success, oversized rejection, and inline attachment case. |
| 6 | Documentation and README updated to reflect supported attachments. | unmet | README.md:23 — ob add docs do not mention ingestion from uploaded file attachments (.md/.txt). |

## Children Status

No children.

data.description:
Problem statement
-----------------
The Discord bot's `ob add` flow ignores text uploaded as message attachments (e.g., `.md`, `.txt`). When a user replies to an attachment with `ob add`, the handler does not fetch the attachment contents and ingestion fails.

Users
-----
- Community members who paste text as files (meeting notes, drafts) and expect `ob add` to ingest them.
- Moderators automating content capture from uploaded notes.

Example user stories
-------------------
- As a user, I can reply to a message that contains a `.md` attachment with `ob add` and have the file's text ingested into OpenBrain.
- As a moderator, I can send `ob add` referencing a message with a `.txt` log and have it ingested without manually copying/pasting.

Success criteria
----------------
- `ob add` ingests text attachments (creates OpenBrain item and posts the item link/ID) for referenced messages.
- Handles inline attachments on the same message as the `ob add` trigger.
- Respects existing size limit (64KB by default) and returns a clear error message when exceeded.
- Validation prevents binary or non-text attachments from being ingested.
- Unit/integration tests added: positive case (`.md`), large attachment negative case, inline attachment case.
- Documentation and README updated to reflect supported attachments.

Constraints
-----------
- Reuse existing fetch + temporary file + `ob add file://...` flow in `src/index.ts`.
- Respect existing MAX_ADD_BYTES limit and current CLI invocation approach.
- Avoid introducing new external dependencies.

Existing state
--------------
- `src/index.ts` contains `ob add` handler and already handles inline `message.content` and referenced message content.
- Save briefing flow in `src/index.ts` demonstrates attachment fetching and ingestion logic to reuse.
- Tests exist (`tests/index.ob-add.test.ts`, `tests/save-button.test.ts`) and include helpers for mocking fetch/attachments.

Desired change
--------------
- Update `ob add` handler in `src/index.ts` to check `message.attachments` and `referenced.message.attachments` for text-like files when no inline payload is present.
- Fetch the attachment body, validate size and content-type/extension, write a temporary file, and call the CLI with `ob add file://<tmp>`.
- Add tests and update docs.

Related work
------------
- Potentially related work items: "Add support for ob add <string>" (SB-0MNM9ZYAZ000R2IZ) — related but distinct; check for overlap.
- Tests: `tests/index.ob-add.test.ts`, `tests/save-button.test.ts` (attachment fetch mocks).

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
---------------------------------------
- Q: "Which file types should be considered 'text-like' by default?" — Answer (agent inference): "Use extensions .md, .txt and Content-Type starting with text/; treat others as non-text. Source: existing Save briefing handler in src/index.ts uses .md and text fetching." Final: yes.
- Q: "Is the current MAX_ADD_BYTES value acceptable (64KB) or should it be changed?" — Answer (user/stakeholder): TBD (OPEN QUESTION). Source: existing code references MAX_ADD_BYTES. Final: OPEN QUESTION.
- Q: "Should attachments be accepted from unverified or external URLs?" — Answer (agent inference): "Fetch as the bot does elsewhere; ensure network fetch follows same rules and error handling as Save briefing flow." Final: yes.

- Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

Potentially related docs:
- src/index.ts: ob add handler and save briefing logic
Potentially related work items:
- SB-0MNM9ZYAZ000R2IZ: Add support for ob add <string> (related intent)

Risks & assumptions:
- Risk: Scope creep — implementing broad attachment type support may expand scope. Mitigation: limit to .md/.txt and text/*, record extra needs as new work items.
- Assumption: Network fetch of attachment URLs is permitted by bot runtime.

Headline summary: Allow ob add to ingest text attachments from referenced messages


Related work (automated report)
--------------------------------
- SB-0MNM9ZYAZ000R2IZ: Add support for ob add <string> — a completed item implementing inline text ingestion into OpenBrain; overlapping goals but focuses on inline message text rather than attachments. See work item for tests and CLI invocation patterns.
- SB-0MNEHNGJX006FM71: Integrate 'ob add' CLI into Discord bot add command — contains history of integrating the CLI runner and NDJSON handling; useful for CLI invocation patterns and error handling.
- tests/index.ob-add.test.ts & tests/save-button.test.ts — existing tests that include attachment-fetch mocking and are good templates for new tests.
data.title: ob add: fails to ingest when referenced message is an uploaded file attachment

data.description:
# Intake draft: Hardening: normalize cli-runner mocks across tests (SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1)

## Problem statement
Convert all test mocks of ../src/bot/cli-runner.js to use the importOriginal+spread pattern and adopt the tests/helpers/createAddGenerator helper consistently. Add or update the TypeScript declaration for tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.js and run/fix tests until the test suite is green. This reduces fragile mock implementations, improves type safety for tests, and standardizes mock patterns across the test suite.

## Users
- Developers running and maintaining tests. User story: "As a developer, I want tests to use a consistent mocking pattern for cli-runner so I can understand and update tests more easily and avoid brittle mocks."

## Success criteria
- All tests referencing ../src/bot/cli-runner.js use vi.doMock with importOriginal and spread the original exports when mocking.
- A single helper (tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.js) is used across tests to create async generator mocks for add-runner events, and the helper has TypeScript declarations at tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.d.ts.
- No new TypeScript type errors introduced by the change; tests/build pass locally.
- Full project test suite passes after changes.
- The tests include at least one example demonstrating the importOriginal+spread pattern (for future contributors).

## Constraints
- Keep external behavior of mocked functions identical (no API changes).
- Do not change production code in src/bot/cli-runner.js other than necessary type exports if missing; prefer test-side adaptations.
- Maintain compatibility with existing test environment (Vitest) and TypeScript settings.

## Existing state
- Multiple tests already use vi.doMock with importOriginal+spread in parts of the suite, but usage is inconsistent. The helper createAddGenerator.js and its declaration exist: tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.js and .d.ts.
- Several tests still implement inline mocks of cli-runner and some may use older patterns.

## Desired change
- Replace inline or bespoke mocks for cli-runner with a consistent vi.doMock(async importOriginal => ({ ...await importOriginal(), <overrides> })) pattern.
- Use tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.js helper where async generator behavior is needed; update its declaration if necessary.
- Run the full test suite and fix any failing tests caused by the change.

## Related work
- tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.js — helper already present and used in many tests.
- Existing tests that mock cli-runner (search results): tests/index.test.ts, tests/index.ob-add.test.ts, tests/save-button.test.ts, tests/discord/interaction.test.ts, tests/bot/cli-runner.* tests.
- Work item SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 — this item (seed).

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Should we update or add any exports in src/bot/cli-runner.js to make mocking or typing easier?" — Answer (agent inference): Prefer test-side changes; only add exports if absolutely necessary and approved. Source: repository inspection (imports show types used in tests). Final: yes (prefer no production changes; create follow-up work item if production changes needed).
- Q: "Is the tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.d.ts present?" — Answer (agent): Yes. Source: tests/helpers/createAddGenerator.d.ts. Final: yes.
- Q: "Who should the work be assigned to?" — Answer (user not provided) — Answer (agent inference): Map (assigned to Map per intake workflow). Final: Map.
data.title: Hardening: normalize cli-runner mocks across tests

data.description:
Summary
-------
Running `wl` commands (for example `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json` or `wl update <id> --audit-text "..." --json`) fails with a SyntaxError originating from the distributed ContextHub client at `/home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js`. The CLI aborts during module load and returns a Node SyntaxError instead of the expected JSON output.

Steps to reproduce
------------------
1. From repository root `/home/rgardler/projects/SourceBase` run:
   `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json`
2. Observe the CLI exit with a SyntaxError similar to the stack trace below.

Observed error (captured)
-------------------------
file:///home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js:357
            }, let, finalPrompt = prompt);
               ^^^

SyntaxError: Unexpected strict mode reserved word
    at compileSourceTextModule (node:internal/modules/esm/utils:346:16)
    at ModuleLoader.moduleStrategy (node:internal/modules/esm/translators:107:18)
    at #translate (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:546:20)
    at afterLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:596:29)
    at ModuleLoader.loadAndTranslate (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:601:12)
    at #createModuleJob (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:624:36)
    at #getJobFromResolveResult (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:343:34)
    at ModuleLoader.getModuleJobForImport (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:311:41)

Node.js v22.22.0

Commands that reproduce the failure:
- wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json
- wl update SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --audit-text "..." --json

Expected behavior
-----------------
`wl` commands should execute and return valid JSON (or perform the requested update) without throwing a SyntaxError. The worklog CLI should be usable for creating/updating work items and storing audit metadata.

Actual behavior / Impact
------------------------
The CLI crashes during module load with a SyntaxError. This blocks all programmatic usage of `wl` in this environment and prevents the agent from recording audits and creating or updating work items. It is blocking for any automation that relies on `wl`.

Environment
-----------
- Repository root: /home/rgardler/projects/SourceBase
- Failing file: /home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js (line ~357)
- Node.js: v22.22.0
- OS: Linux (local dev environment)

Suggested investigation / fix
----------------------------
- Inspect the generated file `/home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js` around line 357. The token `let` appears in an invalid syntactic position; likely a broken bundling or transpilation step produced malformed JS.
- Review the build/transpile pipeline for the ContextHub client (bundler config, target environment). Ensure the build output is valid ESM for the supported Node version.
- Rebuild the ContextHub package (or reinstall a known-good release) and verify that the generated file no longer contains the invalid token placement.
- Add a CI smoke test that runs a minimal `wl` command to catch similar regressions in future.

Acceptance criteria
-------------------
- `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json` returns valid JSON in this environment.
- `wl update <id> --audit-text "..." --json` completes successfully and stores the audit metadata.
- A root cause and fix are documented in the bug's comments.

Logs / evidence
----------------
Include the stack trace above and note that the failure is deterministic in this environment.

Priority: critical
Tags: opencode, cli, blocking, worklog

(If `wl create` fails due to the same SyntaxError, record this bug report file at `.opencode/tmp/wl-cli-syntaxerror-bug-report.md` in the repo and notify maintainers.)
data.title: wl CLI: SyntaxError in ContextHub opencode-client.js when running wl commands

data.description:
Summary
-------
Running `wl` commands (for example `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json` or `wl update <id> --audit-text "..." --json`) fails with a SyntaxError originating from the distributed ContextHub client at /home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js. The CLI aborts during module load and returns a Node SyntaxError instead of the expected JSON output.

Steps to reproduce
------------------
1. From repository root /home/rgardler/projects/SourceBase run:
   `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json`
2. Observe the CLI exit with a SyntaxError similar to the stack trace below.

Observed error (captured)
-------------------------
file:///home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js:357
            }, let, finalPrompt = prompt);
               ^^^

SyntaxError: Unexpected strict mode reserved word
    at compileSourceTextModule (node:internal/modules/esm/utils:346:16)
    at ModuleLoader.moduleStrategy (node:internal/modules/esm/translators:107:18)
    at #translate (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:546:20)
    at afterLoad (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:596:29)
    at ModuleLoader.loadAndTranslate (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:601:12)
    at #createModuleJob (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:624:36)
    at #getJobFromResolveResult (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:343:34)
    at ModuleLoader.getModuleJobForImport (node:internal/modules/esm/loader:311:41)

Node.js v22.22.0

Commands that reproduce the failure:
- wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json
- wl update SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --audit-text "..." --json

Expected behavior
-----------------
`wl` commands should execute and return valid JSON (or perform the requested update) without throwing a SyntaxError. The worklog CLI should be usable for creating/updating work items and storing audit metadata.

Actual behavior / Impact
------------------------
The CLI crashes during module load with a SyntaxError. This blocks all programmatic usage of `wl` in this environment and prevents the agent from recording audits and creating or updating work items. It is blocking for any automation that relies on `wl`.

Environment
-----------
- Repository root: /home/rgardler/projects/SourceBase
- Failing file: /home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js (line ~357)
- Node.js: v22.22.0
- OS: Linux (local dev environment)

Suggested investigation / fix
----------------------------
- Inspect the generated file /home/rgardler/projects/ContextHub/dist/tui/opencode-client.js around line 357. The token 'let' appears in an invalid syntactic position; likely a broken bundling or transpilation step produced malformed JS.
- Review the build/transpile pipeline for the ContextHub client (bundler config, target environment). Ensure the build output is valid ESM for the supported Node version.
- Rebuild the ContextHub package (or reinstall a known-good release) and verify that the generated file no longer contains the invalid token placement.
- Add a CI smoke test that runs a minimal `wl` command to catch similar regressions in future.

Acceptance criteria
-------------------
- `wl show SB-0MNQIEL11007RFT1 --children --json` returns valid JSON in this environment.
- `wl update <id> --audit-text "..." --json` completes successfully and stores the audit metadata.
- A root cause and fix are documented in the bug's comments.

Logs / evidence
----------------
Include the stack trace above and note that the failure is deterministic in this environment.

Priority: critical
Tags: opencode, cli, blocking, worklog

(If `wl create` fails due to the same SyntaxError, record this bug report file at .opencode/tmp/wl-cli-syntaxerror-bug-report.md in the repo and notify maintainers.)

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Implemented crawl instruction support for messages like 'Crawl <url>'. The ingestion flow now builds a plan from the crawl seed, extracts linked pages from the seed article body links, normalizes and deduplicates discovered URLs, and ingests seed + linked pages as separate DB entries while continuing on per-page failures. Updated files: src/ingestion/service.ts, src/ingestion/url.ts, src/ingestion/extractor.ts, tests/ingestion.service.test.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts. Validation: npm test (26 tests passed).

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Implemented crawl politeness safeguards: added robots.txt-aware crawl policy with user-agent-specific allow/disallow parsing and crawl-delay support, integrated it into ingestion so seed/discovered URLs are checked before fetch, and applied per-host pacing fallback delay when robots rules are absent. Updated configuration/docs with CRAWL_USER_AGENT and CRAWL_DELAY_MS and added tests for crawl policy behavior and ingestion skip-on-blocked-link flow. Validation: npm run lint and npm run test both pass (32 tests).

data.author: opencode
data.comment: Fixed crawl discovery fallback: when extractor-provided links[] is empty, ingestion now extracts anchor hrefs from extracted HTML content and merges them with links[] before normalization/deduplication. Updated files: src/ingestion/url.ts, src/ingestion/service.ts, tests/ingestion.url.test.ts, tests/ingestion.service.test.ts. Validation: npm test (28 tests passed).

data.author: ImplementAgent
data.comment:
Critical bug discovered during implementation review: In-progress items are lost on bot restart

**The issue:** When an item starts processing:
1. It's removed from the in-memory queue immediately (queue.shift())
2. Database status changes to 'processing'
3. On restart, only 'pending' items are loaded - 'processing' items are ignored
4. If the bot crashes during processing, the item is never re-queued and is effectively lost

**This is a data loss bug** that should be addressed alongside the restart recovery work.

This issue has been added to child work item SB-0MN4DH7BY1IXZ9S9 (Graceful shutdown with status message cleanup) which will:
- Load 'processing' items as 'pending' on startup
- Re-queue the current item on graceful shutdown
- Ensure no items are lost during restart

The current PR #11 addresses the immediate issue of restoring status messages, but the data loss issue should be prioritized next.

data.author: ImplementAgent
data.comment:
Updated to include critical bug fix: In-progress items are lost on bot restart

Discovery: When items start processing, they are:
1. Removed from in-memory queue (queue.shift())
2. Marked as 'processing' in database
3. NOT re-loaded on restart (initialize() only loads 'pending' items)

This means if the bot crashes during processing, the item is effectively lost.

Updated requirements:
- Load 'processing' items as 'pending' on startup
- Re-queue current in-memory item on graceful shutdown
- Mark interrupted items as 'pending' in database

This is a critical data loss bug that must be addressed alongside the graceful shutdown feature.

data.author: @implement-agent
data.comment:
Completed implementation of graceful shutdown with status message cleanup and lost item recovery.

**Changes Made:**

1. **src/db/queue-repository.ts**:
   - Modified \`getPending()\` to return items with status IN ('pending', 'processing') instead of just 'pending'
   - Added \`resetProcessingToPending()\` method to reset interrupted 'processing' items to 'pending' on startup

2. **src/ingestion/queue.ts**:
   - Modified \`initialize()\` to call \`resetProcessingToPending()\` before loading items, ensuring interrupted items are recovered
   - Added \`requeueCurrentItem()\` method to re-queue the current processing item to the front of the queue during shutdown
   - Added \`getQueueItems()\` method for shutdown cleanup

3. **src/index.ts**:
   - Imported \`closeDbPool\` from db/client
   - Added graceful shutdown handler that:
     - Posts 'Bot Closing Down for Maintenance' message to all channels with active status messages
     - Updates/deletes all progress status messages with 'Processing paused - bot restarting'
     - Updates/deletes all queue status messages with 'Queue processing paused - bot restarting'
     - Re-queues the current processing item if any
     - Closes database connection properly
   - Added signal handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT

4. **tests/queue.restart.test.ts**:
   - Updated fake pool to handle the new SQL query patterns

**Key Features:**
- Lost item recovery: Items with 'processing' status are now re-queued on startup
- Graceful shutdown: 30-second timeout for cleanup operations
- Status message cleanup: All tracked messages are updated or deleted
- Maintenance notification: Users are informed when the bot shuts down
- Database connection: Properly closed before exit

All tests pass (101 passed, 1 skipped) and the build succeeds.

# Planning Complete - CLI Tool Feature Breakdown

## Summary
Decomposed SB-0MN4OXL5P1XUMOAF into 4 feature work items ready for implementation.

## Features Created
1. **CLI Foundation & Entry Point** (SB-0MN4QH5OY1OGF7NW)
   - Stage: plan_complete | Effort: Small (5.17h) | Risk: Low (4/20)
   - Entry point, help text, config integration, error handling

2. **sb add Command - URL Ingestion** (SB-0MN4QH7HG13XCYZU)
   - Stage: plan_complete | Effort: Small (7.50h) | Risk: Medium (9/20)
   - Single/multiple URL ingestion, progress display, YouTube support
   - Depends on: CLI Foundation

3. **sb search Command - Semantic Search** (SB-0MN4QH9MZ03672Q5)
   - Stage: plan_complete | Effort: Small (5.17h) | Risk: Low (4/20)
   - Text query embedding, table/JSON/urls-only output formats
   - Depends on: CLI Foundation

4. **sb stats Command - Database Statistics** (SB-0MN4QHBA9140EO5I)
   - Stage: plan_complete | Effort: Small (3.17h) | Risk: Low (1/20)
   - Database metrics, time-based stats, table/JSON output
   - Depends on: CLI Foundation

## Total Effort Estimate
- Combined expected: 21.01 hours
- Combined recommended: 34.01 hours
- Range: 28.0 - 44.5 hours

## Dependencies
- Features 2, 3, 4 all depend on Feature 1 (CLI Foundation)
- Features 2, 3, 4 have no dependencies between each other and can be developed in parallel

## Open Questions Remaining
- Q4: Should we include `sb --version` command to display package version?
- Q5: What is the preferred output format for similarity scores in table view? (percentage, decimal 0-1, or raw distance?)

## Appendix: Planning Interview

**Q1:** What CLI commands should the tool support?  
**A1:** A (add URL), B (semantic search), C (database stats) - User selected

**Q2:** How should the CLI authenticate with the LLM service?  
**A2:** A - Reuse existing `config.ts` (User selected)

**Q3:** Should the CLI support interactive shell mode?  
**A3:** A - One-shot commands only (User selected)

## Plan: Changelog

data.author: @agent-name
data.comment:
Completed work, see commit 15ddf4f for details.

Changes made:
- Removed embedding generation and Qdrant indexing from all 3 IngestionService paths (article, PDF, file). Ingestion now stores NULL embeddings and relies on BackfillService to generate and index them.
- Added Qdrant indexing to BackfillService.processEmbeddingBackfill() after PostgreSQL upsert
- Added adaptive polling: if document_queue is empty, poll every 1 min and process 5 items; otherwise use longer interval (BACKFILL_INTERVAL_MS)
- Added seedExistingEmbeddings() to handle links with existing embeddings missing from Qdrant (handles the case where Qdrant was cleared during failed reimport)
- Updated BackfillService.startPeriodicProcessing() to use adaptive polling
- Added qdrantStore and getDocumentQueueDepth options to BackfillService
- Updated index.ts to wire up Qdrant store and queue depth function to BackfillService
- Updated test to match new log message

Note: PostgreSQL migration (006_change_embedding_dim.sql) to change embedding column from vector(2880) to vector(1024) must be run before reimport resumes.

data.author: rgardler
data.comment:
Implemented /search slash command with tiered relevance retrieval.

## Changes Made

### New Files
- src/commands/search-handler.ts - SearchCommandHandler implementing CommandHandler interface

### Modified Files
- src/config.ts - Added SEARCH_TOP_K, SEARCH_TIER_HIGH_THRESHOLD, SEARCH_TIER_MEDIUM_THRESHOLD config options
- src/vector/qdrant-store.ts - Added searchWithScores() method and SearchResultWithScore interface
- src/query/service.ts - Added tiered relevance search support with searchWithTieredRelevance()
- src/discord/client.ts - Registered /search slash command with query parameter
- src/index.ts - Wired up SearchCommandHandler using CommandHandler pattern

## Implementation Details

The /search command:
1. Accepts a required 'query' text parameter
2. Embeds the query and searches Qdrant for top-k results (default 10)
3. Categorizes results by tiered relevance based on similarity scores:
   - High: score >= 0.75
   - Medium: score >= 0.50
   - Low: score < 0.50
4. Returns a summary in the message body (first 5 results, within 2000 char limit)
5. Returns full results in a .md file attachment with filename pattern: <sanitized-query>-<YYYYMMDD>.md
6. Uses the CommandHandler interface pattern as specified in the acceptance criteria

## Testing
- All 152 existing tests pass
- TypeScript build succeeds without errors

Commit: c428be1

data.author: opencode
data.comment:
## Planning Complete

### Approved Feature Plan (7 features)

1. **Split Config into CLI and Bot Modules** (SB-0MN9CIGI6005KN9H) — Foundation: split src/config.ts into CLI-only and bot configs
2. **Extract Discord-Free Shared Types** (SB-0MN9CIUK5008VPR6) — Split types.ts and presenter interfaces to remove discord.js from CLI
3. **Remove Discord Coupling from CLI Commands** (SB-0MN9CJIXA0031C2I) — Refactor all 4 CLI commands, IngestionService, and queue schema to be Discord-free
4. **Implement CLI Progress Presenters** (SB-0MN9CK3AL001I8S8) — NDJSON, Console, and Webhook presenters
5. **Add CLI Flags for Context Pass-Through** (SB-0MN9CKJVH007RR6G) — --channel-id, --message-id, --author-id, --format, --webhook-url flags
6. **Refactor Bot to Invoke CLI as Subprocess** (SB-0MN9CL02100258DH) — Replace all direct CLI imports in bot with child_process.spawn calls
7. **Boundary Enforcement and Documentation** (SB-0MN9CLCFY001NSWN) — Automated boundary tests + README update

### Dependency Chain

F1 -> F2 -> F3 -> F4 -> F5 -> F6 -> F7 (linear with some cross-dependencies)

### Key Decisions

- DB will be wiped; no migration needed. Schema changes are unrestricted.
- Temporary bot breakage between Feature 3 and Feature 6 is accepted (no adapter pattern).
- All 3 presenters (NDJSON, Console, Webhook) delivered in this epic.
- Migration checklist (Step 7) removed per user direction.
- Existing child SB-0MN8OKPV8007EN73 closed and replaced by this plan.

### Automated Review Log

1. **Completeness** — All 7 features have complete field coverage; removed adapter pattern reference from Feature 3.
2. **Sequencing & Dependencies** — Linear chain is coherent; no cycles; bot breakage window documented.
3. **Scope Sizing** — All features appropriately sized; Features 3 and 6 are largest but coherent.
4. **Acceptance & Testability** — Tightened criteria; added negative test cases for Features 2-6.
5. **Polish & Handoff** — Standardized tense, bullet style, and title format across all features.

### Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers (Planning Session)

- Q: "Should the existing child SB-0MN8OKPV8007EN73 be broadened, kept narrow, or replaced with the new plan?" — Answer (user): "Replace with new plan." Source: interactive reply. Final: Replace. SB-0MN8OKPV8007EN73 closed.
- Q: "Should all 3 CLI presenters (NDJSON, Console, Webhook) be delivered, or defer webhook?" — Answer (user): "All three presenters." Source: interactive reply. Final: All 3.
- Q: "Should the migration checklist (Step 7) be lightweight or formal?" — Answer (user): "No need for migration, we will wipe the database. Remove this step." Source: interactive reply. Final: Removed.
- Q: "Should the bot-side subprocess refactoring be a single feature or split by operation?" — Answer (user): "Single feature." Source: interactive reply. Final: Single feature (Feature 6).
- Q: "Should the queue DB schema keep or drop the discordChannelId column?" — Answer (user): "Drop the column, there will be no migration, we will wipe the database." Source: interactive reply. Final: Drop column.
- Q: "Should CLI refactoring include any database migration work?" — Answer (user): "Change the schema as much as you want, we do not need to migrate the data. We will start from a blank database." Source: interactive reply. Final: No migration constraints.
- Q: "Will the code remain operable through each of those steps?" — Answer (user): "Accepted temporary bot breakage between Feature 3 and Feature 6. No adapter needed." Source: interactive reply. Final: Temporary breakage accepted.
- Q: "How to handle Feature 3 breaking the bot before Feature 6?" — Answer (user): "Accept temporary breakage — bot may be non-functional between Feature 3 and Feature 6." Source: interactive reply. Final: Accepted.

data.author: @your-agent-name
data.comment:
Completed CLI Subprocess Runner Utility implementation.

## Changes Made

Created src/bot/cli-runner.ts with the following features:

### Core Functions
- runAddCommand(url, options) - Spawns sb add with NDJSON format and yields progress events
- runQueueCommand(url, options) - Spawns sb queue and returns queue result with ID
- runStatsCommand(options) - Spawns sb stats --format json and returns parsed statistics
- runCliCommand(command, args, options) - Generic CLI command runner for extensibility

### Error Handling
- CliRunnerError class captures exit codes and stderr output
- All functions handle non-zero exit codes gracefully
- Subprocess stderr is captured without crashing the bot
- Timeout support with automatic SIGTERM

### Context Support
All functions accept Discord context flags: --channel-id, --message-id, --author-id

### Type Safety
Full TypeScript types exported including CliProgressEvent, CliContext, CliRunnerOptions, QueueResult, AddResult, StatsResult

### Testing
Created tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts with 16 tests covering module exports, CliRunnerError class, type definitions, and environment configuration

## Acceptance Criteria Status
All acceptance criteria met:
- Exports required functions
- Spawns sb binary with proper environment and working directory
- NDJSON stdout parsed into progress event objects with type-safe interfaces
- stderr captured and logged without crashing the bot
- Exit codes handled: 0=success, non-zero=logged error, bot continues
- Context flags passed correctly
- Returns promises that resolve with final result or structured error

data.author: opencode
data.comment:
## Epic Created: Library Refactoring Plan

This epic tracks the migration from custom modules to open source libraries. Created 7 child work items prioritized by value:

### High Priority (Do First)
1. **SB-0MNBSDEV8000N7GA** - Replace LLM Client with openai package
   - Highest value: 10.8k stars, production-ready, handles retries/streaming
   - Effort: Medium

2. **SB-0MNBSDN010091ST9** - Replace Qdrant wrapper with @qdrant/js-client-rest
   - High value: Official SDK, maintained by Qdrant team
   - Effort: Medium

### Medium Priority (Do After High)
3. **SB-0MNBSDUQE008P7CU** - Replace custom logger with pino
   - Good value: 17.6k stars, extremely fast, JSON-first
   - Effort: Low

4. **SB-0MNBSEABT007T6E2** - Improve pgvector integration with pgvector package
   - Moderate value: Better type handling, distance functions
   - Effort: Low-Medium

5. **SB-0MNBSEQ4T002G2QT** - Improve config validation with idiomatic zod
   - Moderate value: Better DX, maintainability
   - Effort: Low

### Low Priority (Cleanup/Documentation)
6. **SB-0MNBSEHU1001WJTR** - Simplify database client by removing wrapper
   - Minor value: Code cleanup, less abstraction
   - Effort: Low

7. **SB-0MNBSEY9G0055O5N** - Document content extraction strategy
   - Knowledge preservation, future-proofing
   - Effort: Low (research/documentation only)

### Expected Impact
- Reduce custom code by ~40%
- Gain production-grade error handling
- Regular security updates from community
- Better TypeScript support
- Easier CLI extraction (fewer shared modules to manage)

### Recommended Order
1. LLM Client (most value, foundational)
2. Qdrant Client (high value, standalone)
3. Logger (easy win, affects whole codebase)
4. pgvector + Config (parallel, independent)
5. Database simplification + Documentation (cleanup)

data.author: @Map
data.comment:
Completed implementation of NDJSON progress event handling with threaded Discord updates.

Implemented in src/index.ts:
- formatProgressMessage(): Maps CLI phases to user-friendly Discord messages:
  - downloading → ⏳ Downloading content...
  - extracting → 📝 Extracting text content...
  - embedding → 🧠 Generating embeddings...
  - completed → ✅ Added to OpenBrain: title
  - failed → ❌ Failed: error message
- formatThreadName(): Creates descriptive thread names from URL domains
- processUrlWithProgress(): Comprehensive progress handling:
  - Creates thread from original message
  - Sends progress updates only when phase changes (throttling)
  - Archives thread after completion
  - Graceful fallback to message.reply() if thread fails
  - Proper error handling and logging

Features:
- Thread auto-archives after 1 hour (60 minutes)
- Progress updates throttled to avoid spam
- URLs wrapped in backticks in final messages
- Thread creation failures gracefully fall back to replies

Build: ✅ No TypeScript errors
Tests: ✅ All 12 tests passing

data.comment: Implemented summary posting after successful add. Added runSummaryCommand and summary retry logic with 3 attempts and exponential backoff, idempotency markers by item id or URL, manual review notification on repeated failures, and fallback posting target logic from thread to configured channel to message channel. Added config variables SEND_SUMMARY_ON_INSERT, DEFAULT_DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID, OPENBRAIN_ITEM_URL_TEMPLATE and updated docs. Added tests in tests/index.test.ts, tests/bot/cli-runner.test.ts, tests/bot/cli-runner.ndjson.test.ts, tests/helpers/mockCliSpawn.ts. Validation passed with npm run lint and npm test (46 tests).

data.author: @opencode
data.comment: Investigated regression reported during live YouTube ingest. Root cause: CLI runner passed unsupported flags (--channel-id/--message-id/--author-id) to OpenBrain CLI, which emitted 'Invalid URL' errors; this broke summary generation retries in production path. Applied fix to use OpenBrain-supported metadata tags (--tag discord_channel_id:..., --tag discord_message_id:..., --tag discord_author_id:...) for add/queue commands only. Updated tests and README context section accordingly. Re-ran validation with npm run lint and npm test (46 passing).
